radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Ms. ___ is an ___ female with history of A. fib on
Coumadin, chronic kidney disease, COPD on 2L O2, severe pulmonary hypertension
who is presenting as a transfer from ___ for bradycardia likely due
to metabolic disturbances in the setting ___ from right sided heart
failure/HFpEF. RHC showing normal PCWP and severe pulm HTN of unclear
etiology.// Please eval lung parenchyma for potential etiology of pulm HTN

TECHNIQUE:  CT of the chest was performed without IV contrast.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 9.0 s, 34.7 cm; CTDIvol = 10.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 336.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 349 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CT for direct comparison.  Correlation with chest
radiographs dated ___ and ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid gland is unremarkable. 
There is no axillary lymphadenopathy.  The chest wall is unremarkable.  Left
breast shows some skin thickening and increased attenuation of fat probably
due to edema.  This may be due to fluid shifts, noting bilateral ill-defined
subcutaneous fluid that is probably due to fluid overload, along each flank,
but left greater than right.  It is possible that the asymmetry in the breasts
may be positional.

UPPER ABDOMEN: There is a small hiatal hernia.  There is cholelithiasis
without evidence of acute cholecystitis.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  The upper esophagus is
thin walled but ectatic and air-filled.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart is normal in overall size, without
pericardial effusion, although left-sided chambers are smaller than right,
which is not normal.  Coronary artery calcifications are present.
PLEURA: There is a small right and trace left pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  There is moderate upper lobe predominant centrilobular
emphysema.  No focal consolidation is identified.  There are a few scattered
pulmonary nodules, the largest measuring 4 mm in the right upper lobe (5:81).
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are clear to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS: The main pulmonary artery measures up to 2.7 cm (5:123).  Central
pulmonary arteries are not particularly enlarged.  The thoracic aorta is
normal in course and caliber, with moderate atherosclerotic calcifications.
CHEST CAGE: There are old healed fractures of the left seventh and eighth
ribs.  Degenerative changes are seen throughout the spine.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No evidence of interstitial lung disease.
2.  Moderate upper lobe predominant centrilobular emphysema.
3.  Small bilateral pleural effusions with minor associated atelectasis. ''
4.  Coronary calcification.
5.  Cholelithiasis without evidence of acute cholecystitis.
6.  Few small lung nodules measuring up to at most 4 mm.  These are very
likely benign, but noting emphysema may be appropriate to consider follow-up
chest CT for surveillance in ___ year.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Follow-up chest CT is recommended for surveillance of very
small, probably benign, lung nodules in ___ year.3.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with rectal cancer, tobacco use and bowel
obstruction with cough.  // Please evaluate etiology of cough and perform
staging.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images

DOSE:  DLP: 272 mGy cm

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis ___

FINDINGS: 

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST: There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal
or hilar lymphadenopathy.  Esophagus is unremarkable.

Heart size is normal without pericardial effusion.  The thoracic aorta and
proximal great vessels are normal in caliber with mild scattered
atherosclerosis.  The main pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.  There is
moderate atherosclerosis of the coronary arteries most notably the left main
and LAD.

The tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level.  Evaluation of
the lung parenchyma is limited by respiratory motion.  There is
mild-to-moderate bronchial wall thickening with retained secretions.  Multiple
centrilobular nodules in the left upper lobe may be smoking related. 
Atelectasis in the right middle lobe, right lower lobe, lingula and left lower
lobe is mild to moderate.  There appears to be a background of mild
centrilobular emphysema.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: There is no concerning bony lesion.

UPPER ABDOMEN: 8 mm right adrenal nodule also seen on yesterday's CT of the
abdomen pelvis is re- demonstrated.  There is a 3.3 x 2.8 cm portacaval lymph
node (5:288). Partially included loops of large bowel remain significantly
dilated similar to yesterday. There is a small hiatal hernia.

IMPRESSION: 

CT CHEST:

1. No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.
2. Bronchial wall thickening, retained secretions, and centrilobular nodules
may reflect respiratory bronchiolitis, a smoking related condition.
3. There is a background of mild emphysema and moderate atelectasis.
UPPER ABDOMEN:

1. 3.3 x 2.8 cm portacaval lymph node could reflect metastatic disease.
2. 8 mm right adrenal nodule.  Please refer to folowup recommendations per CT
of the abdomen and pelvis dated ___.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with hx of of LEFT ORCHIECTOMY RADICAL for Cancer

metastatic disease. Further evaluation with full chest CT is recommended.
Shown on CT Abdomen and Pelvis done on ___ here.// ?Mets

axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

COMPARISON:  Abdomen CT ___ and chest CT ___

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal and hilar lymph
nodes are not enlarged., Mildly ectatic.  The main pulmonary artery is
minimally enlarged measures 3.2 cm.  Cardiac configuration is normal and there
is mild calcification in the LAD.  Pacer leads are in standard position in
right atrium and right ventricle.  There are multiple calcified lung nodules,
stable.  8 mm nodule in the left lower lobe is new from ___.  There
is no pleural or pericardial effusion.
This examination is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation there is mild

There are no bone findings of malignancy

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with CLL and testicular cancer. Admitted with
fevers. Persistent cough and now with chest palpitations.// Eval source of
fevers. Eval PE.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.8
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 13.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
2.7 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 35.1 cm; CTDIvol = 11.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 399.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 404 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest with contrast from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus.
Mildly prominent main pulmonary artery, suggest pulmonary artery hypertension.
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  The heart is normal in size and configuration.  No
pericardial effusion is seen.  A pacemaker is again seen in unchanged
position.  There is Port-A-Cath with tip in the right atrium.  Coronary artery
calcifications

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There are new peribronchovascular nodular opacities in the
right lower lobe compared to the prior study in ___, which are
concerning for infection versus aspiration.  There is new bronchial wall
thickening there is most prominent in the right lower lobe, likely
inflammatory/infectious.  There is no mucous plugging.  Calcified benign
granulomas are seen scattered throughout the lungs, compatible with sequela of
prior granulomatous infection.  The previously seen 8 mm nodule in the left
lower lobe from ___ is not seen on today's exam, although there is
some motion at the left base, it is most likely resolved..  There is no focal
parenchyma opacification in the left lung.  The airways are patent to the
level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is remarkable for
cholecystectomy.  Postoperative changes gastric bypass.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.


IMPRESSION:


1. New opacities in the right lower lobe are concerning for infection versus
aspiration.
2. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
3. There has been interval resolution of the previously seen 8 mm nodule in
the left lower lobe from ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Hemothoraxon, Right// Hemothoraxon, Rightp

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.1 s, 32.5 cm; CTDIvol = 22.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 726.6
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 727 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Compared to chest CT ___.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST PERIMETER: Hypodense lesions in the right thyroid lobe a too small to
warrant further imaging evaluation.  Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes
are not enlarged.  There is no soft tissue abnormality in the chest wall
concerning for infection, malignancy, or bleeding.


CARDIO-MEDIASTINUM:Esophagus is mildly patulous, but distended only with air. 
Atherosclerotic calcification is not apparent in head neck vessels. Patient
has had median sternotomy and coronary bypass grafting, not evaluated by this
examination. There is no mediastinal collection or pericardial abnormality or
abnormality in the chest wall to suggest wound complication..

THORACIC LYMPH NODES: Multiple measurable mediastinal lymph nodes are neither
pathologically enlarged nor growing.  10 mm right hilar nodes are stable.  No
vital structures are compromised.

LUNGS, AIRWAYS, PLEURAE: There has been virtually no change in the moderate
nearly circumferential residual right pleural abnormality, more pronounced in
the mid and lower chest, since ___, following removal of the right
pigtail pleural drainage catheter except that previous small air collections
have been replaced by fluid.  There is no indication of active bleeding.

Moderate loss of volume in the right lung generally, most pronounced in the
lower lobe due to pleural restriction is also unchanged.  A solitary 3 mm left
upper lobe nodule, 4: 84, unchanged since prior study is the only focal
pulmonary abnormality.  Tracheobronchial tree is normal to subsegmental
levels.  Left pleural surfaces are normal.




CHEST CAGE: Multilevel disc degeneration in the thoracic spine is unchanged. 
No compression or pathologic fracture or destructive bone lesion.

IMPRESSION: 

No decrease since ___ and the removal of the right pigtail pleural
drainage catheter in the volume of moderate circumferential residual right
pleural effusion and thickening, producing stable restriction in right lung
volume, primarily in the lower lobe.

Solitary 3 mm left upper lobe lung nodule.  See recommendations below.

RECOMMENDATION(S):   For incidentally detected single solid pulmonary nodule
smaller than 6 mm, no CT follow-up is recommended in a low-risk patient, and
an optional CT in 12 months is recommended in a high-risk patient.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with pleural effusion// pleural effusion interval
f/u

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.4 s, 38.7 cm; CTDIvol = 20.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 772.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 772 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest CTs, most recently ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is unremarkable.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall.  Mild atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  Status post CABG surgery.  Moderate
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, none in the cardiac
valves or aorta.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber
throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  Unchanged appearance of small
hilar lymph node measuring 6 mm in short axis diameter (302:133).  No hilar
lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
Small loculated right pleural effusion, slightly smaller than in prior study. 
No pleural effusion to the left.  Mild bilateral apical scarring.

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall thickening,
bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Small solid nodules are unchanged compared
to prior study, 2 mm nodules in the left upper lobe (302:73 and 100), 2 mm
perifissural nodule in the right upper lobe (302:111).  No new nodules.

CHEST CAGE:
Moderate dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  Stable postoperative
appearance of midline sternotomy.  Diffuse degenerative disease, notably in
both shoulder joints.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show unchanged appearance of several
renal cysts bilaterally.

IMPRESSION: 

The loculated right pleural effusion in the lung base is smaller compared to
prior study.
Stable small solid pulmonary nodules.

Small loculated right pleural effusion, slightly smaller than in prior study. 
No pleural effusion to the left.  Mild bilateral apical scarring.

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall thickening,
bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Small solid nodules are unchanged compared
to prior study, 2 mm nodules in the left upper lobe (302:73 and 100), 2 mm
perifissural nodule in the right upper lobe (302:111).  No new nodules.

CHEST CAGE:
Moderate dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  Stable postoperative
appearance of midline sternotomy.  Diffuse degenerative disease, notably in
both shoulder joints.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show unchanged appearance of several
renal cysts bilaterally.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ male with total knee arthroplasty, presenting with
low-grade temperature and tachycardia, evaluate for pulmonary embolism.

COMPARISON:  Not available at the ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images of the chest were obtained prior to and
following uneventful intravenous administration of Optiray.  Multiplanar
reformatted images were obtained and reviewed.

CTA CHEST:  The quality of pulmonary arterial opacification allows to exclude
pulmonary embolism to the segmental levels.  The main pulmonary artery is
enlarged in diameter, measuring up to 35 mm.  There is trace pericardial
fluid.

Atherosclerotic calcifications are noted in the right and left coronary
circulation.

There is no mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymphadenopathy.

The airways are patent to the segmental levels bilaterally.

The lungs are clear, with the exception of a 2-mm pulmonary nodule in the left
lower lobe (2:41).  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

This study is not optimized for subdiaphragmatic evaluation, except to note a
low-attenuation lesion in the left liver lobe (2:47) and 4-mm right liver lobe
lesion.  The lesions are not well characterized on this non-dedicated study.

Bone windows demonstrate no lytic or sclerotic lesions concerning for
malignancy.  There are multilevel degenerative discogenic changes, with large
anterior osteophytes.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No pulmonary embolism.

2.  16-mm indeterminant left liver lobe lesion and 4-mm right liver lobe
lesion, statistically likely represent cysts.

3.  Slightly enlarged main pulmonary artery, which can be seen in the setting
of pulmonary hypertension.

4.  2-mm pulmonary nodule in the left lower lobe.  In absence of risk factors
for lung carcinoma, no further workup is needed.  If such risk factors are
present, followup in one year is recommended according to ___ Society
guidelines.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with 5 lbs. weight loss, cough, SOB, found to
have large R pleural effusion c/f malignancy// eval for mass or other etiology
for Large R pleural effusion s/p chest tube placement ad lymphadenopathy

TECHNIQUE:  CT of the chest was performed without administration of IV
contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and axial MIP reformat were performed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.3 s, 36.7 cm; CTDIvol = 15.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 582.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 582 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA chest ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: A left chest wall pacemaker remains
in place with its leads terminating in the right atrium and right ventricle.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Again noted is a calcified aneurysm of the splenic artery
measuring 1.7 x 1.5 cm (301:50).  Cholelithiasis is also present.  A partially
imaged left renal cyst is unchanged.  Two 5 mm hypodensity in the inferior
right hepatic lobe (301:61) and left hepatic lobe are nonspecific (301:49).

MEDIASTINUM: Multiple surgical clips are again noted in the superior
mediastinum.

HILA: There is no hilar adenopathy, although evaluation is limited by the
absence of intravenous contrast.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There are postsurgical changes from CABG.  Severe
coronary artery calcifications are noted.  There is no pericardial effusion.

PLEURA: A right chest tube is in place. There is a small right pleural
effusion, which is significantly decreased in size compared to prior CT.  A
small right pneumothorax is also noted.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: Diffuse ground-glass opacity throughout the right lung likely
represents edema in setting of recent re-expansion.  There are areas of more
dense consolidation in the right middle lobe and right lower lobe.  Mild
dependent atelectasis is appreciated in the left lung.  There are multiple
pulmonary nodules in right upper lobe measuring up to 4 mm (for example,
302:30, 67).  There is a punctate nodule in the left lung apex (___) and a
2 mm pulmonary nodule in the left upper lobe (302:47).
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  Evaluation of the vasculature is limited due to lack of IV
contrast.  There is mild atherosclerotic disease in the aorta.  There is no
aortic aneurysm.
CHEST CAGE: Median sternotomy wires are in place.  There is a defect in the
most inferior median sternotomy wire.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Interval reexpansion of the right lung status post chest tube placement,
with scattered areas of ground glass opacity in the right lung likely
representing reexpansion pulmonary edema.
2.  A small right pneumothorax, which was not visualized on prior chest
radiographs, and a small residual right pleural effusion.
3.  Areas of residual opacity primarily in the right middle lobe and right
lower lobe are favored to represent atelectasis, however underlying pneumonia
or a small mass cannot be entirely excluded.
4.  Unchanged calcified aneurysm of the splenic artery.
5.  Nonspecific 5 mm hypodensities in the liver, which can be further
evaluated with MRI Abdomen with contrast.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with fall on right side, complaining of right
rib pain. Evaluate for fracture.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was obtained without
intravenous consciousand reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 456.97 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

MEDIASTINUM: The thyroid is normal.  There is no supraclavicular, axillary or
mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in
size.  There is minimal calcification of the aorta.   The heart size is normal
and there is no pericardial effusion.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax.  There is no pleural effusion.

LUNGS: The airways are patent.  There is no airspace consolidation.  There is
no diffuse interstitial abnormality.  The patient is status post left lower
lobe wedge resection of a mass.  There are no concerning pulmonary nodules. 
There are areas of focal subpleural scarring or atelectasis. There are several
tiny calcified granulomas.

BONES: There are no destructive focal osseous lesions concerning for
malignancy within the imaged thoracic skeleton.  There is cortical
irregularity of the right lateral tenth and right posterior 11th ribs which
may be chronic, but acuity is indeterminate.  No other potential fracture is
seen.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored to evaluate the abdomen. Within
these limitations no gross abnormality is seen, besides a partially visualized
left renal cyst.

IMPRESSION: 

Cortical irregularity of the right tenth and eleventh ribs compatible with
fractures, possibly chronic, but acuity indeterminate. Otherwise, no evidence
of acute fracture.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT Torso without contrast

INDICATION:  ___ woman with left lower rib pain.  Evaluate for rib
fracture.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT images of the abdomen and pelvis were acquired
without intravenous contrast. Non-contrast scan has several limitations in
detecting vascular and parenchymal organ abnormalities, including tumor
detection.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 8.6 s, 67.6 cm; CTDIvol = 21.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,417.6 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,418 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA abdomen and pelvis dated ___.
CT chest dated ___.
Chest CT dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

Detailed evaluation of the solid organs, soft tissues, and vessels is limited
without the use of intravenous contrast. Within this limitation:

CHEST:
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber with normal 3 vessel aortic arch
anatomy.  Atherosclerotic calcifications within the aortic arch and descending
thoracic aorta are mild.  The heart is normal in size.  Scattered coronary
artery calcifications are mild on this nondedicated exam.  Aortic valve
calcifications are minimal.  Mitral annulus calcifications are mild.  No
evidence of a pericardial effusion.

No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.

A few sub-4 mm peripheral pulmonary nodules are unchanged.  Areas of
pleuroparenchymal scarring particularly in the left thorax are unchanged.  The
patient has had prior left lower lobe wedge resection.  No concerning
pulmonary masses or nodules.  No parenchymal opacities concerning for
infection.  Bibasilar atelectasis is moderate.

No pleural effusions or pneumothorax.  The airways are patent to at least the
subsegmental level.

Multilevel degenerative changes in the thoracic spine are most pronounced in
the lower portion and mild-to-moderate, similar to prior exam.  Thoracic
kyphosis is exaggerated, also similar prior exam.  No evidence of acute
fracture.  No prevertebral soft tissue swelling.
Degenerative changes in the right glenohumeral joint are mild-to-moderate.  No
soft tissue hematoma in the chest cage.  No osseous lesions concerning for
malignancy or infection in the chest cage.  Slight loss of T11 vertebral body
height related to a Schmorl's node from the superior endplate is unchanged
(series 602, image 76).

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogeneous attenuation throughout.  No
evidence of focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  No
evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder
contains gallstones without wall thickening or evidence of inflammation.  No
ascites.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  No pancreatic
ductal dilatation.  No peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size.  No evidence of
concerning focal renal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan. 
A left mid renal exophytic cyst is unchanged (series 601, image 74).  A tiny
calcification in the left midpole is likely a nonobstructing stone (series
601, image 79).  No hydronephrosis in either kidney.  No perinephric
abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: A hiatal hernia is small.  The stomach is not distended,
limiting evaluation.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber and wall
thickness throughout.  Colonic diverticulosis is minimal.  The rectum is
within normal limits.  The appendix is normal.  No bowel obstruction.  No free
air.  No intra-abdominal drainable fluid collections.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder is moderately distended and unremarkable.  The
distal ureters are unremarkable.  No free fluid in the pelvis.  Coarse
calcifications in the bilateral pelvis are phleboliths.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Coarse calcifications in the uterus are probably
fibroids.  No adnexal masses.

LYMPH NODES: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  A right pelvic
lymph node measures 5 mm in short axis, unchanged since at least ___ (series
3, image 205).  No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: A saccular infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm measures 3.1 x 3.2
cm, not appreciably changed when accounting for differences in measurement
technique (series 2, image 139).  No surrounding fat stranding or
retroperitoneal hematoma.  Mild-to-moderate calcified atherosclerotic disease
is noted.

BONES: No fracture.  No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy or
infection.  Sclerotic lesion in the L2 vertebral body is unchanged, likely a
bone island (series 602, image 75).  No evidence of an acute fracture. 
Multilevel degenerative changes in the lumbosacral spine are moderate with
multiple levels of prominent Schmorl's nodes.  Broad-based disc bulges at all
levels of the lumbar spine indent the anterior thecal sac.  Spinal canal
however is patent.  No critical neural foraminal stenosis.  Left curvature of
the lumbar spine is mild.  There is mild joint space narrowing of both
femoroacetabular joints.

SOFT TISSUES: A fat containing umbilical hernia is tiny.  No hematoma in the
soft tissue.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No CT evidence of acute traumatic injury in the thorax.  No fracture.
2.  3.2 x 3.1 cm infrarenal saccular abdominal aortic aneurysm, not
appreciably changed.
3.  Multilevel degenerative changes throughout the spine, worse in the lumbar
spine.  Mild broad-based disc bulges but no critical spinal canal stenosis.
4.  Cholelithiasis.
5.  Tiny left mid kidney nonobstructing stone.  No hydronephrosis.
6.  Small hiatal hernia.

levels of the lumbar spine indent the anterior thecal sac.  Spinal canal
however is patent.  No critical neural foraminal stenosis.  Left curvature of
the lumbar spine is mild.  There is mild joint space narrowing of both
femoroacetabular joints.

SOFT TISSUES: A fat containing umbilical hernia is tiny.  No hematoma in the
soft tissue.

1.  No CT evidence of acute traumatic injury in the thorax.  No fracture.
2.  3.2 x 3.1 cm infrarenal saccular abdominal aortic aneurysm, not
appreciably changed.
3.  Multilevel degenerative changes throughout the spine, worse in the lumbar
spine.  Mild broad-based disc bulges but no critical spinal canal stenosis.
4.  Cholelithiasis.
5.  Tiny left mid kidney nonobstructing stone.  No hydronephrosis.
6.  Small hiatal hernia.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man with persistent left lung collapse.

Comparison is made to the chest radiograph of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial MDCT images of the chest were obtained after administration
of 75 mL of Optiray intravenously.  Sagittal and coronal reformatted images
were then acquired.

FINDINGS:  8 cm mass within the left lower lobe grows into the left lower lobe
bronchus and is associated with complete collapse of the left lower lobe.  The
post-radiation changes of the left upper lobe and superior segment of the
right lower lobe are noted. Mild atelectasis of the right lung base is noted
and the remainder of the right lung is clear. The left pleural effusion is
small.  

Partial calcification of the mural thrombus in the left main pulmonary artery
indicates it is chronic.  Aside from an one 11 mm prevascular node, no central
nodes are pathologically enlarged.  The endotracheal tube and NG tube are in
the standard positions. Small amount of pericardial effusion with no evidence
of tamponade. 22 x 31 mm right thyroid lobe and a calcified 13 x 12 mm left
thyroid nodule.

There is a 22 x 20 mm left adrenal mass, which cannot be further characterized
on this study.  There is also a 7-mm enhancing lesion within the right lobe of
the liver.  Calcified granulomas of the liver are noted.  The gallbladder,
spleen, left adrenal gland and the superior pole of the kidneys appear
unremarkable.

BONE WINDOWS:  No concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions are identified.
Left humeral fracture.

IMPRESSION:

1. 8 cm mass of the left lower lobe grows into the left main stem bronchus and
is associated with complete collapse of the left lower lobe. Post- radiation
changes of the left lung and the superior segment of right lung are noted.  

2.  Chronic, nonoccluding embolism within the left main pulmonary artery.

3.  Right adrenal mass, which cannot be further evaluated on this study.
Differential diagnosis includes metastasis  and adenoma. If further evaluation
is required, MRI of the abdomen or CT of the adrenals can be obtained.

4.  7-mm enhancing lesion of the right lobe of the liver.  Differential
diagnoses include flash-filling hemangioma and FNH.

5. Fracture of the proximal left humerus.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PATIENT HISTORY:  ___ years old man with pneumatosis, portal/mesenteric venous
catheter, status post exploratory laparotomy with extended right colectomy for
ischemic colon, prior anastomosis, now status post abdominal closure,
ileostomy with ___, now with fever and tachypnea.  Assess for pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest without infusion of
contrast material agent.  Images were reconstructed as contiguous 5- and
1.25-mm thick axial helical, thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs
projections.

COMPARISON:  Exam is compared to chest CT of ___.

FINDINGS:  Mucoid secretions are in the lower trachea, 4:56, minimally reduced
since ___.  In the left lower lobe, there is new consolidation
involving the superior segment of the left lower lobe and mainly the
posterobasal segment of the same lobe, 4:111 to 4:163.  The bronchus for the
superior segment of the left lower lobe is obstructed by hypodense material
and might be mucus.  Adjacent to the consolidation there is an area of
ground-glass and bronchiolar nodules, 4:125.  These findings are suggestive of
pneumonia.  Right lower lobe atelectasis, 4:172, has improved since ___, now with residual ground-glass and linear opacity.  Two punctate
subpleural nodules in the right upper lobe, 4:50, 54 and 4 mm parafissural
nodule in the right lower lobe are unchanged since ___.  Severe
centrilobular emphysema and bronchial wall thickening are unchanged since the
prior examination.

Thyroid gland is unremarkable.  There is no peripheral or central
lymphadenopathy.  Great vessels have normal in size.  Heart size is moderately
enlarged.  Mild enlargement of all four chambers, but especially the left
chamber.  Aortic valve calcification are mild, 2:34, unchanged since ___.  Mild blood density is compatible with anemia.  There is no pericardial
or pleural effusion.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  Even though this exam is not tailored for abdominal imaging,
it shows normal liver, spleen, adrenals, and pancreas in a patient who has had
cholecystectomy as denoted by metal clips in the gallbladder bed.  NG tube
ends coiled in the fundus of stomach 2:39.

BONES:  There are no bone lesions suspicious for malignancy or infection.

IMPRESSION:
1. Left lower lobe pneumonia, potentialy due to aspiration.
2. Two punctate nodules in the right upper lobe and parafissural nodule in the
right lower lobe are unchanged since ___.  
3. No lymphadenopathy.  
4. Moderate cardiomegaly is unchanged since the prior examination.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History: ___ with SOB, concern for pna vs PE and DVT in RLE u/s 
wt 460 lbs  // History: ___ with SOB, concern for pna vs PE and DVT in RLE u/s
wt 460 lbs

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 6.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 57.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
28.9 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 4.1 s, 32.1 cm; CTDIvol = 23.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 749.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 779 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The exam is limited by body habitus and respiratory motion.

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the segmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar pulmonary
arteries.  Evaluation of the segmental and subsegmental pulmonary arteries is
limited by respiratory motion.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There are ir mildly enlarged left prevascular lymph nodes measuring up to 1.1
cm (02:34).  There is no supraclavicular, axillary, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

There is a 4 mm left upper lobe subpleural pulmonary nodule (02:33).  The
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified.

IMPRESSION:



1. Limited exam.  No evidence of central pulmonary embolism.  Evaluation of
the segmental and subsegmental pulmonary arteries is limited by respiratory
motion.
2. 4 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule.
3. Borderline enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes may be reactive.  Correlate
with clinical symptoms.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Per ___ society guidelines for follow-up of
pulmonary nodules, if no risk factors for malignancy, no followup is
recommended.  If risk factors, recommend followup CT in ___ year.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Increasing oxygen requirement.  Evaluate for PE.

TECHNIQUE:  CTA chest with reformats.

COMPARISON:  CT torso ___ and chest radiograph ___.

FINDINGS:  Calcifications are seen at the coronary vessels.  There is no
pericardial effusion.  The heart is normal in size.  There are no
pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, hilum or axilla
according to CT size criteria.  The airways are patent up to subsegmental
level.  There is worsening emphysema.  There is no pneumonia.  There are
bibasilar atelectases at the lung bases.  There is no pleural effusion or
pneumothorax.  There is a 3-mm nodular opacity in the right middle lobe, 3:37.


There is no filling defect in the pulmonary artery to suggest pulmonary
embolus.  Atherosclerotic changes are seen in the thoracic aorta, with
scattered calcifications in the wall.  Incidental note made of common origin
of the innominate artery and left common carotid ("bovine arch") with
independent origin of the left vertebral artery from the arch. 

In the imaged portion of the abdomen, the liver is hypodense consistent with
steatosis. Cholecystectomy clip is noted. 

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Worsening emphysema.

2.  No pulmonary embolus.

3.  Bibasilar atelectasis at the lung bases.

4.  3-mm nodule in the right middle lobe.  Consider followup in six months to
document stability. Entered in the radiology communication dashboard.

5.  Hepatic steatosis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT TORSO

INDICATION:  History of lymphadenopathy, breast CA with lumpectomy in ___. 
Assess for lymphadenopathy.

COMPARISON:  No prior CT imaging in this institution for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images from the lung apices to the pubic
symphysis displayed with 5-mm slice thickness with oral and IV contrast. 
Coronal and sagittal reformats have been provided.

FINDINGS:

CT THORAX:
There is an enlarged left supraclavicular lymph node measuring 1.7x3.2cm (
3;17).
There is bulky left axillary adenopathy, the largest measuring 2.1 x 1.3 cm
(series 3, image 19).  Surgical clips are noted in the right axilla.  No
breast mass identified.  
No further mediastinal, hilar or internal mammary adenopathy.  
There is a left paravertebral mass identified in the lower thoracic spine
measuring 1 cm in maximum thickness and extending over a craniocaudal length
of 7.6 cm (series 5, image 27).  
There is a large left pleural effusion with compressive atelectasis of the
left lower lobe.  
No pulmonary masses or nodules.  
No pericardial effusion.

CT ABDOMEN:
There is a large retrocrural lymph node measuring 1.5 x 1.4 cm (series 3,
image 46).  
There is a large retroperitoneal conglomerate lymph node mass extending all
the way to the bifurcation with soft tissue encasement of the aorta and IVC.  
The largest nodes are in the aortocaval region measuring 2.6 x 2.6 cm (series
3, image 58) and paraaortic region on the left side, inferior to the left
renal vein measuring 1.7 x 2.9 cm (series 3, image 62).  No further mesenteric
adenopathy identified.  
There is a segment VII low-density lesion too small to characterize (series 3,
image 49). No intra- or extra-hepatic biliary dilatation.  The portal vein is
patent.  
There is splenomegaly of 13 cm (series 5, image 21) with an abnormal
heterogenous attenuation concerning for lymphomatous infiltration. 
Adrenal glands and pancreas are unremarkable.  
There are small bilateral renal cysts.  
Small trace perihepatic and perisplenic ascites is noted.


CT PELVIS:
The retroperitoneal conglomerate mass extends from the level of bifurcation
along the external iliac chains bilaterally to the level of the common femoral
vessels. Again there is soft tissue encasement of the common iliac and
external iliac vessels. The conglomerate mass measures 2.2 x 5.9 cm on the
right side at level of external iliac vessels(series 3, image 89) and 6.1 x
2.3 cm on the left side.  
There is also bulky bilateral inguinal adenopathy, the largest on the right
measuring 1.5 x 2.3cm and on the left side 1.7 x 1.4 cm.  
The bladder, rectum, sigmoid colon are unremarkable.  Small trace of free
fluid is noted within the pelvis.  
No adnexal masses.


CT OSSEOUS SKELETON:  
There is an S-shaped scoliosis of the thoracolumbar spine with multilevel
degenerative change.  
Internal fixation rods are identified in the lower three lumbar vertebrae.


IMPRESSION:  
1. Extensive axillary, supraclavicular and inguinal adenopathy. 
2. There is left paravertebral and retroperitineal conglomerate lymph node
mass.
3. Large left pleural effusion with compressive atelectasis of the left lower
lobe.  
4. Heterogenous attenuation to the spleen concerning for lymphomatous
infiltration.

All of the above findings are concerning for lymphoma. Less likely
differential would include metastatic disease.  Findings posted on the
critical dashboard.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT TORSO

INDICATION:  Lymphoma followup.

COMPARISON:  CT ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images from the lung apices to pubic
symphysis displayed with 5 mm slice thickness with oral and IV contrast. 
Coronal and sagittal reformations have been provided.

FINDINGS:

CT THORAX:
Significant decrease in size of the left supraclavicular and axillary lymph
nodes with no new mediastinal, internal mammary or hilar adenopathy.  
There is decrease in size but residual left pleural effusion with compressive
atelectasis of the left lower lobe again noted.  
There is also decrease in the amount of soft tissue thickening in the left
paravertebral region of the lumbar spine now measuring 3.0 x 5.6 cm (series
301B, image 27) previously 1.0 x 7.6 cm craniocaudal.
4 mm nodule identified in the posterior aspect of the right lower lobe within
an area of gravitational atelectasis (series 2, image 32).  This was not
visualized on prior CT.  However it is likely reactive.  No further lung
masses or nodules identified.


CT ABDOMEN:  
Significant decrease in size in the right retrocrural node, conglomerate lymph
node mass in the retroperitoneum and soft tissue encasement of the aorta and
IVC since prior study.  Two distinct areas of residual adenopathy are seen:
the first in the aortocaval region measuring 0.8 x 1.3 cm (series 2, image 57)
and measuring  7x6mm in the left para-aortic region at the level of the aortic
bifurcation, ( Se 2:70) -  again both are considerably decreased in size since
prior CT. 
Stable low-density lesion identified within segment VII of the liver (series
2, image 2424) stable and unchanged when compared to prior imaging.  No new
liver lesion. Stable post -cholecystectomy intra-and hepatic biliary
dilatation.  
Spleen has also decreased in size since prior imaging and now measures 7.7 cm
(previously 9.5 cm series 2, image 50) and appears more homogenous.  
Both adrenal glands and pancreas are unremarkable.  
Both kidneys are normal apart from a simple cyst in the interpolar region of
the right kidney (series 2, image 54) stable and unchanged when compared to
prior imaging.  

CT PELVIS:
Again significant decrease in the amount of retroperitoneal conglomerate
adenopathy extending along the right common and external iliac veins
bilaterally.  This is now almost unmeasurable apart from some mild stranding
around the vessels. 
Small trace of free fluid is noted within the pelvis.  
Rectum, sigmoid colon, bladder and both adnexa are unremarkable.  
Dystrophic calcification seen in a possibly atrophic uterus, stable. 
There is also significant decrease in size in the inguinal adenopathy, the
largest node now measuring 5 x 12 mm in the right inguinal region previously
measuring 1.5 x 2.3 cm (series 2, image 94).

CT OSSEOUS SKELETON:  
S-shaped scoliosis of the thoracolumbar spine with multilevel degenerative
change noted. There is an internal fixation device noted in the lower three
lumbar vertebrae.  
No new osseous destructive lesion.


IMPRESSION:  
1. Extensive reduction in size of the previously described axillary,
retroperitoneal and inguinal adenopathy since prior imaging consistent with
disease response. 

2. Spleen now appears homogenous and has also decreased in size. 

3. Residual but smaller left pleural effusion with compressive atelectasis of
the left lower lobe.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Patient with history of right breast cancer treated with
lumpectomy and radiation.  Patient now with lymphoma, restaging.

COMPARISON:  Comparison is made to CT torso performed ___ and
___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial contrast-enhanced images were obtained from the
thoracic inlet to the pelvic outlet.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were
performed.

FINDINGS:  There is a stable 6.8 mm hypodensity in the right thyroid lobe,
unchanged from ___ (3:10).  There is no supraclavicular, axillary,
mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy present.  Airways are patent to the
subsegmental level.  Heart size is normal without evidence of pericardial
effusion.  The great vessels appear unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic
calcifications are noted throughout the thoracic aorta without associated
aneurysmal change.  Posterior to the right breast, there is focal right upper
lobe pleural thickening with minimal associated intraseptal thickening, likely
reflecting post-radiation change (3:39).  In the superior right upper lobe
there is a stable 4.6 mm pulmonary nodule (3:26), unchanged from ___.  Minimal atelectactic changes are seen in the dependent portions of the
lung and surrounding the descending aorta.  There is a decrease in the left-
sided pleural effusion, now small in size.

CT ABDOMEN:  In hepatic segment VII, there is a stable 5 mm hypodensity likely
representing a simple hepatic cyst.  No other focal lesions are identified
within the liver.  There is no intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  The
gallbladder has been surgically removed.  The common bile duct is slightly
prominent, likely reflecting post-cholecystectomy change.  The pancreas is
unremarkable.  The bilateral adrenal glands are of normal limb thickness
without convex margin to suggest mass.  Bilateral kidneys are normal in size.
There is a stable simple renal cyst in the right interpolar region.  No
hydroureter seen.  The stomach, small and large bowel within the abdomen are
unremarkable.  There is no retroperitoneal, mesenteric or portacaval
lymphadenopathy identified.

CT PELVIS:  The appendix is not visualized, though there are no secondary
signs to suggest inflammation.  There is no free fluid within the pelvis.  The
rectum is unremarkable.  No significant pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy is
demonstrated.  The bladder is compressed and not fully evaluated.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  Redemonstration of S-shaped thoracolumbar scoliosis with
multilevel degenerative change including stable Grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4
on L5.  Internal fixation devices are noted throughout the lower lumbar
vertebrae without evidence of hardware failure.  No suspicious lytic or
blastic lesions are identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No lymphadenopathy identified on current study, representing a decrease
compared to prior study and overall consistent with continued disease
response.
2.  Stable right thyroid nodule.
3.  Decreased left pleural effusion, now small in size.
4.  Right upper lobe pleural thickening, likely reflecting post-radiation
change.
5.  Stable subcentimeter right pulmonary nodule.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Abnormal chest examination with diffuse crackles and recent
abdominal CT showing periaortic lymphadenopathy.

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___, CT abdomen and pelvis ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical MDCT images were obtained of the chest after the
administration of IV contrast.  Multiplanar reformatted images were generated
in the coronal and sagittal planes as well as axial maximum intensity
projection images.

DLP:  409.5 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:  The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  The trachea is midline and the
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Two 4 mm nodules in the right
lower lobe on image 4:138, 145 are stable since ___.  Numerous other
scattered 1-3 mm nodules are present bilaterally.  Subpleural bullae are
present in bilateral apices as well as abutting the lateral right middle lobe.
There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Heart size is mildly enlarged.  Mild coronary artery calcifications as well as
atherosclerotic calcifications along the normal caliber aortic arch. 
Pulmonary artery is normal caliber.  There are no enlarged supraclavicular,
axillary, hilar or mediastinal lymph nodes by CT size criteria.

This study is not tailored for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic structures. 
however, within those limitations, the visualized portions of the liver,
gallbladder, spleen, adrenal glands, pancreas, and kidneys are grossly
unremarkable.  Again appreciated is significant periaortic retroperitoneal
lymphadenopathy with the largest node measuring 2.3 x 1.2 cm (4:235),
unchanged from recent prior examination in ___.  Lymphadenopathy is
better evaluated on recent abdominal CT examination.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no focal blastic or lytic lesions in the
visualized osseous structures concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Two 4 mm right lower lobe pulmonary nodules unchanged from ___. 
Multiple other scattered 1-3 mm nodules bilaterally.  Follow-up chest CT in 6
months is recommended.
2.  Grossly stable retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy which remains concerning
for malignancy, particularly lymphoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PATIENT HISTORY:  ___ years old man with history of abdominal pain and enlarged
lymphadenopathy.  Please evaluate for increase in size of the lymphadenopathy
or other changes.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisition over entire thorax in inspiration after
administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque nonionic intravenous contrast material
agent.  Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with multiplanar
reconstruction.

COMPARISON:  Exam is compared to chest CT of ___ and ___.

FINDINGS:  The thyroid gland is unremarkable.   There are no pathologically
enlarged lymph nodes in the axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar
regions.  Ascending aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal, S2:I31.  Heart
size is normal.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  Mild isolated
coronary artery calcifications, mainly in the distal LAD, S2:I37, are stable
since ___.

Abdominal findings are described in report of concurrent CT abdomen and
pelvis, clip ___.  1.3 x 1.5 cm hiatal hernia, S2:I50, is unchanged since
___.

BONES:  There are no bone lesions suspicious for malignancy or infection.

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to segmental level bilaterally. 
Centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema are stable since ___, S4:I45. 
All the following nodules are stable since ___ and therefore benign,
series 4:  Images 46, 49, 65, 77, 80, 86, 97, 145, 148.  The following nodules
are stable since ___, S4:I83, 133, and are not concerning for
malignancy.  Linear opacity in the posterobasal segment of the left lower
lobe, S4:I215, is due to parenchymal scarring.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Most of the nodules are stable since ___ and therefore benign. 
Two nodules are stable since ___ and are likely benign as well.
2.  There is no peripheral or central lymphadenopathy.
3.  Abdominal finding are described in concurrent CT abdomen and pelvis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with chest pain// eval for PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 418 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  The heart is enlarged.  Dilation of the main pulmonary
artery to 3.2 cm is noted, and can be seen in pulmonary arterial hypertension.
No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: A prevascular node measures 1.3 cm (02:30). 
Hilar lymph nodes are top normal in size bilaterally.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Diffuse ground-glass opacity throughout both lungs with some
interlobular septal thickening is most compatible with pulmonary edema. 
Bibasilar atelectasis is also noted.  Diffuse peribronchial thickening is
suggestive of chronic small airways disease.  The airways are patent to the
level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.  The right hemidiaphragm is
significantly elevated.

BASE OF NECK: A 2.3 cm hypodense left thyroid nodule is noted.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: Sclerotic appearance of the T11 vertebral body is noted.? There is no
acute fracture.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2. Diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities with interlobular septal
thickening is most consistent with pulmonary edema.
3. Dilation of the main pulmonary artery is noted, and may be seen in
pulmonary arterial hypertension.
4. Diffuse peribronchial thickening suggestive of chronic small airways
disease.
5. Enlarged mediastinal and prominent hilar lymph nodes are nonspecific.
6. 2.3 cm hypodense left thyroid nodule.  According to current ___
College of Radiology guidelines, a nonemergent thyroid ultrasound can be
obtained for further evaluation.
7. Sclerotic appearance of the T11 vertebral body is probably a hemangioma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST; CT ABD AND PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of breast cancer and sacral wound
who presents with orthostatic hypotension in the setting of a 35pound weight
loss over a few months.// Concerned about occult malignancy vs occult
infection

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the chest
abdomen and pelvis in the portal venous phase. Reformatted coronal and
sagittal images through the chest, abdomen, and pelvis, and axial maximal
intensity projection images of the chest were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.0 s, 65.7 cm; CTDIvol = 12.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 802.1
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 7.8 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 43.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
21.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 826 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST); Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.0 s, 65.7 cm; CTDIvol = 12.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 802.1
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 7.8 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 43.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
21.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 826 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA chest ___ CT abdomen pelvis ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:
Patient is status post right thyroidectomy.  2.2 cm hypodense nodule in the
left thyroid lobe is not significantly changed compared to ___.
Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, and hilar lymph nodes are not
pathologically enlarged.  Thoracic aorta and main pulmonary artery are within
normal size limits.  Heart is enlarged.  There is no pericardial effusion.

Trace right pleural effusion is present.  Diffuse bronchial wall thickening is
noted.  Atelectasis is mild in bilateral lung bases.  Pleuroparenchymal
fibrotic changes along the anterior aspect of left lower lobe likely reflect
radiation change.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout.  1.5
cm hypodense lesion in segment 4 A (02:39) is compatible with biliary
hamartoma or hepatic cyst.  Multiple other lesions are too small to be fully
characterized.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation. There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS:  Left adrenal gland has been removed.  Right adrenal gland is
unremarkable.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
Multiple hypodense lesions are identified in bilateral kidneys.  The largest
lesion measures 1.2 cm.  The larger lesions are compatible with simple renal
cysts, however many of the smaller lesions are too small to be fully
characterized.  There is no hydronephrosis.  There is no perinephric
abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The colon and
rectum are within normal limits.  The appendix is normal.  There is no free
intraperitoneal fluid or free air.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Uterus is unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: Compression deformity is noted at L1 vertebral body. 
Patient is status post bilateral mastectomy.  Fat containing anterior left
lateral ventral hernia is noted (2:70).  Several nodular soft tissue densities
in the anterior abdominal subcutaneous fat measuring up to 0.9 cm are
nonspecific and may reflect injection granulomas.  Subcortical cystic changes
in the left sacroiliac joint is likely degenerative, and stable.  Synovial
herniation pit is noted at the left femoral superior head and neck junction. 
Mild superior endplate compression deformity of L1 is probably chronic. 
Sclerosis probably reflecting a hemangioma is unchanged in the T11 vertebral
body.  Soft tissue stranding lies immediately deep to the gluteal cleft but no
bone destruction.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidecne of malignancy or infection is identified in the chest or
abdomen.
2. 2.2 cm hypodense nodule in the left thyroid lobe is not significantly
changed compared to ___.
3. Mildv superior endplate compression deformity of L1 is probably probably
chronic.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man status post MVC with multiple intra-abdominal
injuries, assess for interval change.

COMPARISONS:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained from the thoracic inlet
to the pubic symphysis after the uneventful administration of oral and
intravenous contrast with Gastrografin.  Coronal and sagittal reformations
were prepared.

Examination is limited due to the arms being down and up against the gantry
causing marked streak artifact along the right side of the upper torso.

DLP: 1305 mGy-cm

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Thyroid gland is normal in appearance.  The
aorta and major branches appear patent with normal three-vessel branching
arch.  The heart and pericardium are normal without pericardial effusion. 
Central venous catheter appears to terminate in the anticipated level of
superior cavoatrial junction.  A right subclavian central venous catheter
appears to terminate at the superior cavoatrial junction.  Endotracheal tube
terminates appropriately within the mid trachea.  Nasogastric tube courses
into the stomach.  The esophagus is normal.  The trachea and central airways
are patent to the segmental level.  The lungs are clear with the exception of
bibasilar atelectasis versus consolidation and perhaps small simple pleural
effusions.  No pneumothorax is seen.  A 1-cm left axillary lymph node is
noted, likely reactive given its interval increase in size from the previous
examination.  No additional sites of adenopathy are seen in the axillary,
mediastinal, hilar or supraclavicular regions.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  The liver is diffusely hypoattenuating,
compatible with fatty deposition.  Much of the posterior right lobe appear
slightly more prominently hypoenhancing with numerous locules of air in
addition to branching tubular structures of air, which may reflect biliary or
vascular structures.  Subhepatic and right paracolic gutter simple fluid is
with scattered foci of air are seen with air also collecting along the
inferior border of the right hemidiaphragm.  The paracolic gutter fluid
extends dependently into the pelvis. Additionally fluid is interposed within
bowel loops in the mesentery and to a lesser degree in the left paracolic
gutter. 

Amidst this, the gallbladder is not definitively visualized.  The pancreas and
spleen appear normal.  There is some mild stranding about the left adrenal
gland.  The right adrenal gland lesion is not notably changed in size
measuring 3.3 x 1.9 cm reflecting a hematoma or focal nodule.  The kidneys
enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically without focal lesion or
hydronephrosis.  

The stomach, small and large bowel demonstrate postoperative changes of
ileocecectomy and primary anastomosis.  No evidence of contrast extravasation
is seen; however, contrast has not reached the anastomosis.  No mesenteric or
retroperitoneal adenopathy is seen.  The aorta is poorly opacified due to the
timing of the study and other technical limitations already described without
evidence of filling defect or other traumatic injury.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The bladder is decompressed with a Foley
catheter.  Fluid is seen collecting dependently in the pelvis in continuity
with the other sites of free fluid.  The prostate and rectum are unremarkable.
Small bilateral fat-containing inguinal herniae are noted.  Stranding in the
soft tissues diffusely is compatible with anasarca.  Surgical staples are seen
reflecting the midline surgical incision from laparotomy.  The prostate and
rectum are unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  The cervical spine fractures are not assessed on this
study.  Left-sided T5, T6 and L1 transverse process fractures are noted. 
Bilateral T12 transverse process fractures are not well imaged on this current
study.  Bilateral L2, L3, and L4 transverse process fractures as well as
right-sided L5 transverse process fractures are again noted.  Multiple rib
fractures are seen including anterior fracture of the left fourth rib and
fracture dislocations anteriorly at the costochondral junction of the left
fifth, sixth, seventh and eighth ribs.  On the right side, there are mildly
displaced lateral sixth and seventh rib fractures as well as nondisplaced
anterior fifth and sixth rib fractures of the chondral portion of the ribs.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Heterogeneous hypoattenuation of the posterior right hepatic lobe with
foci of air throughout, which is concerning for infarct and necrosis,
superinfection cannot be excluded. This area is in continuity with the
moderate amount of free fluid in the right paracolic gutter, as well as in the
pelvis, mesentery and left paracolic gutters without rim enhancing collection
to suggest abscess. 
2.  Status post ileocecectomy with intact-appearing anastomosis, though the
oral contrast did not reach the anastomosis to access for extravasation.
3.  Multiple sites of bony injury as described without evidence of newly
evident traumatic injuries.
4.  Unchanged right adrenal hematoma vs nodule. 
5.  Bibasilar atelectasis vs consolidation

Findings discussed with Dr. ___ by Dr. ___ at 1720 on ___ by
phone.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Evaluate for changes in disease status in a patient with
metastatic renal cell carcinoma on Sutent therapy after cycle #2.

COMPARISON:  MRI of the thoracic spine from ___ and CT of the
torso from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images of chest, abdomen and pelvis were
obtained after administration of 100 cc Omnipaque intravenous contrast
material.  Enteric contrast also given.  Three-minute delay abdomen images
obtained.  Coronal and sagittal reformats provided and reviewed.

DLP:  1803.92 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  The visualized portion of the thyroid gland appears normal.  There is
no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  Thoracic
aorta is normal in caliber, as is the pulmonary artery.  There are
atherosclerotic calcifications in the aortic arch but the arch vessels are
patent.  The heart size is normal and there is no pericardial effusion.  The
tracheobronchial tree is normal.  There are no concerning pulmonary nodules. 
There are small focal areas of ground glass and nodular opacity in the right
lung (2:21, 24).  These findings are new.  There is a 2 mm pulmonary nodule in
the left lower lobe which is smaller than on the prior examination (2:25). 
There is no pneumothorax or pleural effusion.

ABDOMEN:  There is a small hiatal hernia.  The liver enhances homogeneously. 
There is a vague subcentimeter hypodensity in the right lobe of the liver
which was present on the prior examination and is too small to characterize by
CT (2:40).  There is no bile duct dilation.  The patient is status post
cholecystectomy.  The pancreas, spleen, and adrenal glands appear normal.  A
1.8 cm simple cyst is seen in the upper pole of the right kidney.  The right
kidney is otherwise normal.  There is a 3.1 x 2.8 cm hypoenhancing mass in the
left mid kidney which extends from the renal medulla to the cortex.  There is
at least one location in which there is no cortical tissue between the mass
and the surrounding perirenal fat and there appears to be some bulging of the
capsule at this location.  Invasion to the perirenal space cannot be excluded
(2:64).  A 1.3 cm simple cyst is seen in the anterior upper pole of the left
kidney, unchanged (2:61).

The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel appear normal aside from colonic
diverticulosis without evidence of diverticulitis and an anterior right
abdominal wall hernia into which normal appearing transverse colon has
herniated.  This hernia has progressed from the prior examination when it
merely contained fat.

The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber with patent main branches and minimal
atherosclerotic calcifications.  The portal vein is patent.  There is a
cluster of enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes near the origin of the left
renal artery measuring 1.9 x 1.1 cm, approximately the same size as on the
prior examination (2:61).  Further inferiorly, there is an enlarged 1.8 x 1.1
cm left paraaortic lymph node which is again stable in size (2:65).  There is
no new retroperitoneal or intraperitoneal lymphadenopathy.  There is no
ascites or free air.

PELVIS:  The rectum, prostate, seminal vesicles, and bladder appear normal. 
There is no pelvic mass, lymphadenopathy, or free fluid.

MUSCULOSKELETAL:  Again noted are lytic metastatic lesions in the T2 and L2
vertebral bodies which have caused pathologic compression fractures, not
significantly progressed from the prior examination (602b:45, 44).  Vague
sclerotic lesions can be seen in T7 (602b:44) and L1 (602b:47).  These are
better evaluated on the MRI.  There are severe degenerative changes of L2, L3
and L4 with another possible sclerotic lesion in L3 (602b:42).  The 1.4 x 1.4
cm sclerotic lesion in the right aspect of the sacrum is not significantly
changed from the prior examination.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Stable appearance of the primary left renal tumor which extends to the
capsular surface and where invasion into the perirenal space cannot be fully
excluded.
2.  Stable appearance of two pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the left
retroperitoneal space.
3.  New areas of focal ground-glass and nodular opacity in the right lung has
the appearance of a developing infectious or inflammatory process.
4.  A 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the left lung is smaller than on the prior
study.
5.  Multiple stable skeletal metastases as outlined above, no new lesions
identified.
6.  Anterior abdominal wall hernia through which transverse colon has now
herniated.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT, ___

HISTORY:  Renal cell carcinoma, on therapy.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
imaging of the abdomen and pelvis to be reported separately on intravenous
infusion of 130 cc Omnipaque nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed
as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs images, compared to CT imaging of the chest since at least
___, most recently ___.

FINDINGS:

Small region of bronchiolar nodulation in the right upper lobe present on
___ has resolved.  Right lung is otherwise clear.

Punctate lesion, left lower lobe, 2:25, cited on the previous study has not
changed in the past two months.  I am not sure if it was actually larger on
___, when there was a fair amount of motion at the level of
imaging, but it is possible that it is a shrinking solitary metastasis.  Aside
from mild linear atelectasis at the base, left lung is otherwise clear. 
Asbestos-related pleural plaque, predominates in the left hemithorax, 4:90. 
It is accompanied by very mild smooth thickening of the left costal pleural
surface, for example 4:108, which was present on ___, 2:34.  Left
hemithorax is slightly smaller than the right, also unchanged.  There is no
discrete pleural mass or effusion to suggest pleural malignancy, and there are
no findings of asbestosis in the lungs.

As before, contrast is retained in the esophagus, with no focal lesion. This
study is not designed for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, which will be rendered
in a separate report.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Solitary tiny left lung nodule, unchanged since ___ could
conceivably be an isolated involuted pulmonary metastasis.  There are no new
lung lesions, adenopathy or any other evidence of active intrathoracic
malignancy.  Previous minimal bronchiolitis in the right lung has resolved.
2.  Asbestos-related pleural plaques, no evidence of pleural malignancy, lung
cancer, or asbestosis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Evaluate focal aortic dissection identified on noncontrast CT.

COMPARISONS:  Noncontrast CT of the abdomen and pelvis, ___ at
00:34.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial MDCT images were obtained through the torso after
the administration of IV contrast per the pre-stent protocol.  Sagittal and
coronal reformatted images were obtained and reviewed.

FINDINGS:  There is a small hypodense lesion with calcifications within the
left lobe of the thyroid gland.  The thyroid is otherwise unremarkable.  There
is no axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The heart is minimally
enlarged.  There is no pericardial effusion.  Atherosclerotic calcifications
are noted in the coronary arteries and the aortic valve.  The thoracic aorta
is normal in caliber without evidence of aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is present along the arch and descending thoracic aorta.  The
pulmonary trunk is normal in diameter.

The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is mild bibasilar
atelectasis.  There is no consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. 
There are multiple pulmonary nodules.  There is a pleural-based nodule at the
right apex which measures 4 mm (2, 23).  There is a 3-mm right middle lobe
nodule (2, 45).  There is a 2-mm pleural-based right middle lobe nodule (2,
50).  There is also a second 3 mm pleural-based right middle lobe nodule (2,
54).  There is a 6-mm right lower lobe nodule (2, 49).  There is a 3-mm
pleural-based left upper lobe nodule (2, 44).  Finally, there is a 6-mm left
lower lobe nodule (2, 57).

ABDOMEN:  The liver is normal in shape and contour.  There are no focal
hepatic lesions.  The portal vein is patent.  There is no intra- or
extra-hepatic biliary duct dilation.  The gallbladder is unremarkable.  The
spleen is normal in size.  There are three hypodense lesions noted within the
spleen, the largest measuring 6 mm (2, 108 and 89).  These are likely small
hemangiomas, although are too small to fully characterize.  The pancreas is
normal.  The bilateral adrenal glands are normal.  In the right kidney, there
are multiple hypodense lesions with the density of simple cysts.  The largest
is exophytic off the midpole measuring 3.7 cm (2, 132).  Two smaller hypodense
lesions measure 1.2 cm and 1.5 cm, and also have the density of simple cysts. 
Several other small hypodensities are too small to fully characterize.  The
left kidney is normal.  There is no evidence of hydronephrosis.  The kidneys
enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically.

The stomach and small bowel are unremarkable.  There is normal bowel wall
enhancement.  There are no surrounding inflammatory changes.  There is no
evidence of obstruction.  There is no free air or free fluid.  There is no
mesenteric, abdominal, or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

PELVIS:  There is diverticulosis without evidence of diverticulitis.  The
large bowel is otherwise unremarkable, without evidence of obstruction,
masses, or inflammatory changes.  The appendix is normal.  The bladder is
unremarkable.  The prostate is minimally enlarged, measuring 5.0 cm.  Multiple
phleboliths are present.  There is no free fluid in the pelvis.  There is no
pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

CTA:  The ascending and descending thoracic aorta are normal in caliber with
moderate atherosclerotic disease.  There are mild atherosclerotic
calcifications at the takeoff of the celiac artery, SMA, and bilateral renal
arteries.  There is no significant narrowing identified.  There is a single
right renal artery.  There are two left renal arteries, with the small
accessory artery supplying the lower pole (2, 129), (300B, 35).  There is
extensive atherosclerotic disease along the descending aorta.

In the infrarenal location, approximately 3 cm from the lowest renal artery
(which is the accessory left renal artery), there is evidence of a focal
aortic dissection.  In its widest axial width, it measures 3.6 cm (2, 150). 
In the craniocaudal dimension, the dissection extends approximately 4.7 cm
(300B, 33).  The proximal end is approximately 1.3 cm from the bifurcation
(300B, 32).  There is evidence of contrast filling the false lumen, likely
from a superior fenestration (2, 145).  Additionally, there may be small
fenestrations inferiorly (2, 155).  The ___ arises from the true lumen and is
patent with normal flow.  There is no evidence of extravasation outside of the
true or false lumen.  There is no surrounding stranding.  There is no evidence
of rupture.

The common iliac, internal iliac, and external iliac arteries are widely
patent with moderate-to-severe atherosclerotic disease, but no narrowing or
occlusion.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic osseous
lesions.  Multilevel degenerative changes are noted in the lower lumbar spine.
No fracture is identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  3.6 cm focal infrarenal aortic dissection with fenestrations and contrast
entering the false lumen.  There is no evidence of rupture.  The age is
indeterminate.
2.  Severe atherosclerotic disease.
3.  Multiple right renal cysts.
4.  Small probable splenic hemangiomas.
5.  Multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures 6 mm.  Per
___ guidelines, if the patient is low risk, recommend a followup CT in
12 months.  If there is a history of risk factors such as cancer or smoking,
recommend a followup CT in six months.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WANDW/O CANDRECONS, NON-CORONARY

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with diaphoretic, tachy, desating  // r/p PE

TECHNIQUE:  Spiral acquisition performed after administration of 100 cc of
Omnipaque intravenous contrast.  Multiplanar reformats were created, including
right and left oblique MIP reconstructions.

DOSE:   This study involved 5 CT acquisition phases with dose indices as
follows:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 1.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.8
mGy-cm.
   4) Stationary Acquisition 6.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 18.2 mGy (Body) DLP =
9.1 mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 3.7 s, 28.9 cm; CTDIvol = 14.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 423.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 434 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

No pulmonary embolus.  Scattered coronary arterial calcifications. 
Interatrial septal closure device.  Normal caliber thoracic aorta. 
Subcentimeter mediastinal and hilar nodes.

Visualized upper abdomen is unremarkable.

No pleural effusions.  Central airways patent.  Atelectasis of the left
greater than right lung bases, without definite focal consolidation.  Mild
biapical scarring.  No pulmonary mass.  2 mm pulmonary nodule right middle
lobe (3, 135).

Degenerative endplate changes of the spine.

IMPRESSION:


-No pulmonary embolus.
-Atelectasis of left more than right lung bases, without definite focal
consolidation.
-2 mm pulmonary nodule right middle lobe.  If the patient has high-risk for
lung cancer, a 12 month followup chest CT is suggested.  If low risk, no
further followup is needed for this finding.
