radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with likely sarcoid and significant parotid
swelling and some difficulty swallowing, parotid eval and lymph node
evaluation// ___ year old woman with likely sarcoid and significant parotid
swelling and some difficulty swallowing, parotid eval and lymph node
evaluation

TECHNIQUE:  ___ MDCT images were obtained through the chest without the
administration of IV contrast per the HRCT prone and supine protocol. 
Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and sagittal axis were generated and
reviewed.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The heart size is normal.  The pericardium is intact
without evidence of an effusion.  The caliber of the ascending aorta and main
pulmonary artery are unremarkable.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: There is no axillary, or supraclavicular
lymphadenopathy.  Enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are seen measuring up to 1
cm in the right paratracheal region.  Additional prominent prevascular, and
anterior mediastinal lymph nodes are seen measuring up to 0.8 cm in short
axis.  Enlarged subcarinal lymph nodes are seen, measuring up to 1.3 cm. 
Enlarged bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy is seen, although difficult to
measure given the lack of intravenous contrast, measuring up to at least 1.3
cm.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  There is no
evidence of significant airway narrowing by the bilateral lymphadenopathy. 
There is extensive bilateral perilymphatic distribution of micro nodules in an
apicobasal gradient.  The micro nodules are seen extending in a
peribronchovascular distribution as well as along the subpleural interstitium
and interlobular septa.

A 0.6 cm nodule is seen within the left lower lobe, series 4, image 191. 
Additional interstitial thickening with nodularity is seen along the left
major fissure.  Along the medial aspect of the left upper lobe, series 4,
image 75, a 0.7 cm nodule is seen.

BASE OF NECK: The thyroid is normal.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the unenhanced upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. Extensive bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy and mediastinal lymphadenopathy
in conjunction with diffuse bilateral perilymphatic distribution of micro
nodules along the subpleural interstitium and interlobular septa, could be
seen in the setting of sarcoidosis.  Differential considerations also include
lymphoma and neoplastic etiologies cannot be excluded.  Recommend tissue
diagnosis for further evaluation.
2. Discrete nodules are seen within the left lung measuring up to 0.7 cm.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Tissue diagnosis is recommended for further evaluation. 
Sampling may be amenable to transbronchial biopsy.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Restaging scan in a patient with ovarian cancer status post
TAH/BSO.  Evaluate for pulmonary nodule given findings on recent chest
radiograph.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.  CTA of the abdomen and
pelvis from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were
obtained after administration of intravenous and enteric contrast. 
Three-minute delay images of the abdomen also obtained.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats reviewed.

DLP:  1291.51 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:  The right lobe of thyroid gland is heterogeneous and enlarged, with
at least one well defined 1.1 x 1.0 cm hypodense nodule (2:5).  Another nodule
inferior to the isthmus measures 8 mm in transverse diameter (2:9).  There is
no supraclavicular, axillary, hilar, or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  The
heart size is normal and there is a trace pericardial effusion (2:38).  There
is a large hiatal hernia.  There are no large pulmonary arterial filling
defects.  There are numerous atherosclerotic calcifications in the great
vessels, which are all of normal caliber and patent.

The lungs are clear.  The apparent pulmonary nodule seen on recent chest
radiograph corresponds to calcified costal cartilage (300B:15).  No pulmonary
nodules are identified.  There is no diffuse airspace abnormality.  There is
no pleural effusion.

ABDOMEN:  There are no focal liver lesions.  The patient is status post
cholecystectomy.  The adrenal glands, spleen, and pancreas are normal.  A
subcentimeter hypodensity in the right kidney is too small to characterize
(2:62).  There are several punctate cortical hypodensities in the left kidney,
also too small to characterize (2:57, 63, 66).  There is bilateral prominence
of the renal pelvices, but no ureteral dilation.  There is no caliectasis. 
The kidneys enhance uniformly aside from the hypodense lesions, likely cysts,
mentioned previously.

The intra-abdominal loops of small and large bowel are of normal caliber,
without surrounding fat stranding, mass, or wall thickening.  There is no free
fluid, fluid collection, or air within the abdomen.  An 8 mm x 4 mm right
paraaortic lymph node does not meet CT size criteria for enlargement (2:58). 
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber but contains very dense
atherosclerotic calcifications.  The main branches are patent.  The portal,
splenic, and mesenteric veins are patent.

There is no omental nodularity noted.

PELVIS:  The patient is status post hysterectomy, oophorectomy.  No new masses
identified in the pelvis.  There is trace pelvic free fluid but no
lymphadenopathy.  The rectum and sigmoid colon appear normal.  The urinary
bladder is unremarkable.

Rotary scoliosis of the lumbar spine is again noted.  There is a heterogeneous
sclerotic lesion in the right femur, likely an enchondroma or prior bone
infarct (___), stable from the prior examination.  There are extensive
severe degenerative changes of the right hip with bone-on-bone appearance and
subchondral cystic changes.  There is moderate degenerative change at the left
hip.  Marked degenerative disc disease accompanies this scoliosis in the
spine.  There are no focal lesions concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of local or metastatic disease recurrence.
2.  A prominent, but not pathologically enlarged, right paraaortic lymph node
should be examined on subsequent imaging studies.
3.  The pulmonary opacity seen on the prior radiograph corresponds to costal
cartilage calcification.  No pulmonary nodules identified.
4.  Multiple thyroid nodules, which may be better characterized by ultrasound
if clinically indicated.

radiology_facts.report_text:
STUDY:  CT torso with contrast and reconstructions.

INDICATION:  Weight loss.

COMPARISON:  CTA abdomen ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial acquired images were obtained from the thoracic inlet
to the symphysis with oral and IV contrast administration.  Multiplanar
reformatted images were obtained.

CONTRAST:  100 cc Optiray 350.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  The right lobe of the thyroid is visualized with a
tiny 5-mm hypoattenuating nodule within.  The left lobe is not visualized,
possibly secondary to prior resection.  No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
adenopathy is detected.  In the right cardiophrenic angle is a small region of
linear enhancement approximately 1 cm in length which was also noted on
previous study and is most consistent with patchy atelectasis in a region of
medial segment middle lobe scarring. No worrisome nodules or opacities are
detected within the lungs. There are no effusions.  The heart is grossly
unremarkable without pericardial effusion. No filling defects are identified
in the pulmonary arteries and no intimal flap detected within the aorta.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST AND RECONSTRUCTIONS:  The liver is normal in
appearance without focal lesion.  The gallbladder, right kidney, pancreas, and
loops of large and small bowel are grossly unremarkable.  There is a tiny
hypoattenuating 2 mm lesion within the interpolar region of the left kidney
which cannot be further characterized and may represent a cyst.  There is
generally stable appearance to a 1.9 cm calcified splenic artery aneurysm.
Mild-to-moderate calcific atherosclerotic plaque is present within the
abdominal aorta and iliac branches without aneurysmal dilatation.  No free
fluid or free air is detected within the abdomen.  There is no adenopathy
detected, given the limitation of paucity of intra-abdominal fat.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND RECONSTRUCTIONS:  The rectum, sigmoid colon,
bladder and distal ureters are grossly unremarkable.  Pelvic loops of
opacified small bowel are normal in appearance.  The pelvic organs are
difficult to evaluate on CT; however, the uterus is anteverted and grossly
unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is intervertebral body disc space narrowing and
vacuum phenomenon at the L5-S1 level consistent with degenerative change.  No
worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesions are detected.  There is a lesion most
consistent with an osseous hemangioma in the T8 vertebral body.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Stable appearance to a calcified splenic artery aneurysm.

2.  Atherosclerosis of the abdominal aorta without aneurysmal dilatation.

3.  No findings to explain patient's weight loss.

4.  Tiny 5-mm hypoattenuating nodule within the right lobe of the thyroid. The
left lobe is not visualized, possibly secondary to previous resection.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is intervertebral body disc space narrowing and
vacuum phenomenon at the L5-S1 level consistent with degenerative change.  No
worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesions are detected.  There is a lesion most
consistent with an osseous hemangioma in the T8 vertebral body.
sections.impression:
1.  Stable appearance to a calcified splenic artery aneurysm.

2.  Atherosclerosis of the abdominal aorta without aneurysmal dilatation.

3.  No findings to explain patient's weight loss.

4.  Tiny 5-mm hypoattenuating nodule within the right lobe of the thyroid. The
left lobe is not visualized, possibly secondary to previous resection.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with thoracic aortic aneurysm, s/p TAVR  //
assess TAA size

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.4 s, 41.4 cm; CTDIvol = 5.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 230.5
mGy-cm.
   2) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 8.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.8
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 6.7 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 173.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
34.7 mGy-cm.
   4) Stationary Acquisition 2.4 s, 27.6 cm; CTDIvol = 33.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,164.6 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,432 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA torso from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: No central pulmonary embolism.  The thoracic aorta is
patent and without evidence of dissection or intramural hematoma.  Pulmonary
artery remains mildly enlarged, which could reflect pulmonary arterial
hypertension.  The heart is normal in size.  No pericardial effusion. An
intra-atrial Amplatz device is redemonstrated.  The right atrium, left atrium,
and right ventricle appear normal.  Interval placement of aortic valve
prosthesis, which appears grossly unremarkable.  Left ventricular hypertrophy
is slightly improved compared to the study from ___.  Coronary
artery calcifications are moderate to severe.

 3D LAB MEASUREMENTS (All measurements accurate to +/-5%):
Aortic Root:  29.5 x 24.2 mm
Sinus of Valsalva:  47.2 x 36.7 mm
Sinotubular Junction:  43.8 x 40.3 mm
Mid-Ascending Aorta:  47.5 x 44.9 mm
Ascending Aorta Proximal to Innominate:  35.7 x 32.7 mm
Aortic Arch Proximal to Left Common Carotid:  35.0 x 30.5 mm
Aortic Arch Proximal to Left Subclavian:  30.9 x 26.8 mm
Aortic Arch Distal to Left Subclavian:  28.6 x 26.1 mm
Mid-Descending Aorta:  31.3 x 28.1 mm
Distal Descending Aorta:   27.8 x 25.4 mm
Main Pulmonary Artery:  38.9 x 34.1 mm


AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Several sub 3 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodules are
unchanged (series 3:11, 3:29).  A 4 mm left lower lobe pulmonary nodule is
unchanged (series 3:53).  A 3 mm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule is
unchanged (series 3:17).  No new or growing pulmonary nodule.  Lungs are clear
without masses or areas of parenchymal opacification.  The airways are patent
to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is notable for gallstones in
the partially imaged gallbladder, without obvious evidence for acute
cholecystitis.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is
no acute fracture.  Patient is status post left breast excisional biopsy, with
fat stranding and a coarse calcification in this area likely representing
postoperative change.


IMPRESSION:


1. Minimally increased aneurysmal dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta
to 47.5 x 44.9 mm, previously measuring up to 46 mm.
2. Minimally improved appearance of left ventricular hypertrophy.
3. Multiple stable sub 3 mm pulmonary nodules bilaterally dating back to
___, which do not require dedicated follow-up.
4. Cholelithiasis.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Consider referral to aortic center.
Follow up CTA chest in 6 months with ECG gating.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT-guided lung biopsy.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with h/o sarcoidosis and pancreatic cancer and
suspicious RUL nodule.  Attempted biopsy at BI-P was not successful (no safe
window).// Please perform bx to r/o primary lung CA, metastatic pancreast CA,
or benign process...will affect surgical planning  **URGENT**

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___ and reference CT ___.

PROCEDURE:  CT-guided lung biopsy.

OPERATORS:  Dr. ___ fellow and Dr. ___ radiologist.
Dr. ___ supervised the trainee during the key components of the
procedure and reviewed and agrees with the trainee's findings.

TECHNIQUE:  The risks, benefits, and alternatives of the procedure were
explained to the patient.  After a detailed discussion, informed written
consent was obtained.  A pre-procedure timeout using three patient identifiers
was performed per ___ protocol.

The patient was placed in a supine position on the CT scan table.  Limited
preprocedure CTscan of the intended biopsy area was performed.  Based on the
CT findings an appropriate position for the biopsy was chosen.  The site was
marked.

The site was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.  1% lidocaine
were administered to the subcutaneous and deep tissues for local anesthetic
effect.  Under CT guidance, a 17 gauge coaxial needle was introduced into the
lesion.  An 18 gauge core biopsy device with a 11 mm throw was used to obtain
5 core biopsy specimens, which were sent for pathology.

A Biosentury device was used at the end of the procedure, this may appear as a
radiopacity on subsequent studies with no FDG activity.

The procedure was tolerated well and there were no immediate post-procedural
complications.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 298 mGy-cm.

SEDATION:  Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of 2
mg Versed and 100 mcg fentanyl throughout the total intra-service time of 40
minutes during which patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously
monitored by an independent trained radiology nurse.

FINDINGS:


1. Minimal intraparenchymal hemorrhage around the right upper lobe nodule was
noted which is expected.
2. There is a tiny amount of air that may be extra pleural as it is localized
to the area of needle pleural puncture, which does not progress on subsequent
images.
3. A Biosentury device was used at the pleural punctured site, this may appear
as a radiopacity on subsequent studies with no FDG activity.
4. The spiculated nodule in the right upper lobe is decreased in size since
___ when it had an oval nearly 2 cm lesion.

IMPRESSION: 

Uneventful CT-guided biopsy of a right upper lobe nodule as above.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with right hilar opacity seen on CXR and prior
CABG (___) ; evaluate for right hilar mass.

TECHNIQUE:  Contrast-enhanced chest CT was performed acquiring sequential
axial images from the thoracic inlet through the adrenal glands. Thin section
axial, coronal, sagittal and axial MIP's were also obtained. 75 cc of
Omnipaque 350 were administered intravenously without reported complication.

DOSE:  Total DLP = 328mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CT available for comparison. Correlation made to
plain chest radiograph dated ___, and abdominal ultrasound dated
___.

FINDINGS: 

The patient is status post median sternotomy with CABG. Left anterior
descending coronary artery stents are in place. Dense coronary artery
calcifications are also present. There is no pericardial effusion. A left
pectoral pacemaker has leads extending into the right atrium and right
ventricle. The main pulmonary artery is normal caliber with no incidental
pulmonary embolism identified. However, there is nonspecific dilatation of the
bilateral pulmonary arteries to 2.9 cm. The thoracic aorta is normal caliber,
but contains mild to moderate diffuse calcific atherosclerosis. A focal
outpouching at the anterior aspect of the ascending aorta may be related to
previous bypass cannulation, or may denote the origin of the bypass graft (3,
20).

In the right cardiophrenic space, lateral to the ascending aorta is a
non-enhancing heterogeneously dense ___ ranging from ___ lesion with
irregular borders measuring approximately 5.1 x 3.2 cm (3, 23). This lesion is
inseparable from the pericardium superiorly. The nearby right internal mammary
vessels are widely patent.

The thyroid gland is unremarkable. There are no pathologically enlarged
supraclavicular, internal mammary, hilar or axillary lymph nodes. A
nonspecific mildly enlarged subcarinal lymph node measures 1.3 x 2.2 cm (3,
29).

Multifocal patchy ground-glass opacities, mild bronchial wall thickening, and
increased artery-to-bronchial ratios are most likely due to pulmonary edema. A
small right nonhemorrhagic layering pleural effusion results in partial right
lower lobe passive atelectasis. There is a punctate 2 mm subpleural right
upper lobe nodule (5, 150). A 2 mm right middle lobe perifissural nodule may
represent a benign lymphoid aggregate (5, 170). Punctate calcified granulomas
are incidentally noted (5: 59 and 239).

Images of the upper abdomen show a 20 x 29 mm caudate lobe cyst and an
indeterminate subcentimeter peripheral hypodense lesion in the posterior right
hepatic lobe (3, 55). There is also a small hiatal hernia and an indeterminate
exophytic isodense 5 mm left renal upper pole cyst (5, 301).

IMPRESSION: 

Non-enhancing heterogeneously dense right cardiophrenic space lesion measuring
5.1 x 3.2 cm which is inseparable from the pericardium superiorly, and
corresponds to the abnormality seen on recent chest x-ray.  The appearance is
suggestive of a mediastinal hematoma.  Underlying lesion that bled would be a
possibility although less likely. Contrast enhanced MRI would be helpful to
exclude neoplasia, if there are no MRI contraindications given the pacemaker
in situ . Otherwise, a 4 week followup CT scan may be performed to assess for
interval change.

Mild CHF with pulmonary edema and a small right pleural effusion.

Nonspecific mildly enlarged subcarinal lymph node may be reactive in nature.

Indeterminate exophytic isodense 5 mm left renal upper pole cyst may
correspond to one of the simple cysts identified on prior abdominal
ultrasound.  However, given the higher attenuation of this cyst, a repeat
renal ultrasound is suggested to assess for interval change.

NOTIFICATION:  The intrathoracic findings were discussed by Dr. ___ with Dr.
___ on the telephone on ___ at 6:18 ___, 20 minutes after discovery of
the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST ABDOMEN AND PELVIS

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with abd pain and hypoxia hx of chrons.  Rule
out pulmonary embolism and evaluate for acute intra-abdominal process.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast in the arterial
phase.  Then, imaging was obtained through the abdomen and pelvis in the
portal venous phase. Reformatted coronal and sagittal images through the
chest, abdomen, and pelvis, and oblique maximal intensity projection images of
the chest were submitted to PACS and reviewed.  Volumen oral contrast was
administered.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 974 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. 
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within
normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Bilateral diaphragmatic eventration is, right greater than left
are associated with areas of atelectasis in the lower lungs.  A 5 mm left
fissural nodule and 4 mm right middle lobe nodule are noted (02:49 and 02:44
respectively).  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic
or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation. There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is an 8.6 x 6.2 cm cyst in the left inferior pole that measures 8.6 x
6.2 cm and is simple fluid density, although has multiple septations (2:131).
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis. There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The colon is
mildly dilated and fluid-filled reflecting Volumen prep.  Thickening and
hyperemia of the colon extends from the proximal transverse colon through the
descending colon.  The sigmoid colon appears relatively normal though slightly
dilated up to 6.8 cm in diameter.  Overall appearance is most suggestive of
acute on chronic Crohn's flare.  No definite bowel obstruction.  There is no
free intraperitoneal fluid or free air.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The uterus contains multiple hypodense rounded structures
and calcification, most likely fibroids.  The adnexae are unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Mild atherosclerotic disease
is noted.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine are noted. 
There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture.  There is
a small fat containing umbilical hernia.

IMPRESSION:


1. No pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Acute on chronic Crohn's flare with thickened hyperemic transverse and
descending colon.  No definite bowel obstruction.
3. Large left upper pole renal cyst with septations may be further assessed
with non-emergent renal ultrasound.
4. Fibroid uterus.
5. Two lung nodules measuring up to 5 mm along the left fissure.

RECOMMENDATION(S):   For incidentally detected multiple solid pulmonary
nodules smaller than 6mm, no CT follow-up is recommended in a low-risk
patient, and an optional CT follow-up in 12 months is recommended in a
high-risk patient.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Chest CT

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with pancreatic head mass suspicious for
malignancy, bx pending  // Please complete for oncologic staging

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained from thoracic inlet to upper
abdomen.  Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with coronal and sagittal
reformats

COMPARISON:  ___ (CT abdomen)

FINDINGS: 

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in diameter.  No mediastinal, hilar or
axillary lymphadenopathy is present.  Image portion of the upper abdomen will
be reviewed separately as part of the CT abdomen corresponding report will be
issued.

Heart size is normal.  No pericardial pleural effusion is seen.

Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.  Lungs are clear
except for left apical subpleural 2 mm nodule and minimal left basal
atelectasis.

There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.  Pulmonary nodule in the left
apex, most likely benign.  Can be reassessed in ___ months for documentation
of stability.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with borderline resectable pancreatic cancer
s/p 2 cycles of FOLFIRINOX  // Restaging exam.  Please r/o metastatic disease
in the chest

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS COORDINATED WITH
INTRAVENOUS INFUSION OF NONIONIC CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5
AND 1.25 MM THICK AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP
AXIAL IMAGES. SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS AND THE TOTAL
DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP reported separatelymGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

2 MM DIAMETER LEFT APICAL LUNG NODULE IS UNCHANGED (50, 5).  NO NEW OR GROWING
NODULES ARE DETECTED.

NO ENLARGED INTRATHORACIC LYMPH NODES ARE PRESENT.  HEART SIZE IS NORMAL, AND
NO PERICARDIAL OR PLEURAL EFFUSION IS OBSERVED.  ASCENDING AORTA APPEARS
ECTATIC, WITHOUT CHANGE.

SKELETAL STRUCTURES OF THE THORAX DEMONSTRATE NO NEW SUSPICIOUS LYTIC OR
BLASTIC LESIONS.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No change in 2 mm left apical lung nodule, which is unlikely to represent
a focus of metastatic disease.  This may be reassessed at future surveillance
CT scans to ensure continued stability.

2. Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for complete
description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p radical pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal
vein resection, resection of replaced R hepatic artery.  Restaging.  //
Evaluate for mets.

TECHNIQUE:   Multidetector scanning of the chest was performed and
reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal
and sagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.  IV Contrast administered.
DOSAGE:   Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.1 s, 34.6 cm; CTDIvol = 7.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 249.5
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 10.8 s, 70.3 cm; CTDIvol = 8.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 604.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 854 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA
PANCREAS (ABDOMEN AND PELVIS))

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___.

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid is unremarkable.  A
right chest port terminates in the right atrium.  There is no axillary or
supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see CT abdomen from the same date.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There is no pericardial effusion.  There are no
significant coronary calcifications.
PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  A 2 mm pulmonary nodule at the left lung apex (series 5, image
48) unchanged.  There are no new or enlarged pulmonary nodules.
-AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent the subsegmental level.
-VESSELS:  There is mild prominence of the ascending aorta measuring up to 41
mm.  The main pulmonary artery is mildly enlarged.
CHEST CAGE: The superficial soft tissues are unremarkable.  There is no
evidence of osseous infection or malignancy.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No evidence of malignancy within the chest.  Please see CT abdomen and
pelvis from the same date for evaluation of the abdomen.
2.  Mild enlargement of the main pulmonary artery concerning for pulmonary
hypertension.
3.  Stable, minimal ectasia of the ascending aorta.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p radical pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal
vein resection, resection of replaced R hepatic artery.  Restaging.// Evaluate
for metastatic disease and/or recurrence

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
contiguous 5 mm and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and
parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.1 s, 33.4 cm; CTDIvol = 10.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 338.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 10.9 s, 70.6 cm; CTDIvol = 11.2 mGy (Body) DLP =
783.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,123 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  Multiple chest CTs dated back to ___, with the most
recent from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE:  The visualized thyroid is normal. 
Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.

MEDIASTINUM:  Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HILA:  Hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HEART:  The heart is mildly enlarged and there is no coronary arterial
calcification.  There is no pericardial effusion.

VESSELS:  Vascular configuration is conventional.  The ascending thoracic
aorta is similarly dilated measuring 4.2 x 4.2 cm.  Mild dilatation of the
main pulmonary artery up to 3.3 cm is also unchanged.  The right Port-A-Cath
terminates in the cavoatrial junction. Focal flap in the right subclavian
artery (series 4, image 17), not seen previously, may represent focal
dissection versus septated thrombus.  Flow is seen beyond the abnormality.

PULMONARY PARENCHYMA:  4 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule (series 5, image
85) is unchanged compared to ___.  A 3 mm right upper lobe
subpleural pulmonary nodule (series 5, image 154) is stable compared ___.  4 mm posterior right lower lobe nodule (series 5, image 176) is stable
compared ___.  These nodules have increased in size compared to ___.  No new or additional growing nodules.  No focal consolidation to
suggest pneumonia.  There is no emphysema.

AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.

PLEURA:  There is no pleural effusion.

CHEST WALL AND BONES:  There is no worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesion. 
Multilevel degenerative changes are mild.  No acute fractures.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  Please see separately submitted Abdomen and Pelvis CT report
for subdiaphragmatic findings.

IMPRESSION:


1. 3 pulmonary nodules in the right upper and lower and left upper lobes,
stable compared to ___, have increased in size compared to ___, concerning for metastatic disease.
2. Focal flap in the right subclavian artery, possibly focal chronic
dissection versus septated thrombus vs artifact from the sharp angle of the
vessel (4;17).  Flow is seen beyond the abnormality.  Recommend attention on
follow up studies.
3. Similar mild dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta and main pulmonary
artery.
4. Please see separate report performed on the same day for detailed
evaluation of the abdomen and pelvis.

NOTIFICATION:  The impression and recommendation above was entered by Dr.
___ on ___ at 09:58 into the Department of Radiology critical
communications system for direct communication to the referring provider.

stable compared to ___, have increased in size compared to ___, concerning for metastatic disease.

dissection versus septated thrombus vs artifact from the sharp angle of the
vessel (4;17).  Flow is seen beyond the abnormality.  Recommend attention on
follow up studies.

artery.
4. Please see separate report performed on the same day for detailed
evaluation of the abdomen and pelvis.

communications system for direct communication to the referring provider.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p radical pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal
vein resection, resection of replaced R hepatic artery.  Restaging.// Evaluate
for metastatic disease and/or recurrence

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector axial images were obtained through the chest after
the uneventful administration of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast as part of
a CT torso.  Multiplanar reformats including coronal, sagittal, axial thin
slice and axial maximum intensity projection images were produced and reviewed
on PACs.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 1,169 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CTs from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The imaged thyroid gland is
unremarkable.  Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the separately dictated CT abdomen and pelvis report
performed concurrently for description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HILA: Hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart size is normal.  No pericardial effusion. 
Coronary artery calcifications are mild.  The thoracic aorta is top normal in
caliber with mild calcified atherosclerotic plaque.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  Biapical pleural scarring is
noted.
LUNG:

PARENCHYMA:  The lungs are clear without focal consolidation.

Several pulmonary nodules are again seen, as follows:

3 mm left upper lobe nodule may be new from prior (7:70).

5 mm left upper lobe nodule is essentially unchanged from ___, but
slightly larger from ___.

2 adjacent left lower lobe nodules measuring about 4 mm are grossly unchanged
(7:168).

4 mm of left lower lobe nodule is essentially unchanged (7:66).

4 mm left lower lobe nodule is minimally increased in size, previously
measuring 3 mm (7:202).

3 mm right upper lobe nodule is unchanged (07:43).

5 mm right upper lobe nodule is slightly increased from prior, when it
measured 4 mm (7:75).

5 mm right lower lobe nodule is slightly increased, previously measuring 4 mm
(7:69).

Sub-3 mm right lower lobe nodule is unchanged (7:72).

AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.

VESSELS:  The main, right and left pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. 
While this study is not optimized for the detection of pulmonary emboli, no
central filling defect is seen.

CHEST CAGE: No acute fracture or suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion
is detected.  Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION:


1. Numerous bilateral pulmonary nodules are marginally increased in size in
comparison with ___.  A 3 month follow-up chest CT is recommended
to evaluate for interval increase in size, as the size difference on the
current study is not enough to establish disease progression.  No
lymphadenopathy.
2. Please see the separately dictated CT abdomen and pelvis report from the
same date for description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  3 month follow-up chest CT.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p radical pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal
vein resection, resection of replaced R hepatic artery.  Restaging.// Evaluate
for metastatic disease and/or recurrence

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.6 s, 34.7 cm; CTDIvol = 12.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 418.0
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 13.3 s, 70.5 cm; CTDIvol = 12.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
905.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,324 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA
PANCREAS (ABDOMEN AND PELVIS))

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: There is no supraclavicular,
infraclavicular or any other axillary lymphadenopathy.  Imaged thyroid with no
incidental findings.

CHEST CAGE: There are minimal multilevel degenerative changes but no evidence
of lytic or sclerotic osteo destructive metastasis to the sternum, ribs or
vertebra.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for
complete description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: Scattered lymph nodes in the mediastinum are stable and not
pathologically enlarged, measuring up to 0.7 cm in the right lower
paratracheal station.
Posterior mediastinum, pericaval and anterior supradiaphragmatic stations with
no lymphadenopathy.
There is no lymphadenopathy in the hila.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart is normal in size scattered calcifications of
aortic valve annulus.  Main pulmonary artery is top normal in caliber.  There
are no pericardial abnormalities.

PLEURA: There are no pleural space abnormalities.

LUNG: Right lower lobe subpleural irregular opacity is slightly larger since
prior, new since ___ currently 1.3 cm, in prior 0.9 cm (5:139). 
Findings are nonspecific and could represent worse infection/inflammation,
although metastasis is not excluded, for further follow-up.

Remaining nodules as follow, in the left lung-
0.8 cm nodule in the left upper lobe (5:81), 0.5 cm on prior, was less
conspicuous ___.
Left lower lobe anterior perifissural group of nodules are more conspicuous in
comparison to prior studies, measuring up to 0.45 cm (5:72).
Posterior 0.4 cm subpleural nodule in the left lower lobe is unchanged
(5:161).
0.5 cm nodule in the medial segment of the left lower lobe is essentially
unchanged, slightly larger since ___ (5:203).

In the right lung-
Right upper lobe subpleural 0.7 cm nodule was 0.5 cm in prior, 0.4 on ___ (5:70).
In the right lower lobe subpleural 0.7 cm was in prior 0.5, on ___
0.4 cm (5:162).

IMPRESSION:


-Slight additional increase in size of multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules
measuring up to 0.8 cm in the left upper lobe, is concerning for progression
of metastasis.
-In the right lower lobe subpleural irregular opacity is larger since prior,
indeterminate and possibly infectious/inflammatory in etiology but concerning
for metastasis as well.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  3 months follow up.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p radical pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal
vein resection, resection of replaced R hepatic artery.  Restaging.// Evaluate
for metastatic disease and/or recurrence

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest, coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.1 s, 32.9 cm; CTDIvol = 3.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 107.6
mGy-cm.
   2) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.8
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 6.7 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 108.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
21.7 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 4.2 s, 27.1 cm; CTDIvol = 16.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 441.5
mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 10.6 s, 69.1 cm; CTDIvol = 16.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,095.3 mGy-cm.
   6) Spiral Acquisition 4.2 s, 27.1 cm; CTDIvol = 16.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 441.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 2,109 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  Compared to chest CT scans since ___ most recently ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST PERIMETER: There are no thyroid findings warranting further imaging.
Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.  Evaluation of the
breasts is reserved for mammography. Elsewhere in the chest wall there are no
soft tissues concerning for malignancy.  Findings below the diaphragm will be
reported separately.

CARDIO-MEDIASTINUM:Esophagus is unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic calcification
is not apparent head and neck or coronary arteries.  Aorta and pulmonary
arteries and cardiac chambers are normal size.

THORACIC LYMPH NODES:
 No lymph nodes in the chest are pathologically enlarged by size criteria.
LUNGS, AIRWAYS, PLEURAE: Nearly a dozen lung nodules have grown generally
slightly since ___, up to the largest, 11 x 17mm, 9: 153, previously,
10 x 13 mm.  This nodule is probably invasive the pleural locally, by there no
pleural effusion or remote pleural nodules.

CHEST CAGE: Although there are no bone lesions in the imaged chest cage
suspicious for malignancy or infection, it should be noted that radionuclide
bone and FDG PET scanning are more sensitive in detecting early osseous
pathology than chest CT scanning.

IMPRESSION: 

Most of the dozen small lung nodules have grown slightly since ___,
including the largest, this 17mm RLL nodule invasing the pleurae; no pleural
effusion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT-guided core biopsy.

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with h/o resected pancreatic cancer (___)
with growing lung nodule RLL concerning for metastatic disease// Bx RLL nodule
please. Pt on ___ trial, in addition to pathology please get ___ addition
cores  placed in media provided. Please page ___ ___ for
pick up.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.

PROCEDURE:  CT-guided lung biopsy.

OPERATORS:  Dr. ___, radiology fellow and Dr. ___,
attending radiologist. Dr. ___ supervised the trainee during the
key components of the procedure and reviewed and agrees with the trainee's
findings.

TECHNIQUE:  The risks, benefits, and alternatives of the procedure were
explained to the patient.  After a detailed discussion, informed written
consent was obtained.  A pre-procedure timeout using three patient identifiers
was performed per ___ protocol.

The patient was placed in oblique right side down on the CT scan table. 
Limited preprocedure CTscan of the intended chest area was performed.  Based
on the CT findings an appropriate position for the right lower lobe pulmonary
nodule was chosen.  The site was marked.

The site was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion. 1% lidocaine
were administered to the subcutaneous and deep tissues for local anesthetic
effect. Under CT guidance, a 17 gauge coaxial needle was introduced into the
lesion.  An 18 gauge core biopsy device with a 22 mm throw was used to obtain
five core biopsy specimens, which were sent for pathology.

A small right pneumothorax is noted during the procedure.  This was aspirated
using ___ Needle.  The procedure was tolerated well.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 530 mGy-cm.

SEDATION:  Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of
1.5 mg Versed and 150 mcg fentanyl throughout the total intra-service time of
73 minutes during which patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously
monitored by an independent trained radiology nurse.

FINDINGS: 

Pre-procedure CT demonstrated a 1.7 x 1.0 cm right lower lobe pulmonary nodule
adjacent to the pleura.  This nodule was selected for biopsy. During the
procedure CT fluoroscopy images demonstrated a small right pneumothorax.  This
was aspirated by ___ needle with no residual pneumothorax seen on
postprocedure CT fluoroscopy images.  2 subsequent chest x-rays in 1 and 3
hours were obtained, showed no evidence of pneumothorax.

IMPRESSION: 

Successful CT-guided core-biopsy of right lower lobe pulmonary nodule.

effect. Under CT guidance, a 17 gauge coaxial needle was introduced into the
lesion.  An 18 gauge core biopsy device with a 22 mm throw was used to obtain
five core biopsy specimens, which were sent for pathology.

A small right pneumothorax is noted during the procedure.  This was aspirated
using ___ Needle.  The procedure was tolerated well.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 530 mGy-cm.

SEDATION:  Moderate sedation was provided by administering divided doses of
1.5 mg Versed and 150 mcg fentanyl throughout the total intra-service time of
73 minutes during which patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously
monitored by an independent trained radiology nurse.

Pre-procedure CT demonstrated a 1.7 x 1.0 cm right lower lobe pulmonary nodule
adjacent to the pleura.  This nodule was selected for biopsy. During the
procedure CT fluoroscopy images demonstrated a small right pneumothorax.  This
was aspirated by ___ needle with no residual pneumothorax seen on
postprocedure CT fluoroscopy images.  2 subsequent chest x-rays in 1 and 3
hours were obtained, showed no evidence of pneumothorax.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of PDAC s/p radical
pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal vein resection, resection of replaced R
hepatic artery, now biopsy proven lung met, on treatment.// please evaluate
disease/response to therapy on clinical trial.

TECHNIQUE:  Multiplanar computed tomography imaging was performed through the
chest after uneventful administration of intravenous contrast. 8 x 8
millimeter maximal intensity projection images (MIPs) as well as sagittal and
coronal reformats were produced.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 24.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.0 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.8 s, 69.6 cm; CTDIvol = 18.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,305.6 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,318 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior comparisons, most recent CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: No thyroid lesions identified which
would warrant further imaging.  There is no supraclavicular or axillary
lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please refer to same-day abdomen/pelvis CT report for
subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: The esophagus is unremarkable.  Borderline lymph node in the
right lower paratracheal station is unchanged compared to prior (series 3;
image 78).

HILA: No hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart is normal in size.  There is no pericardial
effusion.
PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Several scattered solid nodules are seen throughout both
lungs.  Largest nodule in the right lower lobe measures 14 x 8 mm (series 3;
image 226), previously 16 x 14 mm.  Multiple additional nodules are also
smaller compared to prior (for example series 3; images 127, 138, 106, 105,
92, 54, 56, 70, 101, 102, and 140).  Multiple additional nodules are stable in
size.  There is no focal consolidation or ground-glass opacity to suggest
acute infection/inflammation.
2. AIRWAYS: Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  Pulmonary vasculature is persistently enlarged, with main
pulmonary artery continues to measure 3.3 cm.
CHEST CAGE: No acute fracture.  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.

IMPRESSION: 

Interval decrease in size of multiple small pulmonary nodules.  Largest
pulmonary nodule measures 14 x 8 mm, previously 16 x 14 mm.  No new pulmonary
nodules identified.

   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.9

61.2 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 11.0 s, 71.4 cm; CTDIvol = 13.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
969.7 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 34.9 cm; CTDIvol = 14.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 487.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,520 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  To a prior study done on ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: Thyroid is unremarkable.  There are no enlarged
supraclavicular lymph nodes.  Right-sided Port-A-Cath tip projects to the
right atrium

BREAST AND AXILLA  : No enlarged axillary lymph nodes.

MEDIASTINUM: There is stable small mediastinal lymph nodes.  The right
paratracheal node measures 6 mm and is unchanged.  Heart size is normal. 
There is no pericardial effusion.


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion

LUNG: Multiple bilateral pulmonary metastasis are again seen.  The 8 mm left
upper lobe pulmonary nodule is unchanged (6, 71).  The right lower lobe
pulmonary nodule is also unchanged (6, 158).  No new pulmonary nodules.

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones is unremarkable.

lesions.  Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has been dictated
separately

IMPRESSION: 

Stable small mediastinal lymph nodes.

Stable multiple pulmonary nodules, consistent with known metastasis, the
largest in the right lower lobe.  No new pulmonary nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of PDAC s/p radical
pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal vein resection, resection of replaced R
hepatic artery, now biopsy proven lung met, on treatment.// please evaluate
disease/response to therapy on clinical trial.

TECHNIQUE:  CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP: Given in abdominal CT report.

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

Right pectoral Port-A-Cath.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or axillary
lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at the level
of the hilar structures.  Normal sized mediastinal lymph nodes are visualized
(5, 16 and 22).  No evidence of pulmonary embolism.  Stable mild coronary
calcifications.  No pericardial effusion.  The posterior mediastinum is
unremarkable.  Upper abdominal morphology is described in detail in the
dedicated abdominal CT report.  No osteolytic lesions at the level of the
ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral bodies.  The known pulmonary metastasis
are in part stable, for example in the left upper lobe (6, 83).  Other
metastasis, notably in the right lower lobe (6, 155), have slightly increased
in size.  There is no evidence of new pulmonary nodules.  No pleural
effusions.  The airways are patent.  No fibrotic lung disease.

IMPRESSION: 

Some of the pre-existing pulmonary metastases are stable in size and
morphology, others, notably in the right lower lobe, are slightly increased in
size.  No new metastases.  No pleural effusions.  No fibrotic lung disease. 
The airways are patent.

** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: Thyroid is unremarkable.  There is a right-sided Port-A-Cath
with its tip in the cavoatrial junction.  There are no enlarged
supraclavicular lymph nodes

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes

MEDIASTINUM: The mediastinal lymph nodes are unchanged.  Right paratracheal
node measures 4 mm.  A right lower paratracheal lymph node measures 10 mm. 
Heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The aorta and
pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.  The airways are patent up to the
subsegmental level

PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.

LUNG: There are multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules concerning for metastasis
the left upper lobe pulmonary nodule now measures 16 mm and is unchanged.  The
right upper lobe pulmonary nodule has slightly increased in size since the
prior study measuring 10 mm as compared to the prior measurements of 7 mm (6,
70).  A right lower lobe pulmonary nodule measuring 21 by 26 mm has slightly
increased in size it previously measured 18 x 25 mm.  Similarly all the other
previously visualized pulmonary nodules have slightly increased in size.

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones shows no lytic or sclerotic lesions
concerning for metastasis.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen shows fatty
infiltration of the liver.  No adrenal masses.  Please refer to dedicated
report on abdomen which has been dictated separately.

IMPRESSION: 

Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules concerning for metastasis.  These have
slightly increased in size since the prior study.

Fatty liver.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of PDAC s/p radical
pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal vein resection, resection of replaced R
hepatic artery, now biopsy proven lung met, on treatment.  // please evaluate
disease/response to therapy on clinical trial.

TECHNIQUE:  ___ MD CT images were obtained through the chest after the
administration of IV contrast.  Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and
sagittal

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 10.9 s, 70.6 cm; CTDIvol = 12.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
860.5 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 861 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal.  There is no axillary, supraclavicular, or hilar
lymphadenopathy.  The esophagus is normal without evidence of wall thickening
or a hiatal hernia.  The aorta is normal in caliber.  The main pulmonary
artery is normal in caliber.  The heart size is normal.  There is no
pericardial effusion.

For evaluation of the subdiaphragmatic structures, please refer to the
dedicated CT of the abdomen performed on the same day.

Interval bilateral pulmonary metastases are redemonstrated.  There appears to
be mixed behavior of these lesions compared to the prior exam.  For example;
-a right upper lobe nodule measures 6 mm (5; 74), decreased in size compared
to the prior exam at which time this measured up to 10 mm.
-a left upper lobe mass measures 8 mm, decreased in size compared to the prior
exam which time this measured up to 16 mm (5; 84).
-a right upper lobe lesion (5; 125) appears slightly increased in size
compared to the prior exam, now measuring 6 mm, previously measuring no more
than 4 mm.
-a dominant subpleural right lower lobe mass now measures 22 mm x 24 mm (5;
142), not significantly changed compared to the prior exam given differences
in acquisition technique.  This lesion however does demonstrate slight
interval increase in tethering of the right perihilar vessels.
-a right lower lobe subpleural nodule (5; 164) measures 7 mm, not

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of PDAC s/p radical
pancreaticoduodenectomy with portal vein resection, resection of replaced R
hepatic artery, now biopsy proven lung met, on treatment.  // please evaluate
disease/response to therapy on clinical trial.

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 822 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Chest CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
Partially imaged thyroid gland is unremarkable.  Stable small axillary and
thoracic inlet lymph nodes.  There are no chest wall abnormalities.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
Esophagus is unremarkable.  Stable small mediastinal lymph nodes ranging up to
9 mm in the right lower paratracheal station (5:88).  New 18 mm right hilar
enlarged lymph node (5:147).

HEART, PERICARDIUM AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, cardiac valves or
aorta.  Aorta is top normal measuring 38 mm.  Pulmonary artery is top normal
measuring 30 mm.  Right Port-A-Cath terminates in the lower SVC.

LUNGS, AIRWAYS, AND PLEURA:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  Interval development of a
moderate dependent pleural effusion with moderate high attenuation which could
represent an exudate thus metastatic pleural involvement.  The associated
compressive atelectasis obscures the previously described 22 x 24 mm right
lower lobe nodule (5:150), however, it appears to be larger within the
limitations, measuring today approximately 21 x 32 mm (4:50).  Remaining
metastatic nodules in the right (5:68, 77, 94, 126) and left lung ((5: 66, 87)
are stable.

CHEST CAGE:
No acute fractures.  Minimal dorsal spondylosis.  No bone lesions worrisome
for malignancy.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
Please see separately submitted Abdomen and Pelvis CT report for
subdiaphragmatic findings.

IMPRESSION: 

Overall disease progression since ___: New moderate size
exudative right pleural effusion, growth of the right lower lobe mass
partially obscured in the collapsed right lower lobe--approximately 21 x 32
mm, previously 22 x 24 mm, and newly enlarged 18mm right hilar node.

Unchanged size and morphology of the remaining right and left lung metastatic
nodules.

Right Port-A-Cath terminates in the lower SVC.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Right lower lobe pulmonary nodule incidentally identified on
thoracic spine CT.

TECHNIQUE:  CT images were obtained through the chest without intravenous
contrast.  Multiplanar reformations were prepared.

COMPARISON:  CT thoracic spine ___.

FINDINGS: The thyroid gland, aorta and major branches, heart and pericardium
are unremarkable with mild aortic and coronary vascular calcifications.  There
is no pericardial effusion.  Small axial hiatal hernia is seen.  The esophagus
is otherwise normal.  There is no mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymph node
enlargement.  Although this study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic
evaluation, the imaged upper abdomen demonstrates a surgically absent
gallbladder and possibly dropped clip anterior to the liver (2:56).  Small
left-sided Bochdalek type hernia is noted.

The trachea and central airways appear patent to the segmental level.  The
previously described subpleural right lower lobe juxtacardiac lobulated nodule
measures 22 x 14 mm, mildly increased from the previous examination. The
nodule is low in attenuation ___ ___. Three and 6 mm right pulmonary nodules
are noted (4:  55 and 162, respectively) along with a 7mm left lower lobe
superior segment nodule (4:129). Bilateral lower lobe predominant linear
opacities with linear calcifications are noted and of uncertain significance. 


OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  Postsurgical changes are seen in the spine with mild mid
thoracic spine degenerative changes. Thoracolumbar fusion hardware is
incompletely imaged. 

IMPRESSION: Interval increase in size of 22 mm right lower lobe subpleural
juxtacardiac pulmonary nodule with additional nodules measuring up to 7 mm. 
While interval increase in size is concerning, low attenuation nodules are
more commonly benign. Given this discrepancy, consideration could be given to
PET/CT or MRI evaluation of the dominant nodule, though MRI may be limited due
to proximity to the heart and resultant motion. If PET/CT, MRI or surgical
excision is not performed, 3 month interval followup is recommended.

Findings were entered into the radiology department's online record for
notification of critical results on ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with suspicious lesion at the glans penis
concerning for penile cancer// Metastatic and extent of involvement of penile
cancer.

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest, coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.1 s, 24.1 cm; CTDIvol = 8.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 204.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 24.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.0 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 8.2 s, 64.6 cm; CTDIvol = 10.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 664.1
mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 0.7 s, 5.6 cm; CTDIvol = 10.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 60.2
mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 3.1 s, 24.1 cm; CTDIvol = 8.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 203.2
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,144 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS W AND W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS)

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid has a small 4mm hypodense nodule in the right lobe.  No enlarged
lymph nodes in either axilla or thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest
wall.  No atherosclerotic calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta or cardiac
valves.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
No pleural effusions.  No apical scarring bilaterally.

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  Mild diffuse bronchial
thickening with some areas of mucoid impaction noted in the right lower lobe
(4:80).  No bronchiectasis.  Large single air cyst in the right lower lobe
with thin walls measuring 2.2 cm (4:91).  Triangular-shaped nodule in the
right upper lobe measuring 7 mm (04:41), likely an intraparenchymal lymphoid
aggregate.  Faint ground-glass opacities in the right upper lobe (4:90).  No
suspicious lung nodules or masses.

CHEST CAGE:
Mild dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
Please refer to same day abdominal CT report for subdiaphragmatic findings.

IMPRESSION: 

No definitive evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.
Mild indeterminate ground-glass opacity in the right upper lobe, likely an
incidental inflammatory/infectious process.

cm are unchanged compared to the prior exam.  There is no supraclavicular

T11 vertebral body with a soft tissue component measuring up to 19 mm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST ___

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic penile SCC  // Asses for
metastatic disease

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest, coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.5
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.4 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 74.4 mGy (Body) DLP =
14.9 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 10.3 s, 66.9 cm; CTDIvol = 10.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
724.5 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 4.1 s, 26.9 cm; CTDIvol = 8.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 219.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 961 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CT scans available.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST PERIMETER: No thyroid findings warrant further imaging.  Supraclavicular
and numerous small bilateral axillary lymph nodes are not pathologically
enlarged. No soft tissue abnormalities elsewhere in the chest wall. Findings
below the diaphragm will be reported separately.

CARDIO-MEDIASTINUM: Esophagus is unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic calcification
is not apparent head neck vessels or coronary arteries.  Central venous
infusion catheter ends in the right atrium with no evidence of thrombosis. 
Aorta and pulmonary arteries and cardiac chambers are normal size and the
pericardium is physiologic

THORACIC LYMPH NODES: No lymph nodes in the chest, including diaphragmatic,
posterior mediastinal and retrocrural stations, are pathologically enlarged.

LUNGS, AIRWAYS, PLEURAE: 2 x 4 mm triangular opacity, right lung apex, 6:48,
could be a branching vessel, but if it is a lung nodule the shape is more
typical for lymphoid aggregate than metastasis.

2 cm wide, air-filled cystic space in the right lower lobe, 6:201 is of no
active concern.

Left apical location of 3 mm subpleural nodule,  6:33, suggests it is a
granuloma or scar.

Subsegmental atelectasis left lung base, has a mildly nodular configuration.

Lungs otherwise clear.  Bronchial wall thickening in the lower lobes reflects
mild inflammation.  No bronchiectasis or peribronchial infiltration.



CHEST CAGE: Although there are no bone lesions in the imaged chest cage
suspicious for malignancy or infection, it should be noted that radionuclide
bone and FDG PET scanning are more sensitive in detecting early osseous
pathology than chest CT scanning.

IMPRESSION: 

No good evidence for intrathoracic malignancy.  3 mm solid nodule left lung
apex is indeterminate but more likely a scar than a solitary metastasis. 
Recommendations for such incidental findings provided below.

Benign air-filled cyst, right lower lobe.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  For incidentally detected single solid pulmonary nodule
smaller than 6 mm, no CT follow-up is recommended in a low-risk patient, and
an optional CT in 12 months is recommended in a high-risk patient.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

administration of intravenous contrast.  Axial coronal and sagittal
reconstructions were acquired.  Maximum intensity projections were also
acquired

mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 12.7 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 207.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
41.4 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 10.2 s, 66.4 cm; CTDIvol = 9.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 629.9
mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 4.5 s, 29.4 cm; CTDIvol = 9.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 277.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 951 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  To a prior study done on ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: The thyroid is unremarkable.  There is a right-sided
Port-A-Cath with tip in the SVC.  There are no enlarged supraclavicular lymph
nodes.

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes.
MEDIASTINUM: There are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  Heart size
is normal. There is no pericardial effusion.  The aorta and pulmonary arteries
are normal in caliber.  There is no pericardial effusion.


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.

LUNG: A 3 mm left apical pulmonary nodule (6, 34) is unchanged.  Is stable
biapical pleuroparenchymal scarring.  No new or growing pulmonary nodules. 
Minimal bibasilar atelectasis.  Stable cyst in the right lower lobe.

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones is unremarkable.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen are also
unremarkable

IMPRESSION: 

Stable 3 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule.  No new pulmonary nodules.

Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has been dictated
separately.

atrium.  A small left supraclavicular lymph node measuring 4 mm is unchanged.

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes.

MEDIASTINUM: There are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  There is a
prominent right superior pericardial recess.  Heart size is normal.  There is
no pericardial effusion.


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.

LUNG: There is a stable 7 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule (3, 33),
differences in measurements are most likely related to slice selection.  There
is stable biapical pleuroparenchymal scarring.  The 3 mm left upper lobe
pulmonary nodule is also unchanged (3, 35).  There is mild bronchiectasis
within both lower lobes right greater than left.  An emphysematous bleb in the
right lower lobe is unchanged.  Stable scarring in both lower lobes.  No new
pulmonary nodules.

the the right lateral aspect of T11 vertebral body.  (3, 200) Consistent with
known metastasis.  No new lytic or sclerotic lesions concerning for
metastasis.  There are degenerative changes involving the shoulder joints.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable 3 mm left apical pulmonary nodule.  Stable apical pleuroparenchymal
scarring.

Stable lytic lesion involving T11 vertebral body.  No new lytic or sclerotic
osseous lesions.

separately.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic penile cancer, please assess for
recurrence.  // ___ year old man with metastatic penile cancer, please assess
for recurrence.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the thorax following
administration of IV contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, and MIPS reformats reviewed
in PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 3.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 16.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
8.4 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.2 s, 63.1 cm; CTDIvol = 10.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 641.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 650 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior CT chests, most recently the CT chest dated ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL:  Normal partially imaged thyroid
gland.  Numerous measurable bilateral axillary lymph nodes, not enlarged by
size criteria or growing.  No enlarged or growing supraclavicular lymph nodes.
Right-sided chest port in situ, with the tip in the right atrium.  No soft
tissue chest wall abnormality. No atherosclerotic calcification of the imaged
neck arteries.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the CT abdomen and pelvis report dated the same day
for evaluation of the abdomen and pelvis.

MEDIASTINUM: Normal esophagus.  No enlarged or growing mediastinal lymph
nodes.  No mediastinal mass.  Enlarged main pulmonary artery, measuring 3.3
cm, and an enlarged right pulmonary artery measuring 2.9 cm, both slightly
increased compared to prior.  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  No
atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta.

HILA: No enlarged or growing hilar lymph nodes.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Normal heart size. No coronary artery or cardiac valve
calcification.  Physiologic pericardium.

PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. Mild biapical pleuroparenchymal
scarring, stable.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: 7 mm right upper lobe nodule (3:37), stable.  4 mm left apical
subpleural nodule (3:21), stable.  No new or enlarging lung nodule or mass. 
No consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  The tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level. 
Mild bronchial wall thickening in the lower lobes bilaterally, likely
inflammatory in nature.  No bronchiectasis.
3. VESSELS:  Allowing for the non dedicated nature of the examination, no
filling defects in the pulmonary arteries.

CHEST CAGE: 10 mm lucency in the manubrium superiorly (3:31), stable.  14 mm x
13 mm lytic lesion involving the right side of T11 vertebral body (602:68),
stable.  No new lytic or sclerotic lesion concerning for infection or
malignancy.  No fracture.  Mild spondylosis.

IMPRESSION:


-Stable 7 mm right upper lobe and 4 mm left apical nodules.  No new or
enlarging nodule.
-Stable lytic lesion in the manubrium and in T11 vertebral body.  No new
osseous lesion.
-Mild bronchial wall thickening in the lower lobes bilaterally, likely
inflammatory in nature.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic penile cancer, please assess for
recurrence.  // ___ year old man with metastatic penile cancer, please assess
for recurrence.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and axial maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 10.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
2.1 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 10.4 s, 67.6 cm; CTDIvol = 8.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 553.3
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 557 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Thoracic aorta is normal in size. No pathologic aortic
valvular calcifications. Main pulmonary artery is top-normal in size.
Physiologic pericardium. No substantial coronary artery calcification is
identified. Right chest wall Port-A-Cath terminates in the superior right
atrium.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female with history of a 13-cm adenoma, patient is
status post resection.  Subsequently, patient found to have a new liver mass
concerning for adenoma with small cell dysplasia or angiosarcoma.  Evaluation
for metastatic disease in the chest and pelvis.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multiple MDCT-acquired axial images from the thoracic inlet to the
level of the upper abdomen and from the iliac crest to the pubic symphysis
were displayed with 5-mm slice thickness.  No oral contrast was administered. 
Intravenous contrast was administered, and images were acquired in immediate
post-contrast phase.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were prepared.

CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  No
pathologic lymphadenopathy is appreciated in the supraclavicular, axillary, or
mediastinal regions.  There is a pulmonary nodule in the right lung base that
measures 5.6 mm (2:44) and appears unchanged in size compared to the CT
abdomen and pelvis from ___.  No other new pulmonary nodules are
identified.  There are no pulmonary opacities or pleural effusions.  The heart
and great vessels are unremarkable.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The
tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level.  There is
continuation of the hemiazygos vein draining into the left subclavian.  Images
of the upper abdomen show a large heterogeneous mass within the liver hilum,
similar in appearance to the recent MRI.  The spleen appears normal.  Multiple
sutures are visualized in the left upper quadrant, consistent with history of
gastric bypass surgery.  Please see recent MRI of the abdomen for further
details.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The rectum, sigmoid colon, and bladder are
unremarkable.  There is an intrauterine device within the uterus, otherwise,
the uterus and adnexa are unremarkable.  No pathologic lymphadenopathy
appreciated in the pelvis or inguinal region.  There is no free fluid.

BONE WINDOWS:  The osseous structures are unremarkable.  No bone destructive
lesion is appreciated.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of distant metastatic disease or pathologic lymphadenopathy
within the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.
2.  Stable appearance of a right lower lobe pulmonary nodule measuring 5.6 mm,
stable in size compared to CT abdomen from ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with malignancy, and recent prolonged Y90
procedure due to inadvertent coil deplyment in LHA with tachycardia, new
hypotension, and new I2 requirement  // Please perform CT-PA and CT-abd (abd
to be done in arterial and PV phase). Assess for PE or procedural complication

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector scanning is performed from the thoracic inlet
through the iliac crest during dynamic injection of Omnipaque

DOSE:  608 mGy per cm

COMPARISON:  CT of the abdomen and pelvis from PMI at ___  Medical group

FINDINGS: 

CT of the chest with IV contrast:
There is a 2.7 x 2.3 cm hypodense nodule in the right lobe of the thyroid
gland. There is no axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The
esophagus is dilated and fluid filled concerning for reflux. There is a small
right pleural effusion. Atelectasis is noted in the lower lobes bilaterally.
There are patchy areas of consolidation in the right upper lobe on the series
2, ___ 50 as well as in the lingula The pulmonary arteries are well opacified
and there is no filling defect.

CT of the abdomen with and without IV contrast:
There is a small amount of free fluid surrounding the liver. This is new from
prior study however is not clearly hemorrhagic. There are patchy areas of hypo
enhancement involving segments 2 and 3 commence segments for a segment 5.
These areas are new. The previously seen mass in segment 4B is also noted.
This mass extends to the gallbladder and deforms it straight per similar to
prior study. There are wedge-shaped hypodensities in the spleen that are new
the pancreas is unremarkable. The adrenal glands are normal. There is some fat
stranding surrounding the right kidney there are no renal masses. There is no
hydronephrosis. There is no retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The aorta is
normal in caliber.

On arterial phase imaging imaging there is a dissection flap in the celiac
trunk, best seen on series 2 ___ 113 a call is identified in the left
gastric artery and there is no flow in the left gastric artery or in a
replaced left hepatic artery where also coils are noted on ___ 96, this artery
is supplied the lateral segment of the left lobe of liver. Flow was noted in
the common hepatic artery, the right hepatic artery in the artery to segment
4.

On bone windows there are degenerative changes in the thoracic and lumbar
spine. No concerning osteolytic or osteo sclerotic lesions are seen.

IMPRESSION: 

1. New abnormalities in segments 2, 3, 4 a and 6 as described above are
concerning for hepatic infarcts. New splenic infarcts. Status post coiling of
the left gastric artery and a replaced left hepatic artery with no arterial
flow in these vessels. Focal dissection in the celiac trunk.
2. The known mass in segment 4B is unchanged.
3. New small amount of non hemorrhagic ascites surrounding the liver. No
evidence for hematoma or active extravasation.
4.  No evidence of PE .
5.  Partial collapse of the lower lobes bilaterally. Patchy opacities in the
right upper lobe and lingula raise concern for aspiration particularly in the
setting of a dilated, fluid-filled esophagus which may be due to reflux.
6. 2.7 cm right thyroid nodule. Further evaluation with ultrasound is
recommended.
