radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Tachycardia.

COMPARISON:  CTs available from ___, through ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images of the chest were obtained with the use
of IV contrast.  Sagittal, coronal and left and right oblique reconstructions
were performed, the latter to better assess the pulmonary vasculature.

FINDINGS:  
No pulmonary embolism is detected to the subsegmental levels.  There is no
dissection.  The ascending portion of the thoracic aorta is mildly enlarged,
measuring 36 mm in diameter, unchanged since the prior CT examination from
___.  A moderate degree of atherosclerotic calcification is present
throughout the coronary arteries, mitral and aortic valves, and thoracic
aorta.

A mild degree of centrilobular emphysema is present.  The lungs are well
aerated to the subsegmental levels.  No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy
is detected.  Included views of the liver, spleen, stomach, pancreas and
kidneys are unremarkable.  A fat-density nodule within the left adrenal gland
measuring 3 mm is unchanged from prior examination and compatible with a
myolipoma.

There is a 7-mm hypodense lesion within the right lobe of the thyroid (3:9),
unchanged from prior examinations.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No acute fracture or dislocation.  No blastic or lytic
lesions are detected.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism to the subsegmental levels.  No evidence
of dissection.
2.  Mild emphysema, with no focal consolidations or effusions.
3.  Unchanged mildly dilated ascending aorta (36 mm).
4.  Unchanged 3 mm myolipoma within the left adrenal gland.
5.  Stable 7-mm right thyroid nodule.  Ultrasound study can be obtained if
there is a clinical concern for any thyroid disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman intubated d/t copd exac with left lung that is
severely a leukemic. ?Etiology  // with left lung that is severely a leukemic.
?parenchymal process

TECHNIQUE:   Axial CT images through the chest were obtained WITHOUT
administration of IV contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and thin section reformats
and axial MIP images were generated.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.0 s, 38.9 cm; CTDIvol = 7.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 268.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 269 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest CTA dated ___ and ___

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is unremarkable.  The ETT ends 5.7 cm above the carina. An enteric
tube extends into the stomach.  A right central venous catheter ends in the
mid SVC.  There is stable mild cardiomegaly.  Dense mitral annulus
calcification and calcification of the aortic valve appear progressed from
prior.  Dense coronary artery calcifications are noted.  Note is made of
lipomatous hypertrophy of the intra-atrial septum measuring up to 2.1 cm.  No
pericardial effusion.

Motion artifact decreases sensitivity for detecting small pulmonary nodules
and interstitial abnormalities.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental
level.  There is unilateral septal thickening and reticulation in the right
lung.  There is asymmetric emphysema involving the left upper lobe giving it a
hyperlucent appearance.  Consolidations are seen in the bilateral lung bases. 
There is a small right pleural effusion with associated right lower lobe
atelectasis.

Limited views of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

Compression deformity of L1 is unchanged from at least ___.  Mild
loss of height of T8 is unchanged from ___.  No lytic or sclerotic lesions
suspicious for malignancy is identified.

IMPRESSION:


1. Asymmetric hyperlucency of the left lung compared to the right appears
chronic and likely related to a combination of asymmetric pulmonary edema in
the right lung and asymmetric emphysema in the left upper lobe.
2. Superimposed bibasilar consolidations concerning for aspiration or
aspiration pneumonia.
3. Lipomatous hypertrophy of the intra-atrial septum measuring 2.1 cm is
usually asymptomatic but can be associated with arrhythmias.

CTA CHEST WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

COMPARISON:  ___.

HISTORY:  COPD and shortness of breath.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images of the chest were obtained.  IV
contrast was administered.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were performed.

FINDINGS:  There is no evidence of filling defects within the pulmonary
arteries to suggest pulmonary emboli.  There is mild cardiomegaly, stable.
There is no focal consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax.  Emphysematous
changes are stable.  Tiny pulmonary nodules in the right upper lobe (3, 20)
and right middle lobe (3, 69) are unchanged when compared to prior exam.  The
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is no axillary, hilar, or
mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.
Extensive vascular calcifications are noted. Hypodense nodules in the thyroid
gland are smaller when compared to prior exam. 

The osseous structures are grossly unremarkable.  There are no focal lytic or
sclerotic lesions identified.

Limited images of the upper abdomen demonstrate a possible 3 mm left adrenal
myelolipoma.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of pulmonary emboli.
2.  Emphysematous changes, stable.
3.  Incidental note of 3 mm left adrenal myelolipoma.
4.  Vascular calcifications.

Findings were posted to ED dashboard.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with found to have a 5 mm subapical nodule
bilateral.  On the CTA of the neck on ___// evaluation of the pulmonary
apical nodules

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent  reconstructed as
axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 211 mGy

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal.  There are increasing number of borderline bilateral
axillary lymph nodes.  Most of them have normal fatty hilum.  Supraclavicular,
mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary
arteries are normal size.  There is mild cardiomegaly.  No appreciable
coronary calcification.  Respiratory motion limits the evaluation of the
lungs.
3 mm nodule in the left apex is stable (05:29)
There are other several micro nodules (5:70, 16, 92, 116, 119, 121, 132,, 147,
164)
Conglomerate nodules in the left lower lobe measures 4 mm (5:139)
Right middle lobe nodule measures 5 mm (5:155)
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.
This examination is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation there is
fatty infiltration of the liver.
There are no bone findings of malignancy

IMPRESSION: 

Lung nodules measuring up to 5 mm follow-up ___ months is recommended
Fatty liver
Mild cardiomegaly

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST/ABD/PELVIS W/ CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with unwitnessed fall  // Rule out traumatic
injuries

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 7.8 s, 61.3 cm; CTDIvol = 11.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 728.3
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 728 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without
evidence of acute injury.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are
within normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.  Incidentally noted is
aberrant vascular anatomy noting the right subclavian coming off the aortic
arch and coursing posterior to the esophagus and the right vertebral coming
off the right common carotid.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: There is a small right pleural effusion.  No pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Lungs are clear without masses or areas of parenchymal
opacification.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesion or laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: There is a 1.2 cm left adrenal nodule, incompletely characterized on
this exam.  The right adrenal gland is unremarkable.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: There may be mild circumferential wall thickening involving
the antrum/pylorus of the stomach.  Remainder of the stomach is slightly
distended with fluid contents.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber,
wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The colon and rectum are within
normal limits.  The appendix is normal.  There is no evidence of mesenteric
injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder is decompressed about a Foley catheter.  Distal ureters
are unremarkable.  There is no free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The uterus is grossly unremarkable.  There is a 2.4 cm
left adnexal cyst.  Right adnexa is unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
Extensive atherosclerotic disease is noted.

BONES: There is no acute fracture.  No focal suspicious osseous abnormality. 
Dynamic compression screw is seen in the left proximal femur.  There is an
acute, comminuted intertrochanteric fracture of the right femoral neck with
varus displacement and impaction of the distal fragment.  There is a
compression fracture of T11 with approximately 25% loss of height, minimal
retropulsion of fracture fragments and a sclerotic line within the vertebral
body suggestive of an acute or subacute fracture.  There are age-indeterminate
compression deformities of L1 (75% loss of body height) and L2 (25% loss of
body height).  Chronic deformities of the right clavicle and bilateral sacral
insufficiency fractures also noted.  Sclerosis of the left parasymphyseal
region could reflect remote healed fracture.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.


IMPRESSION:


1. Acute, comminuted intertrochanteric fracture of the right femoral neck with
mild varus   displacement and impaction of the distal fragment.
2. Age-indeterminate, likely acute to subacute, fracture of T11 vertebral body
with 25% loss of vertebral body height and minimal retropulsion of fracture
fragments.
3. Age-indeterminate L1 and L2 compression deformities.
4. Bilateral sacral insufficiency fractures.
5. Small right pleural effusion.
6. 1.2 cm indeterminate left adrenal nodule.  If clinically relevant, consider
recommendations below.
7. Possible mild circumferential wall thickening involving the distal stomach.
Correlate clinically, and if clinically indicated despite the patient's
advanced age, can be further evaluated with endoscopy.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Incidentally discovered adrenal lesion without prior
studies for comparison measuring 1-2 cm. If there is no history of malignancy,
this is probably benign. Follow up dedicated adrenal CT in 12 months could be
considered. If there is a history of malignancy, a dedicated adrenal CT is
recommended.

Recommendations based on ___ ACR guidelines:
___

NOTIFICATION:  These findings were discussed with the ACS team at time of
dictation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  CLL status post allogenic transplant with new adenopathy.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the torso after the uneventful intravenous administration
of Optiray contrast, displayed in 5 mm axial collimation with multiplanar
reformations.

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  There is profound adenopathy in the axilla, which has
markedly enlarged from ___, for example, a 5.5 cm right axillary
conglomerate mass (4:20) previously measured 3.3 cm.  There are innumerable
lymph nodes, all measuring between 1 and 3 cm.  There are pathologic number of
mediastinal lymph nodes as well, although most of them do not meet pathologic
criteria, aside from subcarinal node measuring 26 x 12 mm (4:26).  The number
of nodes in the mediastinum has increased from ___, and is thus
suspicious.  The heart and great vessels of the mediastinum are normal.  There
are few small nonpathologic hilar lymph nodes, the largest measuring 7 mm
(4:24). The appearance of the lungs is unchanged from ___,
notable for scarring and nodular opacities in both lung apices with
emphysematous changes. Small mucous plug is again noted in the right middle
lobe and lower lobe.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  The liver, gallbladder, pancreas, stomach, adrenal
glands are normal.

There are multiple vague sub-5 mm hypodense nodular areas predominantly in the
right kidney that are not as well seen on ___.  The new lesions
in the kidneys are more indeterminate and given the overall appearance of
disease, but could potentially represent a renal involvement of disease.
Additional tiny hypodense lesions in the left kidney are unchanged and likely
epresent benign cysts.

Profound adenopathy is also present within the abdomen in all nodal stations
including the porta hepatis and retroperitoneum.  The largest conglomerate
mass measures 6.4 x 3.7 cm (4:70), though there are multiple lymph nodes
measuring above 2 cm in short axis diameter.  The spleen is enlarged measuring
12.3 cm (relatively unchanged in size from ___.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The rectum, sigmoid, and large bowel is notable for
diverticular disease.  There is trace free fluid in the pelvis.  The uterus,
adnexa, distal ureters, and bladder are normal.

Marked adenopathy is also noted in the pelvis along the iliac nodal stations.
The inguinal lymph nodes are relatively spared, without pathologic
enlargement.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lesions are identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Marked progression of disease, particularly in the axilla, abdomen, and
pelvis.
2.  Possible renal infiltration with lymphoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATIONS:  ___ woman with CLL, for restaging.

COMPARISONS:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial CT images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were obtained
with oral and intravenous contrast, and sagittal and coronal reconstructions
were also performed.

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Persistent adenopathy at the base of the
neck is better evaluated on this dedicated CT of the neck performed on the
same day.  There are multiple similarly enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes.
There is also persistent bilateral supraclavicular and left axillary
lymphadenopathy.  This includes a cluster of left subpectoral lymph nodes
measuring 32 x 20 mm in overall extent, probably unchanged allowing for
rotation due to differences in arm positioning.  A well-defined right axillary
node measures 22 x 14 mm (2:29), compared to 26 x 18 mm previously, perhaps
slightly smaller, while a cluster of enlarged left axillary lymph nodes is
also slightly smaller.

A nodular density of 5 mm in extent along the right major fissure lies in the
region of scarring and atelectasis, and is unchanged since at least
___. Other areas of nodularity and scarring at the apices are also not
significantly changed bilaterally.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:  Bilateral cortical based subcentimeter
hypodensities in the kidneys are too small to characterize but unchanged.  The
liver is unremarkable.  There are again gallstones and/or debris within the
gallbladder.  The pancreas appears normal.  There is no intra- or extra-
hepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  The adrenal glands are within normal
limits. The spleen is again moderately enlarged, measuring 17.4 cm in length.

There is trace residual free fluid but considerably less than before. Although
marked periportal and periaortic lymphadenopathy persists, enlarged nodes are
overall unchanged. Representative is an enlarged portacaval node (2:69) that
measures 32 x 23, compared to 31 x 24 mm previously, little changed, while a
conglomerate of left periaortic nodes at the level of the left kidney measures
up to 73 x 37 mm, little changed allowing for differences in measurement
technique. A distinctly definable right common iliac node measures 38 x 28 mm
compared to 41 x 29 mm, also little changed. Several mildly prominent central
mesenteric lymph nodes are also little changed.  A periportal node lying
immediately posterior to segment IV measures 22 x 12 mm (2:69) compared to 21
x 15 mm before.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  A lower right external iliac lymph node
measures 24 x 18 (2:93) compared to 30 x 19 mm, perhaps slightly smaller.
There is similar bilateral pelvic sidewall lymphadenopathy.  A left pelvic
side wall nodal mass measures 43 x 21 mm (2.108) compared to 49 x 22 mm, which
is unchanged. Bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy is little changed.  The
uterus, adnexal regions, distal ureters, bladder are unremarkable.  There is
sigmoid diverticulosis.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1. Perhaps minimal improvement in some of the well-defined enlarged lymph
nodes, but with very little overall change in widespread lymphadenopathy.

2. However considerable improvement in ascites.

sections.technique:
Axial CT images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were obtained
with oral and intravenous contrast, and sagittal and coronal reconstructions
were also performed.

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Persistent adenopathy at the base of the
neck is better evaluated on this dedicated CT of the neck performed on the
same day.  There are multiple similarly enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes.
There is also persistent bilateral supraclavicular and left axillary
lymphadenopathy.  This includes a cluster of left subpectoral lymph nodes
measuring 32 x 20 mm in overall extent, probably unchanged allowing for
rotation due to differences in arm positioning.  A well-defined right axillary
node measures 22 x 14 mm (2:29), compared to 26 x 18 mm previously, perhaps
slightly smaller, while a cluster of enlarged left axillary lymph nodes is
also slightly smaller.

A nodular density of 5 mm in extent along the right major fissure lies in the
region of scarring and atelectasis, and is unchanged since at least
___. Other areas of nodularity and scarring at the apices are also not
significantly changed bilaterally.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:  Bilateral cortical based subcentimeter
hypodensities in the kidneys are too small to characterize but unchanged.  The
liver is unremarkable.  There are again gallstones and/or debris within the
gallbladder.  The pancreas appears normal.  There is no intra- or extra-
hepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  The adrenal glands are within normal
limits. The spleen is again moderately enlarged, measuring 17.4 cm in length.

There is trace residual free fluid but considerably less than before. Although
marked periportal and periaortic lymphadenopathy persists, enlarged nodes are
overall unchanged. Representative is an enlarged portacaval node (2:69) that
measures 32 x 23, compared to 31 x 24 mm previously, little changed, while a
conglomerate of left periaortic nodes at the level of the left kidney measures
up to 73 x 37 mm, little changed allowing for differences in measurement
technique. A distinctly definable right common iliac node measures 38 x 28 mm
compared to 41 x 29 mm, also little changed. Several mildly prominent central
mesenteric lymph nodes are also little changed.  A periportal node lying
immediately posterior to segment IV measures 22 x 12 mm (2:69) compared to 21
x 15 mm before.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  A lower right external iliac lymph node
measures 24 x 18 (2:93) compared to 30 x 19 mm, perhaps slightly smaller.
There is similar bilateral pelvic sidewall lymphadenopathy.  A left pelvic
side wall nodal mass measures 43 x 21 mm (2.108) compared to 49 x 22 mm, which
is unchanged. Bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy is little changed.  The
uterus, adnexal regions, distal ureters, bladder are unremarkable.  There is
sigmoid diverticulosis.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

1. Perhaps minimal improvement in some of the well-defined enlarged lymph
nodes, but with very little overall change in widespread lymphadenopathy.

2. However considerable improvement in ascites.

radiology_facts.report_text:
TORSO CT WITH CONTRAST, ___

INDICATION:  ___ woman with CLL.  Please restage.

TECHNIQUE:  CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed after the
uneventful administration of intravenous contrast, using 5-mm contiguous axial
slice thickness.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were created for better
anatomic delineation.

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  There has been significant decrease in the size and prominence of
several of the axillary, subpectoral, and lower cervical lymph nodes.  Several
of the mediastinal nodes have also demonstrated interval decrease in size.

For instance, a cluster of subpectoral lymph nodes on the left, previously
measuring 32 x 20 mm, now appears less confluent, now measuring approximately
22 x 10 mm.  A right axillary lymph node previously measuring approximately 26
x 15 mm now measures approximately 18 x 12 mm.

There is stable scarring in the apices as well as a stable left apical nodule.
Nodule along the right major fissure is also unchanged.  There are no new or
suspicious nodules or masses.  Minimal emphysematous changes are noted.  There
is no consolidation.  Central airways are clear.  Heart and great vessels are
unremarkable.

ABDOMEN:  There has been dramatic decrease in size of several of the
conglomerate lymph node masses.  In particular, a left retroperitoneal lymph
node cluster previously measuring approximately 7.3 x 3.8 cm now measures
approximately 4.9 x 2.1 cm.  There has been a similar decrease in size of
several retroperitoneal lymph node clusters as well as lymphadenopathy
extending along the bilateral iliac chains.

Liver, pancreas, and adrenals are unremarkable.  Again seen is heterogeneous
material within the gallbladder, likely representing stones and sludge.  There
is no evidence of acute complication.  The spleen has demonstrated an interval
decrease in size.  Presently, it measures a maximum of 16 cm in maximal
craniocaudal extent, previously measuring 18 cm.  Kidneys are unchanged in
appearance, again noting scattered hypodensities which are too small to
characterize.  There is no hydronephrosis.

Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber throughout.  Bowel loops are all normal
in caliber, without evidence for obstruction.

PELVIS:  Uterus and adnexal regions are unchanged in appearance.  There is
sigmoid and descending colonic diverticulosis without evidence of
diverticulitis.  There is no significant free fluid in the pelvis.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  Multilevel degenerative changes are again noted.  As
before, there is a serpentine lucency with associated regions of sclerosis and
subchondral cortical depression, consistent with avascular necrosis.

IMPRESSION:  Substantial decrease in size of lymphadenopathy within the chest,
abdomen, and pelvis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman diagnosed with CLL in ___, post multiple
cycles of chemotherapy and bone marrow transplant on ___.

COMPARISON:  Multiple exams, the most recent dated ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired images were obtained through the chest, abdomen, and
pelvis after the uneventful administration of oral and 100 cc of IV Optiray
contrast.  Multiplanar reformats were reviewed.

CT CHEST:  Axillary, subpectoral, and lower cervical lymph nodes have slightly
decreased in size since ___.  Right axillary lymph node measuring 16 x 11
mm measured 18 x 12 mm on ___ (2:29).  The left subpectoral lymph node
measuring 16 x 9 mm, measured 22 x 10 mm on ___. Heart size is normal.
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion. 5 mm nodule along the right major
lobe fissure (2:47) and subcentimeter left upper lobe nodules are unchanged
since ___. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. Scattered mediastinal
nodes do not meet CT size criteria for enlargement.

CT ABDOMEN:  The liver is unremarkable without focal lesion or duct dilation.
The spleen, adrenals and pancreas are unremarkable. Gallstones and/or debris
in the gallbladder are unchanged since ___. There is no evidence of
cholecystitis. Left paraaortic conglomerate measuring 4.4 x 1.7 cm measured
4.8 x 2.1 cm on ___.  A portacaval node measuring 1.9 x 1.4 cm measured
2.2 x 1.6 cm on ___. Multiple prominent mesenteric lymph nodes appear
smaller since ___. Scattered subcentimeter hypodensities in the kidneys
likely representing cysts are unchanged since ___.  The kidneys secrete
contrast and enhance symmetrically.  There is no evidence of hydronephrosis.

CT PELVIS:  The bladder, uterus, and adnexa are unremarkable.  There is no
pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Bone windows demonstrate no lesion that is suspicious for metastasis or
infection.  30% loss of anterior vertebral body height at L1 is new since
___.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Slight improvement adenopathy since ___ as noted above.

2.  Gallbladder stones/sludge are similar to ___.

3.  No new pulmonary since ___.

4.  New L1 anterior compression fracture since ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with CLL.  Please restage.

COMPARISON:  Multiple CT torsos, most recent of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained from the lung apices to
the pubic symphysis after the administration of Optiray intravenous contrast
and oral contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also
displayed.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Two pulmonary nodules noted at the apex of
the left lung and scarring at the right lung apex are again seen, unchanged.
Ground- glass opacity seen along the major fissure on the left is also stable.
No new pulmonary nodules are identified. The heart size is normal.  Multiple
mediastinal lymph nodes are seen, the largest located precarinally (2:28)
measuring 9 mm in short axis diameter, stable.  No hilar lymphadenopathy is
present.  However, multiple axillary lymph nodes bilaterally have increased in
size, with the largest now measuring approximately 10 mm in short axis
diameter, increased from 7 mm.  In addition, lymph nodes at the base of the
neck appear more prominent on today's study with the largest measuring 10 mm x
18 mm located to of the left carotid artery (2:5), which has greatly increased
in size.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The spleen, pancreas, liver,
stomach, adrenal glands, and intra-abdominal loops of bowel are within normal
limits.  The kidneys again demonstrate multiple small hypodensities, too small
to characterize but unchanged.  The gallbladder contains multiple tiny
calcified stones, stable.

Retroperitoneal lymph nodes have increased in size since the prior study, with
the largest conglomerate located periaortically measuring 6.8 x 2.9 cm,
increased from 4.4 x 1.7 cm (2:66).  Multiple other enlarged lymph nodes
located in the porta cava, splenic hilum, the gallbladder fossa, and within
the mesentery have all increased in size.  There is no free air or free fluid.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The rectum, bladder, and uterus
are within normal limits.  There is diverticulosis without diverticulitis.
Multiple pelvic lymph nodes are seen, especially along the pelvic sidewalls
and a majority have increased in size measuring up to 8 mm.  There is no
inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.  There is stable
anterior vertebral body height loss at L1.  However, there is more than 50%
loss of height of vertebral body T8, with even greater deformity in the
central portions of the superior and inferior endplates.  Vertebral body L5
also demonstrates central height loss of almost 50% with depression of the
superior endplate.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Interval increase of lymphadenopathy diffusely.
2.  New compression fracture of T8 and vertebral body height loss of L5.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  CLL status post allogenic stem cell transplant with recent treatment
for fungal pneumonia with increasing cough.  Evaluate for interval change.

Comparison is made to prior radiographs of ___ and as well, CT torso
___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest without
contrast including 5 mm and thin 1.25 mm axial images.  Coronal and sagittal
reformations were also evaluated.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

There is persistent marked bilateral axillary and central lymphadenopathy as
well as supraclavicular lymph nodes which have slightly diminished in size
when compared to most recent CT chest, previously 10 x 20 mm, currently 10 x
14 mm (2.3). There is marked prominence of the left ventricular wall, which is
consistent with the known underlying anemia.  Retrocrural adenopathy persists,
the largest measuring 12 mm in short axis (series 2.51). Hypoattenuating right
thyroid nodule is again noted.


The lung parenchyma displays persistent biapical scarring with slightly more
prominent homogeneous right upper lobe ground-glass opacity noted on today's
exam, spanning approximately 17 mm (series 4.48).  The previously noted
confluent pleural-based opacities within the left upper lobe and left lower
lobe abutting a major fissure have diminished in size and now display internal
cavitation.  For example, the dominant mass within the lower lobe adjacent to
the fissure currently measures 17 x 19 mm and previously measured 22 x 35 mm
(3.26).  Additional previously solid nodule within the right upper lobe also
is now cavitated (3:23).  Some scattered regions of paraseptal and
centrilobular emphysema within the upper lobes persist as does diffuse regions
of bronchiectasis and bronchiolectasis with regions of bronchial wall
thickening and some inspissated secretions within the distal right middle lobe
bronchioles(4:166).  

New on todays exam is diffuse new and worsening small airways disease with
constellation of ___ opacities, peribronchiolar centrilobular nodules,
and scattered ground-glass nodules diffusely throughout the lung parenchyma. 
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  
Included portions of the upper abdomen display unchanged probable benign renal
cysts and persistent spenic enlargement, which is not fully included on
today's exam.

BONE WINDOWS:  No recent interval change is noted to the previous compression
fracture without bony retropulsion involving L1 and T8.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Mixed response.  Regions of suspected angioinvasive aspergillosis have
diminished in size since earlier examination and now display internal regions
of cavitation, which can be seen as a treatment response.  There are, however,
new diffuse small airways related opacities which is consistent with an
infectious bronchiolitis.

2.  Minimal change to diffuse lymphadenopathy as detailed above.  Unchanged
wedge compression deformities of the thoracic and lumbar spine.

These findings were rediscussed with Dr. ___ on ___ at 10:15 a.m.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with recent treatment for fungal pneumonia in
the setting of allogenic stem cell transplant with recent RSV and pneumonia
presenting with increasing hypoxia.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior exams, the most recent performed ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Noncontrast MDCT-acquired images were obtained through the chest
using 5 mm and 1.25 mm axial collimation.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The lung parenchyma demonstrates numerous new diffuse predominantly
centrilobular patchy ground glass opacities since ___.  The prior dominant
left upper and left lower lung opacities have slightly decreased in size but
other lesions including cavitary nodules are stable since ___.

Heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  Numerous
borderline enlarged mediastinal and hilar nodes are unchanged since ___.
Right thyroid hypodense nodule is stable. 

Although this exam was not optimized for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, the
imaged intra-abdominal organs are unremarkable except to note stable
post-cholecystectomy changes and splenic enlargement as well as multiple
hypodense renal lesions which likely represent cysts.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Marked progression of likely infectious predominantly peribronchiolar
ground glass nodules.  The appearance is concerning for fungal etiology such
as angioinvasive aspergillosis although the differential diagnosis remains
broad and includes other inflammatory and infectious etiologies.  

2.  No change in numerous borderline enlarged lymph nodes.

Findings were discussed with Dr. ___ by phone at the time of dictation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman CLL and pneumonia, now RSV positive with
increasing oxygen requirement.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior exams including ___ CT chest.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast MDCT-acquired images were obtained through the chest
using 5- and 1.25-mm axial collimation.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  Since ___ global bronchocentric infiltration, at all levels from
segmental to lobular bronchi, and large areas of consolidation in the lung
bases has all worsened dramatically.  Also, the dominant cavitary lesion in
the left lower lung  measuring 1.5 cm (3:24) arising from an area of
consolidation that has continued to grow since ___, presumably a site of
active aspergillus infection, but not necessarily the source of the more
widespread abnormalities elsewhere in the lungs. Other subcentimeter cavitary
lesions are unchanged.

Heart size is normal. Small pericardial effusion is unchanged since ___. 
Numerous borderline enlarged mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are unchanged
since ___.  1.4 cm right thyroid nodule is unchanged since ___.

Although this exam is not optimized for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, the imaged
abdominal organs demonstrate multiple stable hypodense renal lesions which
likely represent cysts.

Bone windows demonstrate no lesion concerning for metastasis or infection.
Wedge compression fractures involving the anterior elements of a T8 and L1 are
unchanged since ___. There is no acute fracture.  

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Marked, acute progression of widespread bronchocentric infiltration since
___ with new consolidation in the lower lobes, presumably infectious,
headed by viral or bacterial infection. Although the left lower cavity due to
peristent aspergillosis continues to grow, fungal infections do not generally
progress this rapidly, making multifactorial infection likely. 

2.  No change in numerous borderline enlarged lymph nodes which are likely
reactive.

Findings were discussed by phone with Dr. ___ at the time of dictation.

using 5- and 1.25-mm axial collimation.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were
reviewed.
sections.comparison: Multiple prior exams including ___ CT chest.
sections.findings:
Since ___ global bronchocentric infiltration, at all levels from
segmental to lobular bronchi, and large areas of consolidation in the lung
bases has all worsened dramatically.  Also, the dominant cavitary lesion in
the left lower lung  measuring 1.5 cm (3:24) arising from an area of
consolidation that has continued to grow since ___, presumably a site of
active aspergillus infection, but not necessarily the source of the more
widespread abnormalities elsewhere in the lungs. Other subcentimeter cavitary
lesions are unchanged.

Heart size is normal. Small pericardial effusion is unchanged since ___. 
Numerous borderline enlarged mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are unchanged
since ___.  1.4 cm right thyroid nodule is unchanged since ___.

Although this exam is not optimized for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, the imaged
abdominal organs demonstrate multiple stable hypodense renal lesions which
likely represent cysts.

Bone windows demonstrate no lesion concerning for metastasis or infection.
Wedge compression fractures involving the anterior elements of a T8 and L1 are
unchanged since ___. There is no acute fracture.
sections.impression:
1.  Marked, acute progression of widespread bronchocentric infiltration since
___ with new consolidation in the lower lobes, presumably infectious,
headed by viral or bacterial infection. Although the left lower cavity due to
peristent aspergillosis continues to grow, fungal infections do not generally
progress this rapidly, making multifactorial infection likely. 

2.  No change in numerous borderline enlarged lymph nodes which are likely
reactive.

Findings were discussed by phone with Dr. ___ at the time of dictation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female with worsening substernal chest pain and back
pain for four days with a pleuritic component.  Rule out PE or aortic
dissection.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph performed earlier the same day.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest before
and after administration of 100 cc IV Optiray contrast.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats and oblique maximum intensity projection images are displayed.

CT PULMONARY ANGIOGRAM:  There is no acute pulmonary embolus.  The aorta is
normal without evidence of intramural hematoma, aneurysm or dissection.

There are at least two new lung nodules noted, the first in the right upper
lobe measuring 5 mm (2:28) and a 3-mm nodule in the left upper lobe (2:18). 
There is a third triangular-shaped density in the right lower lobe (2:39)
which could represent an intraparenchymal lymph node.  Scarring is noted at
the lung apices and peripherally in the right lung.  There is minimal
dependent subsegmental atelectasis.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 
Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Heart is normal without
pericardial effusion.

The visualized portion of the liver, spleen, kidneys, and bilateral adrenal
glands are normal.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion is identified.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No acute pulmonary embolus or aortic pathology.

2.  At least two pulmonary nodules measuring up to 5 mm.  Assuming no risk
factors for primary neoplasm, follow up suggested in 12 months.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with dyspnea and tachycardia// eval for pe

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 2.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 21.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
10.8 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 3.4 s, 27.1 cm; CTDIvol = 26.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 729.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 741 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT torso dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: There are pulmonary emboli extending from the distal
right main pulmonary artery to lobar to segmental pulmonary arteries in the
right upper and middle lobes and to lobar to subsegmental pulmonary arteries
in the right lower lobe.  The main pulmonary artery and left pulmonary
arteries and its branches are patent without filling defect.  No evidence of
right heart strain.  Some atherosclerotic changes are seen along the aorta, at
the aortic arch.  are mild to moderate coronary artery calcifications.  No
pericardial effusion.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is bilateral lower lung linear subsegmental atelectasis. 
Scattered areas of subsegmental atelectasis seen elsewhere, including in the
bilateral upper lobes, right middle lobe, and lingula.  No focal
consolidation.  An azygos lobe is incidentally noted.

BASE OF NECK: Scattered hypoattenuating nodule in the right thyroid lobe are
noted measuring up to asthma in 7 mm.

ABDOMEN: Limited evaluation of the upper abdomen demonstrate slight nodular
contour of the liver parenchyma.  The spleen may be surgically absent.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.
Degenerative changes of thoracic spine are moderate.  Evidence of DISH is seen
along the thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Pulmonary emboli extending from the distal right main pulmonary artery to
segmental level in right upper and middle lobes and subsegmental level in
right lower lobe.  No left-sided pulmonary emboli.  Difficult to exclude right
heart strain.  Echocardiogram would further assess.
2.  No focal consolidation.
3.  Mild nodular contour of the liver raise concern for cirrhosis. 
Correlation with liver function test is recommended for further evaluation.
4.  Status post splenectomy.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 4:53 pm, 2 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX

INDICATION:  Longstanding smoker, COPD, opacity noted on previous chest
radiograph.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

FINDINGS:  The apical segments of both upper lobes are completely destroyed by
pulmonary emphysema.  Milder emphysematous lesions, predominantly of
panlobular type are seen predominantly in the upper lobes and the apical
segments of the lower lobes.  In the right upper lobe, series 4, image 50, a
discoid area of consolidation is seen.  The morphology of this consolidation
suggests a combination of atelectasis and parenchymal scarring. On the chest
x-ray, this consolidation was projecting over the right clavicle.

2-mm nodule subpleurally in the right upper lobe (4, 97).  Another subpleural
nodule in the right upper lobe is seen on series 4, image 94, this nodule
measures 5 mm in diameter.  Finally, a 6-mm nodule is seen subpleurally in the
left upper lobe (4, 52).

There is no evidence of mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  Mild coronary
calcifications.  No evidence of pleural effusions.  In the upper parts of the
abdomen, a large left cortical renal cyst is seen.  Small renal cyst on the
right is best seen on the coronal reformats, image 37.  Large gallbladder
without stones.  Otherwise, normal appearance of the liver.

IMPRESSION:

Bullous apical lung destruction.  Two right upper lobe and one left upper lobe
nodules that require CT followup in one year.  Plate-like area of scarring in
the right upper lobe that should also be followed.  Moderate predominantly
panlobular emphysema.  No pleural effusions, bilateral renal cysts.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with Stage II gastric cancer s/p neoadj
chemotherapy and resection (___), c/b abscess, anastomotic leak,
esophageal stricture with recent perforation, J-tube.// recurrence of cancer,
recurrent leak or infection

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chestfollowing the
administration of intravenous contrast. Coronal, sagittal, and MIP
reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 26.5 mGy (Body) DLP =
13.2 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.8 s, 69.3 cm; CTDIvol = 13.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 961.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 974 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid gland is unremarkable. 
There is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the separate report for the same day CT abdomen and
pelvis for subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  Ill-defined prevascular
soft tissue is unchanged and may represent thymic hyperplasia (2:52).

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart size is normal.  No pericardial effusion.  Aortic
valve and coronary artery calcifications are mild.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Biapical and bibasilar scarring is similar to prior.  The
focal consolidation in the right lower lobe has nearly completely resolved.  A
5 mm nodule is noted in the area of prior consolidation (3:51).  A 2 mm right
middle lobe nodule is unchanged (3: 174).  Calcified granulomas are unchanged
(3: 82, 85, 171).  There are no new or enlarging pulmonary nodules.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.
3. VESSELS:  The thoracic aorta and main pulmonary artery are of normal
caliber.  A right chest wall port tip terminates in the lower IVC.

CHEST CAGE: An 8 mm lytic lesion with well-defined sclerotic borders in the
proximal right clavicle is unchanged since at least ___ and likely benign. 
No acute fracture or destructive osseous lesion.

IMPRESSION:


1. 5 mm right lower lobe nodule in the location of prior pneumonia may
represent residual consolidation.  Recommend attention on follow-up. 
Otherwise no new or enlarging pulmonary nodules.
2. Stable prevascular soft tissue density likely reflecting thymic
hyperplasia.
3. Please see separate report for the same day CT abdomen and pelvis for
subdiaphragmatic findings.

c/b abscess, anastomotic leak, esophageal stricture with recent perforation,
J-tube.  // recurrence of cancer, recurrent leak or infection

TECHNIQUE:   MDCT axial images were acquired through the thorax following
administration of IV contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, and MIPS reformats reviewed
in PACS.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest CTs, most recently ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL:  Normal partially imaged thyroid
gland. No enlarged or growing supraclavicular or axillary lymph nodes. Chest
port in the right anterior chest wall, with the tip in the distal SVC.  Mild
edema in the axillary tissue bilaterally.  No additional soft tissue chest
wall abnormality. No atherosclerotic calcification of the imaged neck
arteries.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the CT abdomen and pelvis report dated the same day
for evaluation of the abdomen and pelvis.

MEDIASTINUM: Mildly patulous distal esophagus, superior to the suture line. 
Otherwise normal esophagus.  No enlarged or growing mediastinal lymph nodes. 
No mediastinal mass.  The thoracic aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in
caliber.  Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta.

HILA: No enlarged or growing hilar lymph nodes.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Normal heart size. Minimal coronary artery
calcification.  Mild aortic annular calcification.  No cardiac valve
calcification.  Physiologic pericardium.

PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. Mild biapical pleuroparenchymal
scarring, stable.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: A 5 mm right lower lobe nodule has resolved.  2 mm middle lobe
nodule (3:163), stable.  Stable calcified granulomas (3:79). No new or
enlarging lung nodule or mass.  Stable mild parenchymal scarring at the lung
apices bilaterally, likely post infectious in nature, possibly secondary to
old TB.  There is vascular congestion in the lower lobes bilaterally, slightly
more marked compared with the prior study, without frank pulmonary edema. 
Peribronchial infiltration and scattered ground-glass opacities in the upper
lobes bilaterally and in the superior segment of the left lower lobe, slightly
more marked compared to prior, may be secondary to aspiration.  No
consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  The tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level. 
No bronchial wall thickening or bronchiectasis.
3. VESSELS:  Allowing for the non dedicated nature of the examination, no
filling defects in the pulmonary arteries.

CHEST CAGE:  No lytic or sclerotic lesion concerning for infection or
malignancy.  No fracture.

IMPRESSION:


-No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.  Two stable pulmonary nodules
measuring up to 5 mm.  No new or enlarging pulmonary nodule.
-Stable mild parenchymal scarring at the lung apices bilaterally, likely post
infectious in nature, possibly secondary to old TB.
-Vascular congestion in the lower lobes bilaterally, slightly more marked
compared with the prior study, without frank pulmonary edema.
-Probable sequelae of aspiration in the upper lobes and superior segment of
the left lower lobe.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 1.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 5.5 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 12.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.7 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 19.2 s, 73.9 cm; CTDIvol = 10.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
777.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 811 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

FINDINGS: 

Lungs:

Parenchyma and Airways: Endotracheal tube tip is 4 cm above carina.  There are
mild tracheal, right mainstem bronchus secretions.  There is mild mucous
plugging in the left lower lobe.  Bilateral lower lobe opacities, likely
represent atelectasis.  There is mild interstitial edema.  There are changes
of paraseptal and moderately severe centrilobular emphysema in the lungs. 
There multiple benign calcified granuloma, more spur the prominent in the
right upper lung, with mild volume loss is, scarring.  Prominent subpleural
fat bilateral lung apices.  Few small lung nodules bilaterally, largest
measures 0.4 cm in the left mid lung series 6, image 134.
Vessels: Dilated main pulmonary artery, consistent with pulmonary artery
hypertension.  Central pulmonary arteries are patent.  Atherosclerotic changes
of normal caliber aorta.

Mediastinum and Hila: There few subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes, likely
reactive.  No hilar adenopathy.

Heart and Pericardium: Heart is enlarged.  Suggestion of chronic
subendocardial infarct.  Coronary artery calcifications.  Cardiac pacemaker
with lead tip in the right ventricle.

Pleura: Trace bilateral pleural effusions.

Neck, Thoracic Inlet, Axillae, Chest Wall: No mass, no adenopathy.  0.7 cm
right thyroid lobe nodule, of doubtful significance, no further follow-up is
indicated according to guidelines.

Upper Abdomen: See separately dictated report CT abdomen pelvis from today.

Chest Cage: There few chronic rib fractures.  Few small benign bone islands. 
No acute fractures.

IMPRESSION: 

Bilateral lower lobe opacities, most likely atelectasis, adjacent small
pleural effusions.  Component of pneumonia is unlikely, though it cannot be
excluded particularly on the right.  There is mucous plugging in the left
base, and trachea, right mainstem bronchus secretions.
Paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema.  Pulmonary artery hypertension.
Small lung nodules, largest 0.4 cm.  Follow-up chest CT without contrast in ___
year recommended

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Chest CT without contrast in ___ year

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Chest CT

INDICATION:  ___ admitted w/ nonproductive cough, hypoxia of unclear etiology.
CXR negative x3, no flu syx, TTE WNL, remains hypoxic (SaO2 low ___ with mild
resp alkalosis.  // HI-RES. R/o IPF, other interstitial processes, possible
nitrofuratoin-induced pulmonary toxicity.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained from thoracic inlet to upper
abdomen.  Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with coronal and sagittal
reformats

COMPARISON:   ___

FINDINGS: 

No pathologically enlarged mediastinal hilar or axillary lymph nodes
demonstrated. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in diameter. Coronary
calcifications are present. Heart size is normal. No pericardial pleural
effusion is seen. Image portion of the upper abdomen reveals no appreciable
abnormality

Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. Diffuse bronchial
wall thickening is noted as well as some irregularity of the upper trachea.

No lytic or sclerotic lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm have been
demonstrated. Compression vertebral fracture and vertebroplasty is
demonstrated, unchanged.

Assessment of the lung parenchyma demonstrate diffuse primarily ground-glass
opacities with septal thickening D ground-glass opacities are multiple and
there are also pulmonary nodules noted with surrounding halo,, largest 1 in
the right lower lobe, series 6 image 146.

No distinct interstitial process demonstrated. No evidence of septal
thickening to suggest pulmonary edema is present.

IMPRESSION: 

Combination of ground-glass opacities, solid nodules and solid/ground-glass
nodules within hello might be consistent with diffuse infectious process.
Alternatively atypical mycobacterial infection, hypersensitivity reaction,
aspiration or vasculitis would be a possibility. Neoplasm is substantially
less likely. Cryptogenic organizing pneumonia is another less likely
possibility

Diffuse bronchial wall thickening and endobronchial secretions might reflect
part of the in infection/inflammatory process.

Irregularity of the upper trachea with be beneficial to proceed with direct
evaluation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with L rib cage, flank tenderness after fall// eval
fracture, hematoma, other acute process

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Other than
atherosclerotic calcification in the aortic arch, aortic valve, and coronary
artery calcifications, the heart, and great vessels are within normal limits
based on an unenhanced scan. No pericardial effusion.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Evaluation of the pulmonary parenchyma is slightly limited by
motion.  Few scattered pulmonary nodules measure up to 5 mm in the left lower
lobe pulmonary nodule, stable from ___ (02:24).  Scant chronic hyperdense
material in several left lung pulmonary arteries likely represents embolized
material from prior kyphoplasty, unchanged (4:82; 4:97).  There is minimal
dependent atelectasis.  Otherwise, the lungs are clear without masses or areas
of parenchymal opacification.  The airways are patent to the level of the
segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: Other than atherosclerotic disease in the aorta and mildly atrophic
pancreas, the included portion of the unenhanced upper abdomen is
unremarkable.  Specifically, there is no evidence of traumatic injury or
focally fluid surrounding the spleen, or either upper quadrant in the abdomen.

BONES: Acute minimally displaced left posterior 8th rib fracture (4:130). 
Severe compression deformity of the T8 vertebral body with trace surrounding
soft tissue edema is new from ___ and likely acute to subacute age
(602:70).  There is associated minimal 2 mm of bony retropulsion without
high-grade spinal stenosis.  Moderate to severe compression deformity T11
vertebral body with evidence of prior kyphoplasty, overall unchanged from
___.

IMPRESSION:


1. Acute minimally displaced left posterior eighth rib fracture.
2. No pneumothorax or hemothorax.
3. Interval development since ___ of a severe T8 vertebral body
compression deformity with minimal surrounding soft tissue edema suggesting
that this is acute to subacute in age.  Minimal 2 mm of bony retropulsion
without high-grade spinal stenosis.
4. No additional acute fractures identified.
5. No evidence of intrathoracic or upper abdominal traumatic injury.
6. No evidence of splenic injury or free fluid in the upper abdomen.
7. Scattered pulmonary nodules measure up to 5 mm, not substantially changed
from ___.  No dedicated CT follow up is recommended unless there is
additional clinical concern.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ women with TB C/NASH cirrhosis here for liver
transplant evaluation.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images

DOSE:    This study involved 3 CT acquisition phases with dose indices as
follows:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph

mGy-cm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:      Cirrhosis.  Liver transplant candidate.

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS PERFORMED WITHOUT
INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5 AND 1.25 MM THICK
AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP AXIAL IMAGES.
SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY, AND
WILL PROVIDE THE TOTAL DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP reported separatelymGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

Since a recent study of ___, the patient has been intubated with
endotracheal tube in standard position.  Subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes
are similar to the prior study.  Heart is mildly enlarged, and note is made of
diffuse coronary artery calcifications.  Trace right pleural effusion and
diffuse anasarca are new since the prior study.

Skeletal structures of the thorax demonstrate multilevel degenerative changes
in the spine.

Assessment of the lungs is limited by respiratory motion and submaximal
inspiratory level.  Since the prior study, multifocal, upper lung predominant
ground-glass opacities and a localized region of lobular consolidation in the
periphery of the right upper lobe are new, as well as bibasilar regions of
atelectasis, right greater than left.  Additional scattered subpleural nodular
opacities are observed with mid lung predominance, some of which have a
lobular configuration.  Previously reported 3 mm nodules are better seen on
the prior CT which was not limited by motion.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  New multifocal upper and mid lung predominant opacities are concerning for
infection in the appropriate clinical setting.  Considering the nodular
quality of some of the opacities, Pseudomonas and fungal organisms should be
considered in addition to other pathogens.

2.  Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for complete
description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT torso

INDICATION:  ___ s/p fall from 20 feet landing on back,  no head strike, no
LOC. GCS 15 on arrival. c/o lumbar pain, sacrum tenderness, and left gluteal
abrasion/ecchimosis.  // please rule out acute injury

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT images were obtained through the chest, abdomen
and pelvis.  Axial images were interpreted in conjunction with coronal,
sagittal, right oblique.

DOSE:  DLP: 1186 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  Pelvis and chest x-ray ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

The included portion of the thyroid is normal. Axillary, supraclavicular,
mediastinal, and hilar lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.  The heart
and mediastinum are normal. The pericardium is intact without effusion. 
Airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.

A 3 mm nodule is noted in the right lower lobe (series 2, image 35) and a 4mm
nodule is noted in the left lower lobe (series 2, image 42). The pleura is
intact without effusion. No pneumothorax or pneumomediastinum.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY:  The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout.  A
1.4 x 1.0 cm hypodensity is noted in segment II of the liver. There is no
evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. The gallbladder
is within normal limits, without stones or gallbladder wall thickening.

SPLEEN:  The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.

ADRENALS:  The right and left adrenal glands are normal.  A gastric fundal
diverticulum is noted superior to the left adrenal gland.

URINARY: The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast promptly
without hydronephrosis.

GASTROINTESTINAL:  The small and large bowel are normal in course and caliber
without obstruction. Scattered diverticulosis is noted particularly in the
sigmoid colon. Colon and rectum are within normal limits. Appendix contains
air, has normal caliber without evidence of fat stranding.

MESENTERY AND RETROPERITONEUM: There is no evidence of retroperitoneal and
mesenteric lymphadenopathy. There is no free fluid and no free air.

VASCULAR:  There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  There is no calcium burden
in the abdominal aorta and great abdominal arteries.  The main portal vein,
splenic vein and SMV are patent.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder is decompressed containing a Foley catheter. 
Distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no evidence of pelvic or inguinal
lymphadenopathy.   Reproductive organs are within normal limits

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: No bone finding suspicious for infection or malignancy
is seen. Abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits. Fractures are
noted of the coccyx and left sacrum (series 2, image 109) with a moderate
sized hematoma extending into the presacral space. Additionally, there is a
superior endplate deformity of the T7 vertebral body.  Soft tissue stranding
seen in the subcutaneous tissues to the right of the gluteal fold, likely
posttraumatic.

IMPRESSION: 

1. Left sacral and coccygeal fractures with an associated hematoma extending
into the presacral space.

2. Superior endplate deformity of the T7 vertebral body of uncertain
chronicity. Given the patient's mechanism of injury, clinical correlation is
recommended to exclude an acute injury.

3. Lung nodules noted in the right middle and left lower lobes, as detailed
above. In the case of nodule size <= 4 mm: No follow-up needed in low-risk
patients. For high risk patients, recommend follow-up at 12 months and if no
change, no further imaging needed.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings of this study and an updated wet read were
communicated to ___ at 15:14 on ___ by Dr.
___ by page.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ old man with metastatic melanoma.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis, ___ from ___
___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT data were acquired through the chest after the administration
of intravenous contrast.  Data were reconstructed using lung and soft tissue
kernels.  Images were displayed in multiple planes and slice thicknesses.

FINDINGS:  The thyroid gland enhances homogeneously.  No mediastinal, hilar,
or axillary adenopathy is present.  The heart is not enlarged. There is no
pericardial effusion. The great vessels are normal in caliber.  Diffuse
coronary artery calcifications and thoracic aortic atherosclerosis are
moderate. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

This exam is not tailored to evaluate subdiaphragmatic structures.  Numerous
hypodensities throughout the liver are incompletely characterized.  The
largest lesions are seen in segments ___ and measure up to 2 cm (3:50,
3:51).  Innumerable hypoattenuating lesions are seen in the spleen.  The
adrenal glands have normal morphology and appearance.  A sub-cm hypodensity in
the upper pole of the right kidney is too small to characterize, but likely a
cyst.  A 1.6cm caval lymph node (3:73) is centrally necrotic.

A 12 mm irregular nodule in the lingula (6:213) has decreased in size since
___ and is probably an evolving scar.  Fibrotic changes are present at
both bases.  

The following small nodules are identified: 

RUL 1mm peipheral nodule (6:116)
RUL 3mm peripheral nodule (6:190)
RLL 1mm granuloma (6:280)
RUL 1mm granuloma (6:156)
Milimetric LLL subpleural nodules.

No additional nodules, consolidations, or effusions are present.  There is no
pneumothorax.

There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  12mm irregular lingular nodule is decreasing and most likely represents
scar.  This and other small pulmonary nodules can be followed in 3 months to
ensure expected evolution.

2.  No evidence of pulmoanry metastases.

3.  Extensive hepatic and splenic lesions are most likely metastases but are
incompletely assessed on this exam.  The recent abdominal and pelvic CT on
___ was performed without intravenous contrast.  An abdominal CT with
intravenous contrast would further characterize liver and splenic lesions
lesions if this is clinically relevent for disease staging.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man with metastatic melanoma, assess for response to
treatment.

COMPARISONS:  CT abdomen and pelvis without contrast from ___.  CT chest
with contrast from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial imaging was obtained from the thoracic inlet to the
pubic symphysis following the administration of intravenous contrast material.
Coronal and sagittal reformats were completed.

DLP:  1097.40 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  There are no enlarged supraclavicular, axillary,
mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes.  The heart and pericardium are notable for
coronary calcifications, but there is no pericardial effusion.  The great
vessels are unremarkable.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels. 
Multiple bilateral lung nodules are again seen but are unchanged from the
prior exam.  For example, a 2 mm right upper lobe nodule (4:17), 4 mm right
middle lobe nodule (4:32) and 7 mm lingular nodule (4:38) are stable.  There
are no new lung nodules, focal consolidation or concerning masses.  Bibasilar
atelectasis is similar in appearance to the prior exam.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  There are multiple hypodense lesions within the
liver likely representing metastatic disease.  These are similar in appearance
to the non-contrast scan from ___.  Some of these are ill-defined,
but the largest appears to be in segment ___ (4:43) measuring 4.0 x 3.7 cm. 
The gallbladder, pancreas and adrenal glands are unremarkable.  There are
multiple rounded hypodense lesions within the spleen also most likely
metastatic disease.  The largest in the inferior pole measures 3.0 x 3.3 cm
(4:54).   The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically without any
hydronephrosis.  Again seen is a left parapelvic cyst.  There are two
exophytic lesions within the right kidney, the first from the upper pole
measuring 8 mm (4:60) and second from the interpolar region measuring 1.6 cm. 
These are most compatible with cysts. However, metastatic disease cannot be
completely excluded.  The stomach, small and intra-abdominal large bowel are
unremarkable.  There is an old calcified right renal artery pseudoaneurysm
(4:63).  Again seen are multiple retroperitoneal lymph nodes within the
paraaortic station which are similar to the prior exam. Large right paraaortic
node measures 1.7 x 2.6 compared to 1.7 x 2.3 on the prior exam and is
slightly larger.  More inferior node measures 1.3 cm compared to 1.2 cm on the
prior exam.  Aortocaval node measures 1.4 x 1.4 cm compared to 1.5 x 1.5 cm on
the prior study.  Other scattered left paraaortic nodes are present, but are
not pathologically enlarged.  The aorta and its major branches are patent. 
There is atherosclerotic calcification involving the aorta extending into
iliac arteries.  There is no free fluid or free air within the abdomen.

CT PELVIS:  The rectum and sigmoid colon and prostate gland are unremarkable. 
The prostate is enlarged and presses upon the inferior portion of the bladder.
Significant adenopathy along the right pelvic side wall extending along the
external iliac chain within the right inguinal region is overall unchanged. 
The large right inguinal lymph node measures 2.5 x 2.4 cm compared to 3.5 x
2.3 cm on the prior exam.  The second large nodal conglomerate measures 4.3 x
2.4 cm compared to 5.2 x 3.0 cm.  This is slightly different in configuration
than on the prior exam.  Right pelvic sidewall node measures 2.3 x 1.6 cm
compared to 1.8 x 2.4 cm (4:101).

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Diffuse metastatic disease consistent with known metastatic melanoma. 
Overall, there has been no significant change in disease burden.
2.  Stable subcentimeter pulmonary nodules.
3.  Multiple hepatic and splenic lesions compatible with metastatic disease.
4.  Stable retroperitoneal, right pelvic and inguinal lymphadenopathy.
5.  Two hypodense lesions in the right kidney that are most likely cysts;
however, metastatic disease cannot be completely excluded.

sections.indication:
___ man with metastatic melanoma, assess for response to
treatment.

COMPARISONS:  CT abdomen and pelvis without contrast from ___.  CT chest
with contrast from ___.
sections.technique:
MDCT axial imaging was obtained from the thoracic inlet to the
pubic symphysis following the administration of intravenous contrast material.
Coronal and sagittal reformats were completed.

DLP:  1097.40 mGy-cm.
sections.findings:
CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  There are no enlarged supraclavicular, axillary,
mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes.  The heart and pericardium are notable for
coronary calcifications, but there is no pericardial effusion.  The great
vessels are unremarkable.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels. 
Multiple bilateral lung nodules are again seen but are unchanged from the
prior exam.  For example, a 2 mm right upper lobe nodule (4:17), 4 mm right
middle lobe nodule (4:32) and 7 mm lingular nodule (4:38) are stable.  There
are no new lung nodules, focal consolidation or concerning masses.  Bibasilar
atelectasis is similar in appearance to the prior exam.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  There are multiple hypodense lesions within the
liver likely representing metastatic disease.  These are similar in appearance
to the non-contrast scan from ___.  Some of these are ill-defined,
but the largest appears to be in segment ___ (4:43) measuring 4.0 x 3.7 cm. 
The gallbladder, pancreas and adrenal glands are unremarkable.  There are
multiple rounded hypodense lesions within the spleen also most likely
metastatic disease.  The largest in the inferior pole measures 3.0 x 3.3 cm
(4:54).   The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically without any
hydronephrosis.  Again seen is a left parapelvic cyst.  There are two
exophytic lesions within the right kidney, the first from the upper pole
measuring 8 mm (4:60) and second from the interpolar region measuring 1.6 cm. 
These are most compatible with cysts. However, metastatic disease cannot be
completely excluded.  The stomach, small and intra-abdominal large bowel are
unremarkable.  There is an old calcified right renal artery pseudoaneurysm
(4:63).  Again seen are multiple retroperitoneal lymph nodes within the
paraaortic station which are similar to the prior exam. Large right paraaortic
node measures 1.7 x 2.6 compared to 1.7 x 2.3 on the prior exam and is
slightly larger.  More inferior node measures 1.3 cm compared to 1.2 cm on the
prior exam.  Aortocaval node measures 1.4 x 1.4 cm compared to 1.5 x 1.5 cm on
the prior study.  Other scattered left paraaortic nodes are present, but are
not pathologically enlarged.  The aorta and its major branches are patent. 
There is atherosclerotic calcification involving the aorta extending into
iliac arteries.  There is no free fluid or free air within the abdomen.

CT PELVIS:  The rectum and sigmoid colon and prostate gland are unremarkable. 
The prostate is enlarged and presses upon the inferior portion of the bladder.
Significant adenopathy along the right pelvic side wall extending along the
external iliac chain within the right inguinal region is overall unchanged. 
The large right inguinal lymph node measures 2.5 x 2.4 cm compared to 3.5 x
2.3 cm on the prior exam.  The second large nodal conglomerate measures 4.3 x
2.4 cm compared to 5.2 x 3.0 cm.  This is slightly different in configuration
than on the prior exam.  Right pelvic sidewall node measures 2.3 x 1.6 cm
compared to 1.8 x 2.4 cm (4:101).

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions.
sections.impression:
1.  Diffuse metastatic disease consistent with known metastatic melanoma. 
Overall, there has been no significant change in disease burden.
2.  Stable subcentimeter pulmonary nodules.
3.  Multiple hepatic and splenic lesions compatible with metastatic disease.
4.  Stable retroperitoneal, right pelvic and inguinal lymphadenopathy.
5.  Two hypodense lesions in the right kidney that are most likely cysts;
however, metastatic disease cannot be completely excluded.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT TORSO

COMPARISON:  ___.

HISTORY:  Shortness of breath and intermittent diffuse abdominal pain, status
post Whipple procedure.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images through the chest, abdomen and pelvis
were obtained.  IV contrast was administered.  Coronal and sagittal reformats
were performed.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:  There are no filling defects within the main pulmonary arteries or
its branches to suggest pulmonary embolism.  The heart and great vessels are
unremarkable.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  There is no focal
consolidation or pneumothorax.  The left thyroid gland contains a punctate
calcification.  There is no axillary, hilar, or mediastinal lymphadenopathy. 
There are mild emphysematous changes seen within the lung apices.  A lung cyst
is identified in the left lower lobe measuring approximately 2.5 cm. 2 mm
right upper lobe nodule is visualized, as well as a 3 mm left upper lobe
calcified granuloma.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:  Patient is status post Whipple procedure.  Adjacent to the
afferent limb, anterior to the pancreatic neck, there is a lobulated fluid
collection measuring approximately 2.8 x 1.3 cm (5, 21).  There is expected
pneumobilia.  There is mild persistent central ductal dilatation and
pancreatic ductal dilatation.  The spleen, right adrenal gland are
unremarkable.  The kidneys contain subcentimeter hypodense lesions which are
too small to characterize.  The left adrenal gland is diffusely enlarged and
is heterogeneously enhancing, similar to prior exam.  Scattered mesenteric and
retroperitoneal lymph nodes are identified.  A small fatty lesion is
identified within the duodenum (5, 29), not clearly seen before and may
represent ingested contents, less likely a lipoma.  Calcification of the
descending aorta and its branches are noted.  Small bowel loops are normal in
caliber.  There is no evidence of free air. Small amount of perihepatic fluid
is present.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  The appendix is normal.  The rectum, sigmoid colon,
bladder, and uterus are unremarkable.  There is a small amount of free fluid
within the dependent portions of the pelvis.  There is no pelvic or inguinal
lymphadenopathy.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions identified. 
Multilevel degenerative changes are noted.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolus.
2.  Punctate left thyroid calcification for which clinical correlation is
recommended with possible ultrasound.
3.  Mild centrilobular emphysema.
4.  Small fluid collection within the surgical bed as described above. 
Clinical correlation is recommended and infection of this collection cannot be
excluded.  
5.  Small amount of fluid within the perihepatic region, and dependent portion
of the pelvis. 
6.  Left adrenal gland lesion, unchanged.
7.  2 mm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule. Follow up chest CT in ___ year is
recommended.

Findings were discussed with surgical resident.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CLINICAL INDICATION:  Pancreatic cancer, status post Whipple and two cycles of
adjuvant chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest, abdomen and pelvis was performed following the
uneventful administration of non-ionic intravenous contrast and oral contrast.
Delayed images were obtained through the abdomen.  Sagittal and coronal
reformatted images were reviewed.

Comparison is made to prior exam performed on ___.  Correlation
is also made with MR performed on ___.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  There are no pathologically enlarged thoracic lymph nodes.  There is
no pericardial or pleural effusion.  There are mild emphysematous changes.  A
calcified granuloma is noted in the left upper lobe.  There are no suspicious
pulmonary nodules or masses.  Left lower lobe cyst is again identified.  The
central airways are patent.

There are post-surgical changes from Whipple.  Previously seen post-surgical
fluid collection has resolved, with only minimal fluid anteriorly.  There is
no evidence of soft tissue mass in the surgical bed.  The remaining pancreas
is unremarkable.  There are several new areas of wedge-shaped peripheral
hyperenhancement within the liver seen on the initial phase images.  Some of
these have a central focus of low attenuation; for example, image 51 of series
5 and image 54 of series 5.  There is a focal subcentimeter hypodense lesion
in segment II, too small to characterize, but stable from prior exam.  There
is no biliary dilatation.  There are stable left adrenal nodules, previously
characterized as adenomas.  There are a few subcentimeter hypodense renal
lesions, too small to characterize, but likely representing small cysts. 
There are no concerning renal lesions.  The right adrenal gland and spleen are
normal.  There are no pathologically enlarged lymph nodes by size criteria. 
The abdominal bowel loops are unremarkable and there is no ascites.

PELVIS:  The uterus and adnexa are unremarkable, as are the pelvic bowel
loops.  There is a small amount of pelvic free fluid, decreased from the prior
exam.

Bone windows demonstrate no focal suspicious lesions.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Status post Whipple with interval resolution of fluid collection within
the surgical bed.

2.  New wedge-shaped subcapsular areas of hypervascularity within the liver,
some of which demonstrates a central focus of hypodensity.  This is a
concerning finding and could represent metastatic disease, although could also
represent subclinical cholangitis.  If determination will affect patient care,
then MRI with Eovist could be performed for further evaluation.  If not, then
a short-interval followup is recommended.

Findings were entered into the critical results dashboard.
