radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with pancreatic cancer status post resection,
now completed six cycles of adjuvant Gemzar.  Assess for interval change.

COMPARISON:  Multiple CT torsos, most recent of ___ and MR of the
abdomen of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axially acquired images were obtained from the lung apices to the
pubic symphysis after the administration of 100 cc of Optiray intravenous
contrast.  Three-minute delayed images were also obtained through the abdomen.
Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also displayed.

FINDINGS:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  A 1- to 2-mm pulmonary nodule is again
noted in the right lower lobe (2:24) which is stable since ___.  No
other pulmonary nodules are identified.  There is no mediastinal, hilar, or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  The heart size is normal.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  Patient is status post Whipple
procedure.  The spleen, kidneys, remaining pancreas, stomach, and intra-
abdominal loops of bowel are within normal limits.  Small amount of central
pneumobilia is again noted, unchanged.  In addition, there is minimal intra-
hepatic biliary dilatation which is also unchanged.  Tiny hypodensities are
again noted within the liver, too small to characterize, but unchanged in
appearance since the previous study.

A 2.7-cm left adrenal nodule is again noted, unchanged,described as an adenoma
on the prior MR abdomen.  The right adrenal gland is within normal limits.
Small retroperitoneal lymph nodes do not meet CT criteria for pathologic
enlargement.  There is no free air or free fluid.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The rectum, uterus, and bladder
are within normal limits.  A small amount of free fluid is noted within the
pelvis, which was also present on the previous study.  Intrapelvic loops of
bowel are within normal limits.  There is no pelvic or inguinal
lymphadenopathy.

BONE WINDOWS:  No concerning osseous lesions are identified.

IMPRESSIONS:
1.  Tiny hypodensities in the liver are again noted, too small to
characterize, but unchanged in appearance since the previous study.

2.  Stable 1- to 2-mm pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe which is
unchanged since ___.

3.  Stable appearance of left adrenal adenoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Evaluation of patient with history of pancreatic cancer, status
post Whipple and adjuvant chemotherapy.

COMPARISON:  CT torso with contrast from ___ and CT torso with
contrast from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained from the thoracic inlet
to the pubic symphysis after the administration of oral contrast and IV
contrast.  Three-minute delayed images were obtained through the abdomen. 
Multiplanar reformatted images were prepared.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Two 2-mm right lower lobe nodules (4:25, 29) are
again seen and appear unchanged in comparison to prior study.  No other
pulmonary nodules are identified.  There is no evidence of mediastinal, hilar,
axillary, or subclavicular lymphadenopathy.  The heart and great vessels
appear within normal limits.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The patient remains status post
Whipple procedure.  There is a new 13 mm x 13 mm round hepatic lesion in
segment VIII of the liver (4:59) which is suspicious for metastatic disease. 
Otherwise, multiple tiny hypodensities previously described in the liver which
remain too small to characterize are again seen and remain unchanged.  There
is a small amount of central pneumobilia and mild intrahepatic biliary
dilatation which appears unchanged.

Two nodules within the left adrenal gland are again seen and remain stable. 
The right adrenal gland is unremarkable.  The spleen, kidneys, remaining
pancreas, stomach, and visualized loops of large and small bowel are again
seen and appear unremarkable. There is no evidence of pathologic
retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  No free abdominal air or
fluid.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The uterus, bladder, sigmoid
colon, and rectum appear unremarkable.  There is no free pelvic fluid or air. 
There is no pathologic pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  New 13 x 13 mm round hepatic lesion in segment VIII of the liver which is
suspicious for metastatic disease.  This lesion may be amenable to
percutaneous biopsy.
2.  Stable appearance of right lower lobe pulmonary nodules, left adrenal
adenoma, and other tiny liver hypodensities.

These findings were discussed by Dr. ___ with Dr. ___ at 3:20 p.m. on
___.

sections.findings:
CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Two 2-mm right lower lobe nodules (4:25, 29) are
again seen and appear unchanged in comparison to prior study.  No other
pulmonary nodules are identified.  There is no evidence of mediastinal, hilar,
axillary, or subclavicular lymphadenopathy.  The heart and great vessels
appear within normal limits.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The patient remains status post
Whipple procedure.  There is a new 13 mm x 13 mm round hepatic lesion in
segment VIII of the liver (4:59) which is suspicious for metastatic disease. 
Otherwise, multiple tiny hypodensities previously described in the liver which
remain too small to characterize are again seen and remain unchanged.  There
is a small amount of central pneumobilia and mild intrahepatic biliary
dilatation which appears unchanged.

Two nodules within the left adrenal gland are again seen and remain stable. 
The right adrenal gland is unremarkable.  The spleen, kidneys, remaining
pancreas, stomach, and visualized loops of large and small bowel are again
seen and appear unremarkable. There is no evidence of pathologic
retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  No free abdominal air or
fluid.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The uterus, bladder, sigmoid
colon, and rectum appear unremarkable.  There is no free pelvic fluid or air. 
There is no pathologic pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions.
sections.impression:
1.  New 13 x 13 mm round hepatic lesion in segment VIII of the liver which is
suspicious for metastatic disease.  This lesion may be amenable to
percutaneous biopsy.
2.  Stable appearance of right lower lobe pulmonary nodules, left adrenal
adenoma, and other tiny liver hypodensities.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Metastatic pancreatic cancer.

COMPARISON:  MR ___ Multiple CTs dating back to ___, most
recently ___.

TECHNIQUE:  64 row MDCT-acquired axial images from the thoracic outlet to the
pubic symphysis were displayed with oral and 130 mL Optiray intravenous
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.  Additionally
non-contrast and arterial phase images were obtained through the abdomen. The
arterial phase was performed with 80kvp.

CT CHEST:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of
dissection.  Contrast bolus timing is not optimized to evaluate for pulmonary
embolism, but there is no large central PE.  No pathologically enlarged
mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymph nodes are present.  Atherosclerotic
calcifications are seen at the origin of the left subclavian artery. 
Otherwise, the heart, pericardium and great vessels are within normal limits.
There is a small amount of fluid in the superior pericardial recess.  There is
no pleural effusion.  A 6mm left thyroid nodule as well as a nodule in the
thyroid isthmus are again seen.

Lung window images demonstrate a 4-mm pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe
and a smaller right lower lobe nodule (4B:98, 103), both unchanged from the
prior study.  No new lesion is seen.  A bulla in the left lower lobe is
unchanged.  Airways are patent to the subsegmental levels bilaterally.

CT ABDOMEN: The patient is status post Whipple procedure. A 2.3 x 2.4 cm
hypodensity with peripheral arterial enhancement in segment VIII of the liver
previously measured 1.3 x 1.3 cm. Two smaller lesions in segment VII are too
small to characterize but are unchanged from ___ (4B:137). The anterior
lesion is more conspicuous on today's study. Hyperdensity in segment V on
arterial phase is probably related to perfusion anomaly. Mild pneumobilia and
intrahepatic ductal dilatation are unchanged from ___.  The remaining
pancreas enhances homogenously.

The spleen and right adrenal gland are normal. Left adrenal nodules,
previously characterized as adenomas on MRI ___ are stable. The kidneys
enhance symmetrically and without hydronephrosis.  Bilateral tiny
hypodensities in the renal cortices are too small to characterize (4B:151,
4B:133). The small and large bowel are normal in caliber without obstruction. 
Mild atherosclerotic calcifications are seen in the aorta without aneurysmal
dilatation.  The main portal vein, splenic vein, and SMV are patent.  There is
no free fluid and no free air.  No mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymph nodes
are pathologically enlarged.

CT PELVIS:  The rectum, sigmoid colon, bladder and uterus are normal.  There
is no free fluid and no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONE WINDOWS:  No lytic or sclerotic lesion suspicious for malignancy is seen.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Increased size of right hepatic metastasis. Other small hepatic
hypodensities are stable.
2.  Stable right lower lobe pulmonary nodules
3.  Stable left adrenal adenoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with pancreatic cancer status post Whipple with
known hepatic lesions.  Rising CA ___.  Assess for interval change.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT images were obtained in a multiphasic fashion from the
thoracic inlet to the pubic symphysis.  Oral and 130 cc of Optiray IV contrast
were administered.  Axial images were interpreted with conjunction with
coronal and sagittal reformats.

DLP:  715 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  

A 4-mm nodule of the thyroid isthmus is unchanged.  A 6-mm left thyroid lobe
nodule is again seen.  A right chest wall port catheter terminates in the
right atrium.  No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are present in the
axillary, subclavian, mediastinal, or hilar chains.  The heart is
unremarkable.  The pericardium is intact with small unchanged pericardial
fluid.  The great vessels are unremarkable.  The airways are patent to the
subsegmental levels without endobronchial lesion.  Small 3-mm right lower lung
nodule (2:20) is unchanged since the prior exam.  An unchanged bulla is
located in the left lower lobe.  The lungs are otherwise clear without new
focal lesion.  The pleura is intact without effusion.

ABDOMEN:

The patient is status post Whipple operation.  The lesion in liver segment
VIII has undergone significant morphologic change.  Previously, the lesion was
homogeneous, nearly completely cystic, and measured 2.4 x 2.3 cm.  The lesion
now appears multifocal with an increased proportion of solid components, and
the conglomerate lesion measures 2.6 x 2.2 cm.  Two smaller lesions in liver
segment VII (2:57) are too small to characterize but are unchanged since
___.  Mild pneumobilia is unchanged.  No definite new sites of
metastatic disease within the hepatic parenchyma.  The portal and hepatic
vessels are patent and unremarkable.

The remaining pancreas enhances homogeneously without focal lesion.  The
spleen is unremarkable.  Left adrenal nodules, previously characterized as
adenomas on MRI, are stable.  The right adrenal gland is normal.  Bilateral
kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically.  Tiny bilateral renal
hypodensities are too small to characterize.  The ureters are unremarkable.

The esophagus and stomach are normal.  The small and large bowel are normal in
caliber, with expected changes after Whipple.

Atherosclerotic calcifications are seen of the abdominal aorta and common
iliac arteries.  No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric
lymph nodes are present.  No ascites or pneumoperitoneum.  No abdominal wall
hernia.

PELVIS:

The bladder is normal.  The uterus is unremarkable.  No pathologically
enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes.  No free pelvic fluid.  No inguinal
hernia.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No focal lytic or sclerotic lesions concerning for
malignancy are present.  Multilevel degenerative changes are present of the
thoracolumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Liver segment VIII lesion has undergone morphologic change.  Lesion was
previously single and nearly completely cystic, and is now multifocal with an
increased proportion of solid components.  The current conglomerate lesion is
minimally larger than the previous single lesion.

2.  No definite sites of new metastatic disease.  Unchanged liver segment VII
hypodense lesions.  Unchanged right lower lobe nodule.

3.  Status post Whipple operation with stable appearance of anastomosis.

4.  Stable left adrenal adenoma.

Findings were entered into the critical results database by ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with redo stenotomy/ AVR  // eval for aortic
calcifications      eval for aortic calcifications

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric, multidetector CT of the chest was performed without
intravenous or oral contrast administration.  Images are presented for display
in the axial plane at 5 mm and 1.25 mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar
reformation images are also submitted for review.

Total exam DLP: 631 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Same-day preoperative chest radiograph. Otherwise, no prior
studies are available for comparison.

FINDINGS: 

There is a 7 mm hypodense nodule in the left lobe of the thyroid gland (2:6).
A right lower paratracheal lymph node measures 19 x 12 mm (2:23). No
significant axillary, supraclavicular or hilar lymphadenopathy is detected.
The esophagus is unremarkable.

The patient is status post aortic valve replacement and homograft of the
ascending aortic root with heavy calcification of the valve and homograft.
Coronary calcification is mild in the proximal LAD and circumflex arteries.
The heart is mildly enlarged without pericardial effusion. Calcification of
the mitral valve is mild.

Approximately 1 cm above the valve, the ascending aorta measures 33 x 30 mm
(02:31). At the same level, the pulmonary artery measures 29 mm in diameter
and the descending thoracic aorta measures 29 x 28 mm. The ascending aorta
measures up to 41 mm in maximal diameter above the level of the aortic
homograft (2:26) with a typical 3 vessel takeoff from the arch.  There is no
substantial calcification of the ascending aorta above the level of the
homograft or in the aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta. The pulmonary
arterial trunk is normal in caliber, but the right pulmonary artery is dilated
measuring 34 mm in diameter.

There is a small to moderate right pleural effusion and small left pleural
effusion, which are not hemorrhagic. There is associated mild compressive
atelectasis of the bilateral lower lobes. There is focal scarring and
bronchiectasis in the medial right middle lobe. Peribronchovascular
ground-glass opacities in the superior segment of the right lower lobe and
apical segment of the right upper lobe do not have the typical smooth
appearance of pulmonary edema.  Paraseptal emphysema is mild in the right
upper lobe.

The patient is status post median sternotomy without evidence of
complications. No blastic or lytic lesion suspicious for malignancy is
present.  Multilevel degenerative changes are moderate in the mid to lower
thoracic spine.

Although this study is not tailored for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
contents, the imaged upper abdomen shows no acute abnormality. There is mild
vascular calcification of the upper abdominal aorta.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Peribronchovascular ground-glass opacities in the right upper and lower
lobes are suspicious for
2.  Borderline enlarged right lower paratracheal lymph node is likely
reactive.
3.  Small to moderate right and small left pleural effusions.
4.  Dilation of the right pulmonary artery.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were discussed by Dr. ___ with NP ___
of cardiac surgery via telephone on ___ at 4:36 ___, 5 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with cough and wheeze, evaluate for
interstitial lung disease.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT acquired axial images of the chest were obtained without IV
contrast.  1.25-mm reconstructed thin section and coronally reformatted images
were also displayed.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  There is a 5-mm semisolid right upper
lobe pulmonary nodule (4:100) and a 4-mm peripheral left lower lobe nodule
(4:163).  There are no focal consolidations. There is a small area of
nonspecific, minimal bronchial wall thickening in the right middle lobe
(4:126). Minimal, nonspecific mild interstitial abnormality and dependent
ground glass opacity seen at the right and left lower lobes.

There are no pleural effusions.  There are no enlarged mediastinal, hilar,
axillary lymph nodes.  Heart and great vessels appear unremarkable, without
pericardial effusion.

Imaged portions of the upper abdomen appear unremarkable.

There are no bone findings of malignancy.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Nonspecific lung findings which could represent infectious or inflammatory
etiology, including reactive airways disease.

2.  5-mm right upper lobe and 4-mm left lower lobe pulmonary nodules. With a
history of smoking or malignancy, repeat imaging in six months recommended
(one year followup otherwise) to assess stability.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with history of PE, now presenting with left
sided back pain similar to prior PE.  Evaluate for pulmonary embolus.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 2.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 6.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 3.0
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 4.4 s, 34.6 cm; CTDIvol = 8.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 287.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 290 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: A pre-vascular mediastinal node measures 10 mm
in transverse dimension (series 2, image 43), but 4 mm cranial caudal..  No
axillary, supraclavicular, or hilar lymphadenopathy is present.  No
mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Small wedge-like parenchymal opacity in the right lower lobe is
sequelae of prior pulmonary infarct, decreased in size in the interim (series
2, image 83; series 602, image 12).  Tiny 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the left
lower lobe is unchanged and nonspecific (series 2, image 77).  Bibasilar
atelectasis is mild-to-moderate.  The airways are patent to the level of the
segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: The left thyroid lobe is unremarkable.  The patient appears to
have had prior right partial thyroidectomy.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen demonstrate a small hiatal
hernia, cholelithiasis, and an accessory and/or replaced left hepatic artery
off the left gastric artery (series 2, image 103).

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? no acute
fracture.  Focal coarse calcification in the right posterior breast is
unchanged.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2.  Interval decrease in right lower lobe parenchymal wedge-shaped opacity,
sequelae of prior pulmonary infarct.
3.  Unchanged nonspecific 2 mm left lower lobe pulmonary nodule.
4.  Small hiatal hernia.
5.  Cholelithiasis

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Shortness of breath, hypoxia.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT data were acquired through the chest after administration of
intravenous contrast.  Images were displayed in multiple planes.  Although the
patient is listed as having a contrast allergy, the patient was interviewed by
Dr ___ confirmed that the patient only experienced nausea after
drinking oral contrast.  The patient denied prior SOB, hives, itch, and throat
swelling.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen pelvis after ___

FINDINGS:

Chest CTA:  Opacification of the pulmonary arterial tree is adequate for the
exclusion of pulmonary embolus to the subsegmental level.  The left main
pulmonary artery is focally dilated to 2.6 cm.  The aorta and great vessels
are normal caliber appearance.

Chest CT:  Mild subpleural scarring is present at both lung apices.  There is
a diffuse pattern mosaic attenuation of the lung parenchyma.  The airways are
patent to the subsegmental level.  Several small peribronchial nodules are
visualized.  For example, there is a 3 mm nodule in the right upper lobe
(3:48) and a 6 mm nodule in the lingula (3:  71).  Mediastinal and hilar nodes
are mildly enlarged.  For example, a lower paratracheal node measures 12 cm in
short axis (2:27).  The heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial
effusion. 

There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic bone lesions.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No pulmonary embolism.

2.  Mild mediastinal adenopathy.  

3.  Mosaic attenuation of the pulmonary parenchyma is non-specific but may
related to pulmonary edema or small airways disease.

4.  Multiple peribronchial pulmonary nodules may relate to a inflammatory or
mild infectious process.  The largest 6 mm nodule may be followed up in ___
months depending on risk factors.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ with progressive shortness of breath.   Obtain
inspiratory and expiratory views and prone positioning.

TECHNIQUE:  Patient was scanned supine end inspiration and end expiration, and
in the prone position at full inspiration.  Combination of 5 mm and 1.25 mm
thick continuous and interrupted reconstructions were made from both series.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___. 

FINDINGS:

As compared to the scan on ___, lung volumes are much improved and
previously reported nodules have resolved.  There is still a generalized
heterogeneity in background density of the lung, and with expiration imaging
one sees relative retention of volume in the low density areas, indicating
small airways obstruction.  The only other focal abnormalities in the lungs
are too small regions of peribronchial infiltration at the periphery of the
upper lobes where there is the suggestion of mild traction bronchiectasis. 
This likely  mild fibrosis is not repeated elsewhere.  There is no
bronchiectasis or appreciable wall thickening in the bronchial tree.

Central lymph node enlargement is widespread but relatively mild, ranging in
diameter up to 12.5 mm in the right lower paratracheal station, 5:17,  smaller
in the upper paratracheal and prevascular stations, similar size in the
subcarinal.  Lymph nodes are borderline enlarged in both hila but there is no
bronchial impingement.

Main pulmonary diameter, 40 mm, is strongly associated with pulmonary arterial
hypertension.  Atherosclerotic calcification is restricted to the left
anterior descending coronary.  Distal esophagus is mildly distended with air
but the wall is not thickened.  The study is not designed for subdiaphragmatic
evaluation heterogeneity in the unenhanced liver is best explained by fatty
infiltration.

IMPRESSION:

1. Diffuse moderate to severe small airway obstruction, but no particular
bronchial wall thickening, mucoid impaction, bronchiectasis, or atelectasis. 
The explanation for small airway obstruction is not obvious radiographically. 

2. Minimal regional fibrosis, both upper lobes, there is not a generalized
process. 

3. Probable pulmonary arterial hypertension conceivably but not necessarily
that due to small airways obstruction.

4. Left anterior descending coronary atherosclerosis.

5. Fatty infiltration of the liver.

IMPRESSION:

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Right temporal lobe hemorrhage.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical MDCT images were obtained from the suprasternal
notch to the upper abdomen after the uneventful administration of IV contrast.
The abdomen and pelvis were subsequently scanned.  Coronal, sagittal and MIP
reformats were generated in an independent workstation.  

FINDINGS:

CT THORAX:  The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  There is no supraclavicular
lymphadenopathy.  There is no central or axillary lymphadenopathy.  The heart
and great vessels are within normal limits. The pulmonary arteries are not
dilated. There is no pericardial effusion.  The esophagus is within normal
limits without evidence of wall thickening or hiatal hernia.  

LUNG AND AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. There is
a mosaic pattern of attenuation of the lung parenchyma. A 3 mm nodule is
present in the right middle lobe (8:140).  No other focal opacities are
observed.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no lytic or blastic lesions concerning for
malignancy. 

For further details on infradiaphrapgmatic structures please refer to
concurrent abdomen/pelvis CT report.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Mosaic pattern of attenuation of the lung parenchyma is consistent with
air trapping most commonly seen in expiratory phase scans. Pulmonary
hypertension is unlikely given the normal caliber of the pulmonary arteries.

2.  No evidence of thoracic neoplasm. 3 mm nodule in the right middle lobe is
clinically irrelevant and below the threshold for recommended follow up as per
___ society guidelines.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ with with lung cancer on chemo p/w weakness and dyspnea, had
desaturdation to 85% on EMS arrivalNO_PO contrast  // eval for PE vs
dissection

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast in the arterial
phase.  Then, imaging was obtained through the abdomen and pelvis in the
portal venous phase. Reformatted coronal and sagittal images through the
chest, abdomen, and pelvis, and oblique maximal intensity projection images of
the chest were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 680 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___
CT abdomen pelvis ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. 
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within
normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM:  Bilateral prominent axillary lymph nodes are
unchanged in size compared to prior CT.  There is no mediastinal hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  A partially cavitated, heterogeneous and poorly enhancing
spiculated left upper lobe lesion is similar in size to CT from ___ measuring approximately 2.2 x 3.1 cm, however measuring 1.7 x 2.0 cm in
___.  There is centrilobular emphysema.   The airways are patent to the
level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic
or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation. There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: Since prior CT in ___ there are unchanged bilateral
heterogeneously hypoenhancing adrenal metastasis measuring 6.2 x 5.3 cm on the
right and 5.5 x 5.5 cm on the left (previously measuring 2.5 x 1.8 cm and 2.4
x 1.7 cm, respectively, in ___.  The right adrenal mass has mild
mass effect on the IVC which remains patent (2b:135).

URINARY: Several well-circumscribed hypodensities in bilateral renal cortices
are compatible with simple renal cysts.  Smaller hypodensities elsewhere in
the renal cortices are too small to characterize but likely represent renal
cyst.  Otherwise, the kidneys enhance normally and symmetrically.  There is no
evidence of hydronephrosis. There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  Diffuse
diverticulosis of the colon, particularly the sigmoid colon is noted, without
evidence of wall thickening and fat stranding.  The appendix is normal.  There
is no free intraperitoneal fluid or free air.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate and seminal vesicles are unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is more conspicuous retroperitoneal lymph nodes compared to
priors with the, including at least 3 centrally necrotic lymph nodes noted the
located between the aorta and pancreas (2b:143, 139, 138).  No mesenteric
lymphadenopathy.  There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  A left soft tissue nodule deep to the left gluteus
musculature is slightly enlarged compared to prior CT, now measuring 1.3 x 1.0
cm, previously measuring 1.0 x 0.8 cm.  The bony structures are essentially
unchanged from prior CT, including healing pathologic left ninth, tenth and
right eighth rib fractures.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2. Interval worsening and enlargement of retroperitoneal lymph nodes,
specifically with development of at least 3 centrally necrotic lymph nodes
along the posterior aspect of the pancreas.
3. Slight interval increase in size of left gluteal soft tissue nodule since ___.
4. Bilateral adrenal metastatic lesions are unchanged in size from ___ but significantly larger than ___.
5. Unchanged left upper lobe pulmonary mass.

x 1.7 cm, respectively, in ___.  The right adrenal mass has mild
mass effect on the IVC which remains patent (2b:135).

URINARY: Several well-circumscribed hypodensities in bilateral renal cortices
are compatible with simple renal cysts.  Smaller hypodensities elsewhere in
the renal cortices are too small to characterize but likely represent renal
cyst.  Otherwise, the kidneys enhance normally and symmetrically.  There is no
evidence of hydronephrosis. There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  Diffuse
diverticulosis of the colon, particularly the sigmoid colon is noted, without
evidence of wall thickening and fat stranding.  The appendix is normal.  There
is no free intraperitoneal fluid or free air.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate and seminal vesicles are unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is more conspicuous retroperitoneal lymph nodes compared to
priors with the, including at least 3 centrally necrotic lymph nodes noted the
located between the aorta and pancreas (2b:143, 139, 138).  No mesenteric
lymphadenopathy.  There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  A left soft tissue nodule deep to the left gluteus
musculature is slightly enlarged compared to prior CT, now measuring 1.3 x 1.0
cm, previously measuring 1.0 x 0.8 cm.  The bony structures are essentially
unchanged from prior CT, including healing pathologic left ninth, tenth and
right eighth rib fractures.

2. Interval worsening and enlargement of retroperitoneal lymph nodes,
specifically with development of at least 3 centrally necrotic lymph nodes
along the posterior aspect of the pancreas.
3. Slight interval increase in size of left gluteal soft tissue nodule since ___.
4. Bilateral adrenal metastatic lesions are unchanged in size from ___ but significantly larger than ___.
5. Unchanged left upper lobe pulmonary mass.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with glioblastoma, presented with
tachycardia/hypoxia and found to have PEs, now with worsening hypoxemia
despite anticoagulation  // Looking for any new parenchymal lung disease which
might explain persistent/worsening hypoxemia

TECHNIQUE:  MD CT axial slices of the chest were obtained without the
administration intravenous contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, axial MIP
reformations were obtained and reviewed on PACS

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.6 s, 35.2 cm; CTDIvol = 10.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 358.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 358 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: There are no suspicious thyroid
lesions.  There is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy.  There are
no suspicious chest wall lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The imaged upper abdominal structures are unremarkable.

MEDIASTINUM: There are multiple subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes
measuring up to 7 mm in the right lower paratracheal station, not
pathologically enlarged based on CT size criteria (5:103).  There is no
mediastinal mass.  The esophagus is unremarkable.

HILA: There are multiple subcentimeter hilar lymph nodes bilaterally, not
pathologically enlarged.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  There are no
coronary artery calcifications.  The heart and pericardium are within normal
limits.  There is no pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: There is interval development of peribronchovascular
ground-glass opacities in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe, new
from prior study (5:85).  A more focal consolidative opacity in the
paramediastinal right upper lobe may represent focal atelectasis or a
developing pneumonia (3:21).  There are multiple bilateral calcified
granulomas measuring 3 mm in the right middle lobe and 6 mm in the left lower
lobe.  There is a single 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe
(5:146).
2. AIRWAYS:  There is slight interval increase in moderate right lower lobe
and left basilar atelectasis.
3. VESSELS:  The previously noted bilateral segmental and subsegmental
pulmonary emboli are better evaluated on prior CTA chest dated ___.

CHEST CAGE: There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:


1. Peribronchovascular ground-glass opacities in the right upper lobe are
nonspecific, due to either early pneumonia or, in the setting of
anticoagulation, focal pulmonary hemorrhage.
2. Slight interval increase in the extent of moderate bilateral lower lobe
atelectasis.
3. 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe.  Attention on follow-up
imaging is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
REASON FOR EXAM:  Pulmonary nodules incidentally seen on prior shoulder CT.

COMPARISON:  Shoulder CT from ___.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT images of the chest with 5-mm and 1.25-mm axial
slices and coronal and sagittal reformats were submitted for interpretation. 
No IV contrast was administered.  

FINDINGS: The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.  Minimal soft tissue in the
anterior mediastinum likely represents residual thymus. There is no
pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy by size criteria,
evaluation is limited due to lack of IV contrast.  Heart is not enlarged. 
There is no pericardial effusion. Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications of
the aorta is noted.

Multiple pulmonary nodules are identified:  4-mm nodule in the left upper lobe
(4:49).  sub-mm bronchiolar-subpleural nodules in the right upper lobe (4:62).
1-mm inferior right upper lobe nodule (4:62).  3-mm lingular nodule (4:103). 
4-mm right lower lobe nodule with spiculation (4:117).  Ill-defined opacity at
the base of the lingula likely represents scarring. There is no pneumothorax,
pleural effusion, or focal pulmonary consolidation.  Airways are patent to the
level of subsegmental branches.  There is bilateral mild apical
thickening-scarring.  Subtle emphysema in bilateral upper lobes are mild.  

Upper abdominal organs are partially imaged and appear grossly unremarkable.

No osseous lesion to suggest malignancy or infection is identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Multiple pulmonary nodules as described above, with the largest measuring 4
mm with spiculation in the right lower lobe.  Chest CT in ___ year is
recommended for further evaluation.
2.. Mild bi-apical emphysema.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic pancreatic cancer  // restaging
-- starting new chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:   Acquisition sequence:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Spiral Acquisition 4.9 s, 38.5 cm; CTDIvol = 8.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 340.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 340 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal.  A right-sided Port-A-Cath terminates at the lower SVC.
Multiple measurable mediastinal lymph nodes are smaller from prior examination
and none is pathologically enlarged.  The largest 0.8 cm in short axis at the
subcarinal station (02:30) was previously 1.4 cm.  There are no pathologically
enlarged supraclavicular, axillary, or hilar lymph nodes.

The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in size.  No incidental pulmonary
embolism.  The heart is normal in size and demonstrates mild aortic valvular
and moderate coronary artery calcifications. There is no pericardial effusion.

No pneumothorax or pleural effusion is identified. The airways are patent to
the subsegmental level.  As compared to the prior examination, there has been
interval waxing and waning of numerous bilateral pulmonary nodules all of
which measure less than 5 mm in size (4:  40, 49, 100, 115, 129, 161, 165). 
No new dominant suspicious pulmonary nodule or mass is identified.

Additionally, there has been interval resolution of numerous previously seen
ground-glass opacities and centrilobular micronodules.  However, a new cluster
of nodules in the left upper lobe are noted, and likely infectious in nature.

No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.  A sclerotic focus within the
lateral aspect of the right sixth rib is unchanged.

For description of the intra-abdominal contents, please see the separate CT
abdomen and pelvis examination performed on the same day.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Numerous, small, pulmonary nodules continue to demonstrate waxing and
waning characteristics as compared to the prior examination.  No new nodules
concerning for metastatic disease.  Recommend continued attention at followup.
2.  No pathologic intrathoracic or extrathoracic lymphadenopathy.  A
previously enlarged subcarinal lymph node is now within normal limits by size
criteria.
3.  Previously seen scattered ground-glass opacities and centrilobular
micronodules are no longer identified, likely representing interval resolution
of a previous viral infectious process.
4.  For description of the intra-abdominal contents, please see the separate
CT abdomen and pelvis examination performed on the same day.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic pancreatic cancer  // assess for
disease response/progression

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.0 s, 39.1 cm; CTDIvol = 8.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 348.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 349 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are unremarkable.  Heart size is enlarged with LV
enlargement.
Coronary calcifications are extensive.  There is no pericardial pleural
effusion.

Image portion of the upper abdomen will be reviewed separately is part of the
MRI of the abdomen and corresponding report will be issued

Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.  New irregular
nodule in the right upper lobe series 4, image 77 measuring 13 x 7 mm. 
Previously seen right lower lobe nodule has increased now measuring 6 x 7 mm
previously 3 mm.  Subpleural triangular nodule in the left upper lobe series
4, image 64 is stable.  Subpleural nodule in the right lower lobe is partially
obscured by atelectasis series 4, image 90 and grossly stable.

Port-A-Cath catheter appears unremarkable with its tip at the level of low
SVC.

No lytic or sclerotic lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm
demonstrated.

IMPRESSION: 

New irregular nodule in the right upper lobe and interval increase of the
right lower lobe nodules concerning for progressive metastatic disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with pleural thickening on CXR  // eval for
pleural thickening      eval for pleural thickening

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity

radiology_facts.report_text:
STUDY:  CT of the chest and abdomen.

INDICATION:  ___ male with HCC, pre-liver transplantation.  Evaluate
for focal liver lesions and metastasis to the lungs.

COMPARISON:  CT chest and abdomen dated ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT images of the chest and abdomen were performed before and
after administration of intravenous contrast with the images of the liver
obtained in multiple phases.

Multiplanar reconstructions were performed and reviewed. Due to the patient's
h/o stroke the arms had to be placed across the abdomen and streak artifacts
from the patient's arms, limit evaluation of the liver.


DOSE REPORT:  Total exam DLP:  ___ mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  The thyroid gland appears within normal limits.  There is no axillary,
mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The cardiac chambers appear within
normal limits.  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  There is
atherosclerotic vascular disease of the thoracic aorta and its branches as
well as the coronary arteries.  Stable bullous changes in the right upper
lobe.  There is a tiny 2-mm nodule in the left upper lobe (3b, 122) new since
the previous study, nonspecific.  Mild centrilobular pulmonary emphysematous
changes are identified.

There is no pleural effusion.  The tracheobronchial tree appears normal.

ABDOMEN:  The liver is dysmorphic, consistent with the patient's history of
cirrhosis.  Again noted are stable post-radiofrequency ablation changes
involving the dome of the liver.  There are no suspicious regions of
enhancement at this site to suggest local recurrence.  The previously
identified focus of enhancement anteior to RF ablation site is no longer seen.
No additional regions of hyperenhancement or washout is seen to suggest a new
focus of ___.  Note is again made of multiple calcified gallstones.  There is
no intra- or extra-hepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  Portal vein is patent.

The spleen is mildly enlarged measuring about 13.5 cm in size.  The pancreas,
adrenal glands and kidneys are normal.

Prominent venous collaterals and splenorenal shunting are seen, stable.

The abdominal aorta demonstrates atherosclerotic vascular disease without
evidence of ectasia or aneurysm.  There is evidence of gynecomastia.

Evaluation of bowel is limited due to lack of enteric contrast, however, no
obvious pathology is seen.

A calcified pleural plaque is again seen in the right anterior hemithorax.

Multilevel degenerative changes are seen in the spine without evidence of
osteolytic or osteoblastic lesions.

The previously described enhancing focus anterior to the ablation zone in the
dome of the liver is not as well evaluated.  

IMPRESSION:
No evidence of recurrence at the site of previous radiofrequency ablation.  No
new lesions.

Evidence of cirrhosis and portal hypertension.

Cholelithiasis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ man with history of HCV, cirrhosis and HCC, please
assess for lesions in the chest.  Please assess for focal lesions.

TECHNIQUE:  64-row MDCT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was obtained with IV
contrast only.  Entire Torso was scanned during the portal venous phase.
Abdomen was additionally scanned during arterial and delayed phases as per
multiphasic liver protocol.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were generated.

DLP:  2115 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  ___ and ___.

FINDINGS:

CT of the chest with IV contrast:  
A 9 mm hypodensity within the right lobe the thyroid gland is unchanged and
likely represents a nodule.  There is no supraclavicular, axillary,
mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  Trachea is midline and airways are
open to subsegmental level.  Right and left lungs show mild dependent
atelectasis but there is no evidence of focal nodules, or consolidation.  The
2 mm nodule seen within the left upper lobe on the previous study is no longer
clearly visualized.  There is no pleural effusion. Right anterior upper lobe
calcified pleural plaques are unchanged. Heart is normal size and there is no
pericardial effusion.  The great vessels are within normal limits.

CT of the abdomen with IV contrast:
Liver contour is nodular consistent with a history of cirrhosis. Again, a
stable hypodensity is seen within the dome of the liver reflecting post
treatment changes. There is no intra or extrahepatic biliary dilatation. 
Within the gallbladder multiple small faceted stones are seen without
gallbladder distension or wall thickening.  Spleen is normal size and
homogeneous in enhancement.  Pancreas enhances homogeneously with no ductal
dilatation, focal lesion, or peripancreatic stranding.  Adrenal glands are
within normal limits. Left and right kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete
contrast briskly with no focal cystic or solid lesions and no hydronephrosis.

Stomach, duodenum, and loops of small bowel are decompressed.  There is no
wall thickening or signs of obstruction.  Visualized segments of the colon are
normal.

Extensive calcifications are seen along the distal abdominal aorta and
proximal common iliacs with no aneurysmal dilatation.  Celiac axis, SMA,
portal vein, SMV, and splenic vein are patent. A splenorenal shunt is seen on
the left. Multiple varices are seen.   

There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.  There is no free
fluid within the abdomen.  

Skeletal:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.


IMPRESSION:
1. Stable hypodense lesion at the dome of the liver from prior ablation.  No
evidence of recurrence and no new suspicious lesions are identified. 

2. No lung nodules are seen.

3. Nodular liver consistent with a history of cirrhosis. There is no
splenomegaly or ascites. Multiple varices and a splenorenal shunt are again
noted, unchanged compared to the previous study.

4. Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis.

DLP:  2115 mGy-cm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with hx left retroperitoneal sarcoma status
post colonic/pelvic liposarc resected ___// 6 month f/u, assess for
recurrence

TECHNIQUE:  Axial images of the chest were obtained after administration of IV
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 7.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 36.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
18.1 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.6 s, 67.6 cm; CTDIvol = 17.4 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,173.1 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 3.5 s, 27.6 cm; CTDIvol = 17.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 471.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,663 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  ___ chest CT

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Imaged thyroid gland is notable for
stable hypoattenuating subcentimeter left thyroid nodules which require no
further follow-up.  Surgical clips in the right axilla and denote prior lymph
node dissection.  There is no axillary adenopathy.  Scattered right breast
calcifications; evaluation of the breasts are reserved for mammogram.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see same-day CT abdomen and pelvis for detailed
abdominopelvic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: No mediastinal adenopathy.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart remains severely enlarged with multi chamber
enlargement.  There is no pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Middle lobe scarring/chronic atelectasis is unchanged (3:160).
Subsegmental compressive atelectasis is noted in the dependent lung bases,
more so on the left than the right.  There are scattered small calcified
granulomas noted bilaterally.
2. AIRWAYS:  Patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.
3. VESSELS:  The aorta is normal in caliber.  The main pulmonary artery is
dilated, measuring 3.5 cm, suggestive of chronic pulmonary hypertension.
CHEST CAGE: Lumbar spine posterior fusion hardware is incompletely visualized
and is better assessed in the same day CT abdomen and pelvis.  Hyperdense
material within the left aspect of T12 and the included L1 vertebral bodies
(3:223) are noted.  No aggressive thoracic osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.
2. Severe cardiomegaly for which evaluation with echocardiogram is recommended
if not previously evaluated.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with hx of left colonic/pelvic liposarc
resected ___.  Evaluation for interval change, metastatic disease.

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Found no primary dose record and no dose record stored with the sibling
of a split exam.
!If this Fluency report was activated before the completion of the dose
transmission, please reinsert the token called CT DLP Dose to load new data.

COMPARISON:  Comparison to CT chest with contrast from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The imaged thyroid gland is again
notable for stable hypoattenuating subcentimeter left thyroid nodules, which
are too small to warrant further imaging follow-up.  Supraclavicular and
axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.  Surgical clips in the right axilla are
consistent with prior lymph node dissection.  Few scattered right breast
calcifications are similar in appearance to prior study, and better assessed
on dedicated mammography.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the separately dictated CT abdomen and pelvis report
performed concurrently for description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HILA: Hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart size remains severely enlarged with multichamber
enlargement.  Mitral valve prosthesis is noted.  No pericardial effusion.  The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.

PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: Right middle lobe scarring/chronic atelectasis is unchanged
(3:171).  Minimal dependent atelectasis is again noted.  Scattered small
calcified granulomas are seen bilaterally.  No new suspicious pulmonary
nodules or masses identified.
2. AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.
3. VESSELS:  The main pulmonary artery is mildly dilated measuring up to 3.3
cm, which can be seen in the setting of pulmonary arterial hypertension. 
While this study is not optimized for the detection of pulmonary emboli, no
central filling defect is seen.
CHEST CAGE: No acute fracture or suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion
is detected.  Lumbar fusion hardware is again seen, better assessed on CT
abdomen/pelvis.  Hyperdense material within the T12 and L1 vertebral bodies,
unchanged.  Median sternotomy wires are again noted.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.
2. Chronic scarring/atelectasis in the right middle lobe, unchanged.
3. Stable severe cardiomegaly.
4. For complete description of subdiaphragmatic findings, please see dedicated
report of CT abdomen/pelvis performed concurrently the same day.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of right breast cancer and hx
intra-abd liposarcoma resected ___ with L colectomy, ___ with sigmoid
colectomy, ___ lesser sac recurrence  // new baseline, f/u for patient with
long hx recurrent gr 1 liposarcoma, most recent resection ___ from lesser
sac

TECHNIQUE:   Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 24.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.0 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.8 s, 69.6 cm; CTDIvol = 16.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,117.9 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 3.3 s, 26.1 cm; CTDIvol = 13.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 357.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,488 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___ and ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: There is a stable 1.1 cm left lower
pole thyroid nodule (2; 7).  There is no supraclavicular or axillary
lymphadenopathy.  Surgical changes in the right axilla from prior lymph node
dissection is similar to prior.  Postsurgical changes are seen in the right
breast, better assessed with dedicated mammography.  Redemonstration of
prominent venous structure in the right anterior neck (3; 9).

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see separate report for CT abdomen and pelvis performed
on same day for description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There is moderate cardiomegaly with biatrial
enlargement.  There is no pericardial effusion.  Mitral annuloplasty is noted.
PLEURA: There is a new small right pleural effusion.  No left pleural
effusion.  There is no pneumothorax.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Stable 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the left upper lobe is noted
(3; 42).  Calcified granuloma in bilateral lower lobes are stable.  Chronic
atelectasis or scarring in the right middle lobe is stable.  No new or growing
pulmonary nodules.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.
3. VESSELS:  The main pulmonary artery is mildly prominent measuring 3.4 cm in
caliber (2; 30).  The aorta is normal in caliber.  No central pulmonary
embolism is noted.

CHEST CAGE: No suspicious osseous lesion is identified.  Suture anchors are
noted in the right humeral head.  Postsurgical changes are partially
visualized in the lower thoracic spine.  Patient is status post median
sternotomy.

IMPRESSION:


1. New small right pleural effusion.
2. Stable 2 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule.  No new or growing pulmonary
nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Prostate cancer.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent  reconstructed as
axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Reported in the concurrent abdomen CT

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal and hilar lymph
nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there are mild coronary calcifications in the LAD.
Multiple less than 3 mm lung nodules are as follows (6: 100, 105, 106, 131,
138, 139, 145, 157, 160, 181, 192, 193, 196), there is a 6 mm nodule in the
right lower lobe (6, 211)
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.
Please refer to the concurrent CT abdomen for complete description of the
intra-abdominal findings
There are no osseous blastic lesion.

IMPRESSION: 

Multiple unspecific lung nodules, largest in the right lower lobe, followup in
3 months is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with prior chest ct scan that showed nodules;
please compare to prior exam and assess stability as recommended by radiology 
// Interval change

TECHNIQUE:   Multidetector scanning of the chest was performed and
reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal
and sagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images. No IV Contrast administered.
DOSAGE:   Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 7.0 s, 37.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 364.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 365 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT ___ dating back to ___.

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Thyroid is surgically absent. 
There is no axillary or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.  The superficial soft
tissues are normal.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Subcentimeter hypodensities in the left lobe of the liver are
too small to characterize but likely represent simple hepatic cysts.A tiny
right adrenal nodule is consistent with an adenoma or myelolipoma. The abdomen
is otherwise normal.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There is no pericardial effusion.  Aortic annular and
coronary calcifications are moderate.
PLEURA: There is no pleural abnormality or pleural effusion.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Numerous pulmonary nodules in the right lower lobe (series 5,
image 139, 148, 160, 203, 222) measure up to 5 mm.  There is mild scarring at
the lung bases, bilaterally.  A pulmonary nodule at the left lung base (series
5, image 192) is unchanged.  A 3 mm pulmonary nodule in the superior segment
of the left lower lobe (series 5, image 128) is unchanged.  A 3 mm pulmonary
nodule in the left upper lobe (series 5, image 99) is unchanged.  There are no
new or enlarging pulmonary nodules.
-AIRWAYS:  Narrowing of the coronal diameter of the upper trachea is mild,
usually seen in patients with chronic lung disease.  The airways are patent to
the subsegmental level.  The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in
size.
CHEST CAGE: There are no concerning osseous lesions for malignancy or
infection.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Multiple pulmonary nodules, unchanged from ___.
2.  Tiny right adrenal nodule is consistent with an adenoma or myelolipoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:
  Patient with history of prostate cancer evaluate for metastasis

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT of the chest was performed after the
administration of intravenous contrast.  Axial coronal and sagittal
reconstructions were acquired.  Maximum intensity projections were also
acquired

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.4 cm; CTDIvol = 5.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 2.3
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 32.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 560.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
112.0 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 10.4 s, 67.4 cm; CTDIvol = 16.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,085.2 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,200 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:
To prior study done on ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: The thyroid is unremarkable. There are no enlarged
supraclavicular lymph nodes.  Note is made of saber sheath appearance of the
trachea.

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes.

MEDIASTINUM: There are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  Heart size
is normal.  The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.  There is
moderate coronary artery calcification.  There is no pericardial effusion.


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion

LUNG: All the previously visualized pulmonary nodules are unchanged in size
and density.  (6, 89, 131, 135, 193, 195) no new pulmonary nodules.  Minimal
bibasilar atelectasis

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones is unremarkable.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen shows mild bilateral
adrenal thickening.  Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has
been dictated separately.

IMPRESSION:



RECOMMENDATION(S):  The all the previously visualized pulmonary nodules are
unchanged in size and density and the range in size from 1-2 mm.  No new
pulmonary nodules.

Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has been dictated
separately.

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at
the level of the hilar structures.  No abnormalities at the level of the large
mediastinal vessels.  No incidental pulmonary embolism.  Moderate coronary
calcifications.  Mild aortic valve calcifications.  No pericardial effusion. 
The posterior mediastinum is unremarkable.  The upper abdomen is reported in
detail in the dedicated abdominal CT report.  No osteolytic lesions at the
level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral bodies.  Stable mild to
moderate degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression fractures.
Stable severe she trachea.  Respiratory motion limits the assessment of the
lung parenchyma.  Stable scattered pulmonary micro nodules but no new or
growing nodules suspicious for metastatic lesions.  Bilateral areas of basilar
atelectasis.  No pleural thickening, no pleural effusions.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable examination of the thorax.  No evidence of metastatic disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:
CT torso with contrast

INDICATION:  History: ___ with fall 3 days ago onto right lateral flank/thorax
and right buttocks/hip with continued pain.  // ? pelvic fx/hip fx or right
renal contusion

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 8.5 s, 66.9 cm; CTDIvol = 12.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 811.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 811 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis ___.
Chest CT ___.

FINDINGS: 

VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of acute
injury.  The infrarenal abdominal aorta is aneurysmal measuring up to 3.5 cm
and contains an intramural thrombus.

CHEST:
HEART:  No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM:  A 1.0 cm right axillary lymph node is noted. 
A 1.2 cm right lower paratracheal lymph node is noted.  There is no hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES:  A 1.1 cm calcification along the second right anterolateral
rib likely represents a pleural calcification, unchanged since ___. 
No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  Parenchymal opacification along the posterior left lower lobe
may represent aspiration versus pulmonary contusion versus pneumonia.  5 mm
nodule is seen in the left upper lobe (2:69).  Nodular opacity is seen along
the periphery of the left major fissure (2:89).  Multiple scattered
ill-defined opacities are seen along the left upper and lower lobes (2:89,
02:103) concerning for infection.  The airways are patent to the level of the
segmental bronchi bilaterally.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
Subcentimeter hypodensities scattered throughout the liver are too small to
characterize.  There is no evidence of laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
A 6.5 cm exophytic simple cyst is seen in the lower pole of the left kidney. 
Multiple subcentimeter hypodensities in bilateral kidneys are too small to
characterize, likely simple cysts.  No hydronephrosis is seen bilaterally. 
There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: A large hiatal hernia is noted.  Small bowel loops
demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The
colon and rectum are within normal limits.  The appendix is not visualized. 
There is no evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.  Multiple bilateral inguinal lymph
nodes measure up to 1.5 cm.

BONES: L1 through L3 acute right transverse process fractures noted.  A left
lateral 9th rib fracture is subacute to chronic.

SOFT TISSUES: Postsurgical changes from prior inguinal hernia repairs. 
Partially imaged small left hydrocele.

IMPRESSION:


1. Consolidation in the posterior left lower lobe may represent aspiration
versus pulmonary contusion versus pneumonia.
2. Acute right L1 through L3 transverse process fractures.
3. A left lateral 9th rib fracture is subacute to chronic.
4. 5 mm nodule is seen in the left upper lobe (2:69). Nodular opacity is seen
along the periphery of the left major fissure (2:89) and multiple scattered
ill-defined opacities are seen along the left upper and lower lobes (2:89,
02:103) are nonspecific, but may represent infection.  Recommend follow-up
chest CT in 3 months.
5. Right axillary, mediastinal, and bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy,
increased in size compared to the prior study.
6. Infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm, measuring up to 3.5 cm, contains an
intramural thrombus, unchanged compared to the prior study.
7. Large hiatal hernia.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Chest CT in 3 months.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were emailed to ED QA nurse by ___,
M.D. on ___ at 9:42 ___, 5 minutes after discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with abnormal chest CT scan. Told to have repeat
in 3 months// ? changes to nodules?

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.5 s, 34.6 cm; CTDIvol = 11.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 379.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 380 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Compared to chest CT scanning since ___, most recently ___.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged and there are no
soft tissue abnormalities in the chest wall suspicious for malignancy.  This
study is not appropriate for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis but shows no adrenal
mass or suspect findings in the upper abdomen.

There are no thyroid findings warranting further imaging evaluation. 
Atherosclerotic calcification is mild to moderate in head and neck vessels,
severe in the coronary arteries, particularly left anterior descending.  Small
aneurysm of the left coronary sinus is unchanged since at least ___.  Aortic valvular calcification is moderately severe.  Ascending thoracic
aorta is mildly enlarged, 41 mm, unchanged.  Pulmonary arteries are enlarged,
main 34 mm, right 32 mm previously 33 mm and 31 mm, respectively.Esophagus
above the stable moderate size hiatus hernia is unremarkable.

There is no pericardial effusion. Small nonhemorrhagic left pleural effusion
is new. Right pleural calcification is an isolated finding, not an indication
of previous asbestos exposure.

Lymph nodes:

15 mm right lower paratracheal lymph nodes, 4:97, were 17 mm in ___.

Partially calcified 11 mm lymph node at the anterior to left upper lobe
bronchus in the left hilus is unchanged since ___.  It is not
responsible for bronchial narrowing.  Lymph nodes elsewhere in the chest are
not pathologically enlarged.


Lungs:

New regions of consolidation and ground-glass opacification in the anterior
segment of the left upper lobe and ground-glass opacification in the lingula
is probably pneumonia, chronicity indeterminate.

Previous left lower lobe pneumonia has cleared.  Combination of bibasilar
ground-glass opacification at the lung bases and mild bronchiectasis suggests
aspiration.

There are no lung lesions of concern for malignancy.

There are no bone lesions in the chest cage suspicious for malignancy or
infection.  There is no compression or pathologic fracture or destructive bone
lesion.


IMPRESSION: 

No lung nodules concerning for malignancy.

New pneumonia, left upper lobe, stage of activity is indeterminate.  In the
setting of prior left lower lobe pneumonia and bibasilar findings suggesting
chronic aspiration, this could be aspiration pneumonia, also attributable to
hiatus hernia and reflux.

Coronary atherosclerosis.  Moderate aortic valvular calcification.  Small
chronic left sinus of Valsalva aneurysm.  Probable pulmonary arterial
hypertension.

Granulomatous calcifications, left upper lobe and left hilar lymph node.  No
evidence of active granulomatous infection.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST AND CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with respiratory failure and shock of unclear
etiology// please evaluate for pulmonary embolism

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.8 s, 37.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 259.3
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 3.7 s, 49.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 385.4
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   4) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 648 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None available.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta or cardiac
valves.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout. 
No aortic dissection, aneurysmal dilation of or penetrating atherosclerotic
ulcers.  No filling defects in the main pulmonary artery throughout its
subsegmental branches bilaterally.  No signs of right heart strain.

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is unremarkable.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall.  Mild atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
Enteric tube passing through the esophagus which is otherwise unremarkable. 
Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none pathologically enlarged by CT size
criteria. Borderline No left hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
No pleural effusions.  No apical scarring bilaterally.

LUNGS:
The patient is intubated with an appropriately placed ETT.  Collapse of the
right main bronchus during expiration.  The remaining airways are patent to
the subsegmental levels.  Moderate diffuse bronchial wall thickening is noted
in the right lower lobe associated to postobstructive atelectasis.  Background
of severe centrilobular pulmonary emphysema.  3 mm nodule in the lingula
(301:80).  No suspicious nodules or masses.

CHEST CAGE:
No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show no significant abnormal
findings.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
There is suggestion of right bronchomalacia in the current CT scan with
complete collapse of the right main bronchus.  Additionally bronchial wall
inflammation is noted in both lower lobes with a new area of mucoid plugging
to the right associated to small postobstructive atelectasis.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 11:12 am.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST; CT ABD AND PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with COPD and HTN, presenting with aphasia and a
new hemorrhagic lesion identified on OSH MRI, with altered mental status and
concern for expanding bleed/increased ICP.// Any evidence of malignancy,
disseminated disease to be correlated with lesion in brain?

TECHNIQUE:  Single phase contrast: MDCT axial images were acquired through the
chest, abdomen and pelvis following intravenous contrast administration.
Oral contrast was administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.  MIPS
reformats of the chest were reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.3 s, 70.2 cm; CTDIvol = 12.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 893.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 3.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 16.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
8.4 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 903 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST); Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.3 s, 70.2 cm; CTDIvol = 12.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 893.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 3.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 16.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
8.4 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 903 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis from ___
Chest radiograph from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
There are no thyroid findings that warrant further imaging. No enlarged lymph
nodes in either axilla or thoracic inlet. There are no abnormalities on the
chest wall. Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications in the head and neck
arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape. No pericardial effusion. Mild
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries and aorta. The aorta
and pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus contains an enteric tube but is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal
lymph nodes, none pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria, and the largest
being a 0.8 cm right lower paratracheal node (series 302:106).  No hilar
lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA & LUNGS
There is a 2.8 x 3.3 cm intermediate density pleural based lesion at the right
lung base (series 2:50) which appears distinct from the right hemidiaphragm
(series 2:50 and 601:16).  This was not previously seen on CT abdomen pelvis
from ___.  No pleural effusions.

Evaluation of the small airways is limited by motion, but there is
peribronchial wall thickening and mucous plugging in the right lower lobe and
mild scattered mucous plugging throughout the lungs.  Multiple ill-defined
nodules in the right lower lobe are possibly infectious or inflammatory, for
example, a 6 mm pulmonary nodule (series 302:152, a 4 mm and 5 mm pulmonary
nodule (series 302:143) and a 4 mm nodule (series 302:134).  There is a 3 mm
nodule in the right upper lobe (302:115) and a 2 mm nodule in the left upper
lobe (302:46).

Mild to moderate centrilobular emphysema.

CHEST CAGE:
No acute fractures.  There is a 16 mm irregular lytic lesion with surrounding
sclerosis in the right scapula (2:5).

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is a 1.5 x 1.0  cm hypoattenuating lesion in segment 2, not
significantly changed from CT abdomen and pelvis on ___ allowing for
differences in technique.  This is incompletely characterized but may possibly
represent a hepatic cyst.  No additional liver lesions seen.  There is no
evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder
is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of solid renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The previous gastrostomy tube has been removed.  There is a
enteric tube with the tip in the gastric body.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout. Evaluation of the
rectum is motion limited, however within this limitation there still appears
to be some asymmetric thickening of the right side of the anus and lower
rectum (series 2: 123). The appendix is not seen but there are no secondary
signs of acute appendicitis.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate is enlarged.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Mild atherosclerotic disease
is noted.

BONES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture.

SOFT TISSUES: Areas of soft tissue density in the right anterior abdominal
wall are probably related to prior subcutaneous injections.  Otherwise, the
abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. A 3.3 cm pleural-based lesion at the right lung base, new from ___
and suspicious for malignancy.
2. Asymmetric thickening of the right side of the anus and lower rectal wall. 
Recommend direct visualization with possible anoscopy to exclude an underlying
mass.
3. A 16 mm lytic lesion in the right scapula with sclerotic margins, which is
incompletely characterized, but may represent a metastasis.
4. Multiple ill-defined nodules measuring up to 6 mm in the right lower lobe,
which are favored to be infectious or inflammatory, though metastatic lesions
cannot be entirely excluded.  Additional small nodules in the bilateral upper
lobes.  Attention on follow-up imaging is recommended.
5. Prostatomegaly.
