radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with retrosternal hematoma on MR but unknown sternal
fracture.  Evaluate for sternal fracture.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.9 s, 38.5 cm; CTDIvol = 22.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 873.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 873 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE: The imaged thyroid is unremarkable. 
Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged by CT size criteria.

MEDIASTINUM: Mediastinal lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.

Density in the retrosternal region likely corresponds to the provided history
of retrosternal hematoma.  This measures approximately 7.1 x 1.9 cm
(transverse, AP) (series 4, image 44, series 602, image 81).  A small
calcification within the hematoma (4:41) likely represents a donated bone
fragment from the known fractures.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in size.
Heart size is normal with no significant coronary artery calcifications. Trace
pericardial effusion is likely physiologic.

PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. There is no
focal consolidation. Bibasilar atelectasis is mild.  Incidental note is made
of a 2 mm pulmonary nodule in the right middle lobe (4:113).  Another 1 mm
nodule is identified in the right upper lobe (4:98).

UPPER ABDOMEN: This study is not tailored to evaluate subdiaphragmatic
structures. Allowing for this, there is a small accessory spleen.

CHEST CAGE/BONES: Multiple displaced fractures of the posterolateral right
ribs are identified, including the right first through fourth ribs (4:11, 27,
54, 66).  Minimally displaced fracture of the right fifth rib (4:103).  There
is a minimally displaced fracture at the junction of the left aspect of the
manubrium with the ossified left first rib costal cartilage (4:48), with
approximately 3-4 mm of displacement.  There is a minimally displaced fracture
of the right first rib costal cartilage at the costochondral junction (4:46). 
Notably, there appears to be posterior subluxation of the right
sternoclavicular joint (4:30).  There is also small nondisplaced fracture of
the left second rib costal cartilage (4:63).  Deformities of the anterior
right second through fourth ribs and left third and fourth ribs may be
chronic.

Incidental note is made of a chronic injury of the right distal clavicular
region with ossification in the region of the coracoclavicular ligament, as
well as a right os acromiale.

There is no evidence of thoracic spinal fracture.  Multilevel degenerative
changes of the thoracic spine are moderate in severity, with anterior bridging
osteophytes in the upper to mid thoracic spine.


IMPRESSION: 

1.  Minimally displaced fracture at the junction of the left manubrium with
the ossified portion of the left first rib costal cartilage, with 3-4 mm
displacement (series 4, image 48).  Associated retrosternal hematoma measuring
approximately 7.1 x 1.9 cm (transverse by AP).

2.  Posterior subluxation of the right sternoclavicular joint.

3.  Minimally displaced fractures of the right first rib costal cartilage at
the costochondral junction and left second rib costal cartilage.

4.  Displaced fractures of the posterolateral right first through fourth ribs.
Minimally displaced fracture of the right fifth rib.  No evidence of
pneumothorax or pleural effusions.

5.  Incidentally noted pulmonary nodules measuring 1-2 mm in the right upper
lobe and right middle lobe.

RECOMMENDATION(S):
For incidentally detected multiple solid pulmonary nodules smaller than 6mm,
no CT follow-up is recommended in a low-risk patient, and an optional CT
follow-up in 12 months is recommended in a high-risk patient.

See the ___ ___ Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver c/b ___ s/p liver
transplant in ___// chest CT to r/o mets.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  734 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at
the level of the hilar structures.  Stable appearance of the breasts.  Stable
mild coronary calcifications, no relevant valvular calcifications, no
pericardial effusion.  The anastomosis of the liver transplant is of identical
morphology.  There is a minimal new right pleural effusion.  No osteolytic
lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral bodies.  Mild
degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression fractures.
No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  No diffuse lung disease.  No
pleural thickening.  The airways are patent.

IMPRESSION: 

New small right pleural effusion.  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses. 
The airways are patent.  No diffuse lung disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Noncontrast chest CT

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver and HCC and PVT
and a hx of RCC.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Chest CTs between ___ and ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Heart size is normal.  A small pericardial effusion is
increased compared to the most recent prior examinations, without evidence of
hemodynamic compromise.  Coronary artery calcifications are mild.  The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Thoracic aorta and great vessel origin
calcifications are mild.  The main pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  Indistinct soft tissue in the anterior mediastinum is unchanged
probably reflects reactive thyroid hyperplasia.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is
mild linear left base atelectasis or scarring.  No pulmonary consolidation or
concerning pulmonary nodule.

BASE OF NECK: A 1.7 cm hypoattenuating thyroid nodule at the level of the
thyroid isthmus is unchanged since ___.

ABDOMEN: A similar-appearing wedge-shaped area of hypoattenuation is seen in
the right hepatic lobe, with subtle decrease of central density, corresponding
to right hepatic lobe radiofrequency ablation site.  The liver is slightly
nodular, compatible with provided history of cirrhosis.  Small accessory
spleen.  Varices are identified.  Surgical clips are noted in the upper
abdomen.  This is better characterized on the same-day dedicated abdominal MR.

___: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.
2. Increased, small pericardial effusion without evidence of hemodynamic
compromise.
3. Suboptimal evaluation of the upper abdomen, but minimally changed
appearance of the right hepatic lobe radiofrequency ablation site.  Cirrhotic
liver morphology.  This is better characterized on the concurrent dedicated MR
examination.
4. A 1.7 cm hypoattenuating thyroid nodule is unchanged since ___.  If
not previously performed, dedicated thyroid ultrasound should be considered
per ACR guidelines on incidentally discovered thyroid nodules.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  A 1.7 cm hypoattenuating thyroid nodule is unchanged since
___. If not previously performed, dedicated thyroid ultrasound should be
considered per ACR guidelines on incidentally discovered thyroid nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver and HCC and PVT
and a hx of RCC.  Evaluate for metastatic disease.

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector axial CT images were obtained through the chest
without intravenous contrast.  Multiplanar reformats including coronal,
sagittal, axial thin slice and axial maximum intensity projection images were
produced and uploaded to PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.4 s, 34.5 cm; CTDIvol = 22.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 789.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 790 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CT from ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid gland is heterogeneous
with a 1.7 cm nodule at the isthmus.  No enlarged supraclavicular or axillary
lymph nodes.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The study is not optimized for the evaluation of
subdiaphragmatic structures.  Within this limitation, cirrhotic liver
morphology is present.  Enlarged spleen is partially imaged.  Hypodensity in
hepatic segment VII corresponds to the patient's known lesion.  A fiducial
marker is present in the left hepatic lobe. There is a small hiatus hernia.
Esophagus is patulous without evidence of obstruction.  Complete abdominal
findings will be reported on separately dictated abdominal MRI from the same
date.

MEDIASTINUM: No enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes. Air is present in the
esophagus without evidence of a mass or stricture.

HILA: No enlarged hilar lymph nodes

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Mild cardiomegaly has increased since ___. 
Coronary artery calcifications are mild.  Pericardium is physiologic.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Interlobular septal thickening and ground-glass appearance at
the lung bases is suggestive of mild dependent interstitial edema.  No focal
consolidation, mass or nodule suspicious for malignancy.
-AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
-VESSELS: The pulmonary veins are engorged, suggestive of pulmonary vascular
congestion.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are top normal in caliber. 
The left pulmonary artery is mildly dilated. Thoracic aorta is normal in
caliber with mild calcified atherosclerotic plaque.
CHEST CAGE: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion or acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of metastatic disease in the chest.
2. Cardiomegaly has increased in comparison with ___.  This is
incompletely assessed on this study.  Recommend correlation with
echocardiography and clinical examination.
3. Pulmonary vascular congestion and mild dependent interstitial pulmonary
edema.
4. 1.7 cm thyroid nodule for which a non-urgent thyroid ultrasound is
recommended.
5. Abdomen is incompletely assessed on this study.  Lesion in the right
hepatic lobe is grossly unchanged and a fiducial marker is present in the left
hepatic lobe.  Please see the separately dictated liver MRI from the same date
for description of abdominal findings.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Nonurgent thyroid ultrasound.
Correlation of increasing cardiomegaly with echocardiography and clinical
examination.

sagittal, axial thin slice and axial maximum intensity projection images were
produced and uploaded to PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:

with a 1.7 cm nodule at the isthmus.  No enlarged supraclavicular or axillary
lymph nodes.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The study is not optimized for the evaluation of
subdiaphragmatic structures.  Within this limitation, cirrhotic liver
morphology is present.  Enlarged spleen is partially imaged.  Hypodensity in
hepatic segment VII corresponds to the patient's known lesion.  A fiducial
marker is present in the left hepatic lobe. There is a small hiatus hernia.
Esophagus is patulous without evidence of obstruction.  Complete abdominal
findings will be reported on separately dictated abdominal MRI from the same
date.

MEDIASTINUM: No enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes. Air is present in the
esophagus without evidence of a mass or stricture.

HILA: No enlarged hilar lymph nodes

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Mild cardiomegaly has increased since ___.

PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

consolidation, mass or nodule suspicious for malignancy.

congestion.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are top normal in caliber.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver and HCC and PVT
and a hx of RCC// chest CT to r/o mets. PLEASE DO NOT GIVE IV CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector images of chest were obtained after the
administration of intravenous contrast with coronal, sagittal, axial maximum
intensity projection reformats.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:

mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 727 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTs chest dated ___ and ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: There is a heterogeneous
hypoattenuated nodule in the isthmus of the thyroid measuring up to 1.7 cm
(2:7) stable dating back to CT chest ___.  There is no axillary or
supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: There is a cirrhotic morphology liver.  Ill-defined hypodensity
in segment VII of the liver (02:50) corresponds to patient's known lesion. 
Fiducial marker is noted in the left lobe of the thyroid.  There is a small
hiatal hernia.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

HILA: Within the limitations of a study without intravenous contrast, there is
no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart is not enlarged.  There are minimal
calcifications in the coronary arteries.  There is no pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  There is no discrete nodule or consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  There is a patent to the subsegmental levels.
3. VESSELS:  The main pulmonary artery is borderline enlarged in size
measuring up to 2.9 cm (302:110)
CHEST CAGE: There is no acute osseous abnormality.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of metastatic disease in the thorax.
2. Stable thyroid nodule.
3. Abdomen is incompletely evaluated on this study.  Within these limitations,
there is a cirrhotic morphology liver and ill-defined hypodensity in segment
VII of the liver that corresponds to patient's known lesion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male status post liver transplant for hepatitis-C and
hepatocellular carcinoma.

TECHNIQUE:  Contrast-enhanced chest CT was performed acquiring sequential
axial images from the thoracic inlet through the adrenal glands. Thin section
axial, coronal, sagittal and axial MIP's were also obtained. 150 cc of
Omnipaque 350 were administered intravenously without reported complication.

DOSE:  As per CT abdomen/pelvis.

COMPARISON:  Diagnostic chest CT dated ___.  Correlation also made to
PET-CT dated ___ and CT abdomen/pelvis dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid gland is unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged supraclavicular,
mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymph nodes are present.

There is stable mild cardiomegaly with multichamber enlargement. There is no
pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary artery and thoracic aorta are normal
in caliber.  No incidental central pulmonary embolus is identified.

The previously described non-FDG avid 11 mm right lower lobe pleural nodule
more likely represents an invagination of the right hemidiaphragm. There is no
new pulmonary nodule, mass or consolidation. No endobronchial lesion or
pleural abnormality is identified.

Bilateral gynecomastia is noted.

Multiple small varices surrounding the distal esophagus at the level of the GE
junction are stable since at least ___. For a more detailed
discussion of the upper abdomen, please refer to the separate report from the
CT abdomen/pelvis performed concurrently.

Multilevel spinal degenerative changes are present.  An old healed right rib
fracture is incidentally noted.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of pulmonary metastases.

Stable small paraesophageal varices.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CTA CHEST AND ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST

DATE:  ___.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis ___, reference CT abdomen
___, CTA abdomen ___, CT abdomen without contrast
___.

CLINICAL INDICATION:  ___ male with cirrhosis and HCC status post RFA
and TACE.  New liver transplant evaluation.  Needs multiphasic liver CT with
volumes for pretransplant assessment.  Please assess for focal liver lesions,
patency of vessels, and liver volumes.

TOTAL EXAM DLP:  2297.29 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

The thyroid gland is within normal limits.  The aorta, central pulmonary
arteries and great vessels are unremarkable.  There is no axillary,
mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.  The heart is normal in size.  There is no
pericardial or pleural effusion.  There is a 2-3 mm pulmonary nodule in the
left lower lobe (4B:104) which is stable from ___.  No new pulmonary
nodules are identified.  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Note
is made of bilateral gynecomastia, a hiatal hernia and paraesophageal varices.

ABDOMEN:

The liver is shrunken with a macronodular contour.  Splenic varices and small
gastric varices are apparent.  There is a splenorenal shunt.  There is
recanalization of a prominent paraumbilical vein measuring 1.3 cm in the
falciform ligament.

The portal and hepatic veins are patent.

There is a replaced right hepatic artery arising from the SMA.  There is a
replaced left hepatic artery arising from the left gastric artery.

The spleen is enlarged measuring 14.3 cm in craniocaudal dimension.

The ___ site demonstrates expected post-RFA hypodense region without
peripheral nodular areas of enhancement to suggest residual or recurrent
disease.  This is within segment VI and measures approximately 3.2 x 1.3 cm. 
Within segment III, there is a small area of Ethiodol marking the prior TACE
procedure.  Compared to the prior examinations, the amount of Ethiodol has
decreased.  There is a 7 x 9 mm arterial-enhancing region which demonstrate
wash-out (4A:25, 4B:143).  This is suspicious for recurrent HCC.  Distal to
this region, there is a wedge-shaped peripheral area of hypoenhancement and
minimal capsular retraction, likely in part related to the procedure.

There are a few regions which demonstrate vague arterial enhancement without
wash-out, for example in the border of segment VII/VIII measuring
approximately 9 mm (4A:15) and peripherally in segment VIII (4A:18).  

The adrenal glands and pancreas appear within normal limits.  The gallbladder
has been removed.  Small fluid collection measuring 1.8 x 1.8 cm in the
gallbladder fossa is unchanged from the prior examination.  There is no
mesenteric or retroperitoneal adenopathy.  No free air, free fluid or
pneumoperitoneum.  The visualized upper abdominal bowel loops are normal. 
Again seen in the small bowel mesenteric and partially imaged are rim
calcified structures (4B:202, 211).  Previous correlation with operative note
reflects these are likely dropped gallstones.

The kidneys demonstrate symmetric uptake of contrast.  The left kidney was not
imaged on delayed images to confirm symmetric excretion.  There is no
hydronephrosis.  Visualized portions of the abdominal aorta demonstrates
normal caliber aorta without atherosclerotic calcifications.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Prior TACE of segment II/III lesion with decreasing amount of Ethiodol and
new 7 x 9 mm arterial-enhancing lesion with wash-out concerning for recurrent
HCC.

2.  Replaced right hepatic artery arising from the SMA.  Replaced left hepatic
artery arising from the left gastric artery.

3.  Total liver volume 1360.1 cm3.

4.  Cirrhosis with sequelae of portal hypertension including varices,
splenomegaly and gynecomastia.  There is no ascites.

5.  Prior segment VI RFA site without evidence of residual or recurrent
disease.

6.  Stable postoperative changes status post cholecystectomy with small fluid
collection in the gallbladder fossa.  Redemonstration of calcified structures
in the mid abdomen felt likely to represent dropped gallstones.

Result #1 posted to the radiology critical results reporting system to be
brought to the attention of the ordering clinician.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT WITHOUT CONTRAST.

INDICATION:  HCC, assess for lung lesion.

COMPARISON:  Chest CTs and CT torsos from ___ to ___.

TECHNIQUE:

Axial helical MDCT images were obtained from the suprasternal notch to the
upper abdomen without administration of IV contrast.  Multiplanar reformatted
images in coronal and sagittal axes were generated.

FINDINGS:

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:

3-mm left lower lobe nodule adjacent to the descending aorta (series 5, image
126) is unchanged since over a year.  There is no new or enlarging lung
nodule.  The airways are patent until subsegmental level.

MEDIASTINUM:

Thyroid is unremarkable.  There is no pathologic supraclavicular, mediastinal,
or axillary lymph node enlargement by CT size criteria.  Significant
gynecomastia is unchanged.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion. 
Small hiatal hernia measures 2.2 cm.

UPPER ABDOMEN:

This study is not tailored for assessment for intra-abdominal organs.  There
are stigmata of cirrhosis and portal hypertension.  Ethiodol uptake from
chemoembolization of HCC is less extensive than abdominal CT of ___. 
This unenhanced study is not tailored to assess the residual or recurrence of
disease.  Small amount of ascites is seen around the liver and fat
infiltration of the mesentery is unchanged.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is no bony lesion concerning for malignancy. 
Multilevel degenerative change of thoracic spine is stable.

CONCLUSION:

No new or enlarging lung nodules.  A 3-mm left lower lobe nodule is unchanged
since ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with pancreatic adenocarcinoma// r/o mets

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest, coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.9 s, 31.9 cm; CTDIvol = 6.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 197.0
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 11.2 s, 72.7 cm; CTDIvol = 6.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 460.6
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 658 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA
PANCREAS (ABDOMEN AND PELVIS))

COMPARISON:  Chest CT one ___ performed elsewhere.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged and there are no
soft tissue abnormalities elsewhere in the chest wall concerning for
malignancy.  Findings below the diaphragm will be reported separately.

Cardio mediastinum:

Esophagus is mildly patulous, otherwise unremarkable.  There are no thyroid
findings warranting further imaging evaluation.  Atherosclerotic calcification
is mild in head and neck vessels, present in at least left main and anterior
descending coronary arteries.  Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. 
Heart is not enlarged.  Pericardium is physiologic.

Thoracic lymph nodes:

None pathologically enlarged.

Lungs, airways, and pleura:

Bilateral apical pleuroparenchymal scarring, more nodular than usual, but
symmetric and unchanged since ___.

Mild scattered subpleural ground-glass and interstitial thickening, posterior
right lung base is probably post inflammatory, has changed slightly in
distribution, most often seen with chronic aspiration.  There is no pneumonia
or bronchiectasis.

Well-circumscribed 6 x 4 mm soft tissue nodular opacity, left lower lobe,
4:228, unchanged since ___.  8 mm long linear lesion, anterior left
lung base, 4:237, and a triangular region of subpleural atelectasis in the
lingula surrounding minimally dilated peripheral bronchus, 4:228, also
unchanged.  No new or enlarging lung nodules or other focal lesions of
consequence.

Chest cage:

 Although there are no bone lesions in the imaged chest cage suspicious for

intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WANDW/O CANDRECONS, NON-CORONARY

INDICATION:  ___ man presenting with chest pain, now with hypoxia.
Evaluate for pulmonary embolism.

TECHNIQUE:  Helically acquired MDCT axial images were obtained from the
thoracic inlet through the lung bases in the early arterial phase after
administration of 100 mL Omnipaque intravenous contrast material.  Volumetric
data was collimated to display axial images with 2.5 mm and 1.25 mm slice
thickness.  Image post-processing further included multiplanar reformation to
construct coronal and sagittal images, and creation of oblique, 5 mm x 5 mm
MIPs oriented along each pulmonary artery.

DOSE:  DLP:  692.95 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None available.

FINDINGS: 

CTA CHEST:

The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber, without evidence of aneurysm or
dissection. The main, lobar, segmental, and subsegmental pulmonary arteries
are well opacified and without filling defect. The remaining great vessels are
normal in appearance.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:

The imaged thyroid is normal. There is no axillary, supraclavicular,
mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria. The heart is
structurally normal and there is no pericardial effusion. There is no evidence
of diffuse lung disease.  There is no airspace consolidation concerning for
infection.  There is considerable segmental atelectasis of the bilateral lower
lobes. The airways are patent. There are 3 mm pulmonary nodules in the right
middle lobe (3:88), and lingula (3:100). There is no pneumothorax or pleural
effusion.

The esophagus and visualized upper abdominal organs are unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No focal lytic or sclerotic lesion concerning for
malignancy.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute aortic syndrome or pulmonary embolism.
2. No evidence of pneumonia or pulmonary edema.
3. Considerable segmental atelectasis in the bilateral lower lobes.
4. 3 mm incidental pulmonary nodules. According to the ___ guidelines
for incidentally detected pulmonary nodules, if the patient has a history of
smoking or other known risk factors for malignancy, a follow-up chest CT in 12
months is recommended.  In the absence of such risk factors, no follow-up is
required.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT TORSO

COMPARISON:  ___.

HISTORY:  ___ female with advanced BAC.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
were obtained.  IV contrast was administered.  Coronal and sagittal reformats
were performed.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:  Extensive bilateral airspace consolidation is seen within the
lungs.  Again there is dense consolidation in the lower lobes, right greater
than left.  More patchy ground-glass consolidation is seen in the upper lobes
and multiple pulmonary nodules are also identified.  These findings are
relatively stable when compared to prior exam.  There is no hilar or axillary
lymphadenopathy.  Within the mediastinum, there is a 1.1 x 1.9 cm pretracheal
lymph node (3, 23) which is unchanged.  The heart and great vessels are
otherwise unremarkable.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion
identified.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:  The spleen, kidneys, adrenal glands, pancreas, and
gallbladder are unremarkable.  Within the liver, there is an ill-defined
hypodense area (3, 53) which likely represents focal fatty infiltration.  No
discrete lesions are identified.  There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal
pathologically enlarged lymph nodes identified.  There is no free fluid or
free air.  The small bowel loops are normal in caliber without focal wall
thickening.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  The bladder, uterus, rectum, and sigmoid are unremarkable.
There is a right ovarian cyst measuring 1.3 x 1.9 cm which likely represents a
follicle.  There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Extensive bilateral patchy and confluent airspace consolidation and
pulmonary nodules consistent with known bronchoalveolar carcinoma.  There has
been no significant change from study dated ___.
2.  11-mm pretracheal lymph node, unchanged.
3.  No evidence of metastatic disease within the abdomen or pelvis.

Please note that oncology table was not created due to lack of reproducible
discrete target lesions.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female with non-small cell lung cancer, on clinical
trial.  Assess for interval changes.

TECHNIQUE:  CT torso with oral and IV contrast.  MDCT images were acquired
from the thoracic inlet to the pubic symphysis.  Multiplanar reformatted
images are displayed in 5-mm slice thickness.

COMPARISON:  CT torso on ___.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  There is a 1-cm filling defect at the inlets of
the left atrium, representing a sizable tumor thrombus.  This finding is new
compared to the prior study.  The cardiac size is otherwise within normal
limits.  Target lesion #1 (image 3:26) now measures 11.4 x 10.0 mm.  There are
no other abnormalities identified for the heart.

The lungs again demonstrate extensive bilateral lower lobe airspace
consolidation, consistent with patient's known bronchoalveolar cell cancer.
The overall appearance is unchanged.  There are stable bilateral ground-glass
nodules predominating in the apical lobes.  Again seen is a stable, small
calcific density in the right lower lobe (image 3:35), likely a calcified
granuloma.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

CT ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  In the liver, there are subcentimeter hypodense
lesions, too small to be characterized but likely to be cysts.  The
gallbladder is normal without evidence of radiopaque stones.  There is a small
hypodense lesion in the pancreatic head, of uncertain clinical significance.
The spleen, adrenal glands, kidneys are normal.  The stomach, duodenum and
loops of contrast-filled small bowel are normal.  There are normal bowel gas
and fecal matter in the colon.  There are no pathologically enlarged lymph
nodes in the retroperitoneum or messentery .  There is no air or fluid within
the intra- abdominal cavity.

CT PELVIS WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The bladder is normally distended
without focal abnormality.  The uterus is normal in size for menstruating
female.  The large right adnexal cyst seen in the previous study is resolved.
The small right adnexal cyst seen before is now 18 mm in diameter, unchanged
in size.  The rectal vault is normal with bowel gas and fecal matter.  There
is no air or fluid within the pelvis.

VASCULATURE:  In addition to the new filling defect in the left atrium, there
is an arterial thrombosis at the right common iliac artery, starting just
distal to the aortic bifurcation (image 3:71) and extending at least to the
bifurcation of the right external and internal iliac arteries (image 3:87).

Bone window:  There are no suspicious blastic or lytic osseous lesions.  There
are no acute fracture or dislocation.  The underlying soft tissues are
unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Sizeable filling defect in the left atrium, consistent with tumor
thrombosis.  Evidence of arterial thrombosis at the right common iliac artery.

2.  Stable, extensive and diffuse bilateral airspace consolidation consistent
with the bronchoalveolar cell cancer.

3.  No abdominal or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

4.  Interval resolution of the large right adnexal cyst.  Stable-appearing
small right adnexal cyst.

The findings of the tumor thrombus and arterial thrombosis have been urgently
communicated to the ordering attending Dr. ___ by phone at 2:45 p.m.
on ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Mass seen at ___ a few months ago.  Presenting with pericardial
effusion. ?Lung mass.  

COMPARISON:  Multiple previous chest radiographs, most recent dated ___. 

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT of the thorax was performed without contrast. 
Coronal and sagittal reformats were provided for interpretation.  

FINDINGS:

There are two nodules within the right middle lobe measuring 6 mm (5:181) and
4 mm (5:188) respectively.  There are also pleural-based nodules within the
right middle and lower lobes measuring 4 mm (5:175) and 6 mm (5:166)
respectively.  There are moderate-sized bilateral pleural effusions.  There is
complete atelectasis of the left lower lobe with partial atelectasis of the
right lower lobe, likely secondary to compression from the effusions.  

There is a small pericardial effusion.  A single-chamber pacemaker is noted
with its tip in the right ventricle.  There is mild cardiomegaly.  Multiple
subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes are noted and are likely reactive.  No
axillary adenopathy. The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  

The visualized upper abdominal viscera is unremarkable.  Multilevel
degenerative change is noted within the lower thoracic and upper lumbar spine.
Osseous structures are otherwise unremarkable.  

IMPRESSION:

1.  Multiple subcentimeter nodules as described within the right middle and
lower lobes, with the largest measuring 6 mm.  Correlation with the previous
imaging would be of benefit to ensure stability.  Follow-up CT in ___ months
is recommended as per ___ society recommendations.  

2.  Moderate-sized bilateral pleural effusions. 

3.  Small pericardial effusion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Chest CTA

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with ?type II NSTEMI// ? PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.4 s, 31.7 cm; CTDIvol = 4.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 129.3
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 1.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.8
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 1.2 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 132 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  No prior CTs of the chest are available.

FINDINGS: 

Mild cardiomegaly is mostly due to right atrial enlargement.  There is no
pericardial effusion.  Mild calcifications of aortic valve leaflets.  Minimal
calcifications of mitral valve annulus.
There is no pericardial effusion.

Atherosclerotic calcifications are extensive, mainly along the left coronaries
as well as along the normal caliber thoracic aorta and main branches. The
aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of stenosis,
occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no clear evidence of
penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the segmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental
pulmonary arteries.  Respiratory motion artifacts limit evaluation of
subsegmental vessels particularly in the upper lung fields.  The main and
right pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of
right heart strain.

Bilateral layering pleural effusions are small, left greater than right. 
There is subsequent adjacent atelectasis.

Tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental level.  Scattered ill
defining ground-glass opacities in peribronchovascular distribution involve
mainly the upper lobes (___:46 for example).
Bilateral lower lobes minimal atelectasis is secondary to the pleural
effusions, as mentioned above.  Scattered subsegmental platelike atelectasis
in the lingula (2:63).

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
In the right lobe of the thyroid 1.5 cm hypodense nodule with central coarse
calcifications (301:16).

Limited images of the upper abdomen demonstrate reflux of contrast into the
IVC and hepatic veins which could be due to mild cardiac insufficiency or
rapid contrast injection.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified. 
Diffuse mild degenerative changes are predominantly of lower thoracic level.

IMPRESSION:


-No evidence of pulmonary embolism.
-Scattered peribronchial ground-glass opacities in the bilateral upper lobes
compatible with pneumonia.
-Small bilateral layering pleural effusions, left greater than right.
-Extensive atherosclerotic calcifications involving coronaries as well as the
normal caliber thoracic aorta.
-15 mm right lobe thyroid nodule.  This can be further evaluated via thyroid
ultrasound if clinically indicated.
-Right heart failure with mainly right atrial enlargement with reflux of
contrast into the hepatic veins.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with breast cancer w staging Chest CT in ___
showing non-specific lung nodules, needs interval follow up// follow up on
indeterminate lung nodues

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT of the chest was performed after the
administration of intravenous contrast.  Axial coronal and sagittal
reconstructions were acquired.  Maximum intensity projections were also
acquired

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.1 s, 32.9 cm; CTDIvol = 8.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 256.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 257 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  To a prior study done ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: Thyroid is diffusely enlarged with multiple hypodense areas
within it, could be related to multinodular goiter.  Further evaluation with
an ultrasound may be helpful.

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There stable small right axillary lymph nodes.  There are
stable postsurgical changes to the right breast.

MEDIASTINUM: Left-sided Port-A-Cath tip projects to the cavoatrial junction
there are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  Heart size is normal. 
There is no pericardial effusion


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion

LUNG: 1 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule (4, 42) is unchanged.  2 tiny
pulmonary nodules in the right middle lobe (the the 4, 145) no new pulmonary
nodules.  There is subsegmental atelectasis in both lung bases.  No new or
growing pulmonary nodules

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones is unremarkable.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen shows no focal liver
lesions.  No adrenal masses are seen.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable tiny 1-2 mm pulmonary nodules majority of these nodules are
perifissural are subpleural.  No new pulmonary nodules.

Stable postsurgical changes to the right breast.

Left-sided Port-A-Cath with its tip in the SVC.

Diffuse enlargement the thyroid with multiple hypodense areas within it, most
likely represents a multinodular goiter.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with breast cancer w staging Chest CT in ___
showing non-specific lung nodules, repeat in ___// follow up on
indeterminate lung nodues

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  339 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  CTA from ___.

FINDINGS: 

Stable diffusely enlarged thyroid gland.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular
or axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at
the level of the hilar structures.  The cardiac structures are stable, with
mild coronary calcifications.  No pericardial effusion.  Right breast post
surgical changes.  No abnormalities are noted in the upper abdomen.  No
osteolytic lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral
bodies.  Mild degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression
fractures.  The lung parenchyma shows new scarring in the right lung apex (4,
34).  The scarring extends to postradiation fibrosis in the anterior portions
of the right upper lobe (4, 87).  No pleural thickening or pleural effusions. 
No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  The airways are patent.

IMPRESSION: 

Interval occurrence of right postradiation fibrosis extending into the right
lung apex.  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  No adenopathy or
pleural abnormalities.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with chest pain, shortness of breath,pleuritic //
Septic Emboli, Pulmonary Embolism

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 1.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.8
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 2.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 6.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 3.0
mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 2.8 s, 22.3 cm; CTDIvol = 12.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 279.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 283 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  Patient is status post tricuspid valve replacement.  The
right atrium appears mildly enlarged.  The great vessels are within normal
limits.  No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Several foci of peripheral parenchymal opacities are noted in
the right lower lobe and left lower lobe, which are nonspecific but may be of
infectious or inflammatory etiology.  Given recent history of endocarditis,
septic emboli cannot be excluded.  There is a small lucent focus adjacent to
the right lower lobe rounded consolidation (3:98).  The right lower lobe area
of opacity in totality measures roughly 20 x 9 mm.  Additional nodule in the
right lung base measures 3 mm.  At the lateral left lung base density measures
12 mm.  In the left lung base anteriorly, a nodular density measures 15 mm. 
There is a 3 mm subpleural nodule in the right lung apex (3:8).  The airways
are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.
There is a defect of the lateral right fourth rib, likely postsurgical.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Several foci of peripheral parenchymal opacities are noted in the right
lower lobe and left lower lobe, with subtle lucent focus adjacent to the right
lower lobe consolidation, which may represent early cavitation and given
recent history of endocarditis, favor septic emboli, though nonspecific
infectious or inflammatory conditions remain differential possibilities.
3. Patient is status post tricuspid valve replacement.

HISTORY:  ___ male with AFib and tachypnea, recently on Coumadin.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous helical acquisition through the chest was performed
with intravenous contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, and MIP images of the pulmonary
arteries were created.

FINDINGS:  The aorta opacifies normally without evidence of aortic dissection.
There is atherosclerotic disease of the descending aorta and aortic arch.  The
pulmonary arteries opacify normally without intraluminal thrombus.  There is
atherosclerotic disease involving the coronary arteries.  No mediastinal or
hilar lymphadenopathy is identified.  A 2-mm left lower lobe nodule is again
identified and unchanged since ___.  No focal consolidations are
identified within the lungs.   There are no pleural effusions or pneumothorax.
Median sternotomy wires are identified.  A dual-lead pacemaker is in place.

Images of the upper abdomen demonstrate a normal-appearing liver, spleen,
adrenal glands, kidneys, and pancreas.  Multilevel degenerative changes are
noted throughout the spine.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No acute pulmonary embolism or intrathoracic process.

2.  Stable-appearing 2-mm left lower lobe pulmonary nodule, unchanged since
___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with fall and femur fracture, please assess for
other abdominal injuries.  Please assess for extent of pneumothorax and rib
fracture.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous MDCT images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were
performed after administration of intravenous contrast.

COMPARISON:  There are no comparison studies available.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST: 
There is no pneumomediastinum and no mediastinal hemorrhage. There is no acute
aortic syndrome including no aortic dissection or aortic aneurysm formation. 
There are moderate atherosclerotic calcifications of the aortic arch and the
coronary arteries.

The main pulmonary arteries are prominent bilaterally likely due to pulmonary
hypertension.

There is no pericardial effusion.  There is a small left pleural effusion and
mild bibasilar opacities representing atelectasis or lung contusions.  A small
left anterior and medial pneumothorax is seen.  There are displaced left
lateral and posterior rib fractures involving the ribs 3 through 9, concerning
for flail chest.  There is associated small left hemothorax and mild soft
tissue emphysema.
There are no right-sided rib fractures.  The transverse processes are intact.
There is a ill-defined 7 mm right pulmonary nodule (se2, img30). 


CT OF THE ABDOMEN: 
The liver is normal.  Multiple gallstones are seen, but no cholecystitis. 
There is no intra- or extra-hepatic biliary dilatation.  The pancreas, spleen,
and bilateral adrenal glands are normal.  Multiple hypoattenuating renal
lesions are seen, too small to characterize, but likely representing simple
cysts.
No free air and no free fluid.
The esophagus, stomach, duodenum and remainder of the small and large bowel
are normal.  The mesentery is normal.  There is no evidence of bowel or
mesenteric traumatic injury.

There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

CT OF THE PLEVIS:  
There is a pessary device seen in the uterus.  Old right inferior pubic ramus
fractures are seen.  The patient is status post left total hip replacement.

BONES:  The height of the vertebral bodies of the spine is preserved.  No
evidence of spinal fractures.  The sternum is normal.

No evidence of acute pelvic or bone fractures.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Displaced fractures of the left posterolateral #3 through #9 ribs with a
left pneumothorax and concern for flail chest.
2.  Small left hemothorax.
3.  Bibasilar lung opacities representing atelectasis or lung contusions.
4.  No traumatic injury of the abdomen or pelvis.
5.  No pelvic fractures.
6.  Ill-defined 7 mm right pulmonary nodule. F/u CT in 6 months is
recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History of fall with left femur fracture, multiple left rib
fractures, pleural effusion and small left pneumothorax, to assess for fluid
collection.

TECHNIQUE:  Unenhanced MDCT of thorax was performed using a standard
department protocol.  Contiguous axial images at 5-mm and 1.25-mm slice
thickness were reviewed concurrently with coronal and sagitta reformats.

Comparison was made with prior CT torso study from ___.

FINDINGS:

MEDIASTINUM:  Thyroid gland is normal.  There are no pathologically enlarged
mediastinal, supraclavicular, or axillary lymph nodes.  The right pulmonary
artery, at 30 mm, is substantially enlarged.  Low-density cardiac contents
suggest anemia.  Atherosclerotic calcification in the coronary arteries is
mild, whereas it is severe in the aortic arch.  Heart is normal size without
pericardial effusion.

ABDOMEN:  The study is not tailored for the evaluation of abdomen; however,
limited views are remarkable to reveal multiple gallstones suggestive of
cholelithiasis, but without any evidence of cholecystitis.

BONES:  Fractures are seen involving third to ninth ribs on the left side with
marked displacement and overriding of the fractured ends.  Bones show
generalized osteopenia.  There are no bone lesions suspicious for
malignancy/infection.

AIRWAYS AND LUNGS:  Previous, minimal left pneumothorax has resolved. 
Posteriorly layering, non serous, left pleural effusion has increased and left
lower lobe is newly collapsed. The left lower bronchus, as well as the rest of
the bronchial is tree is patent. Bilateral peripheral subpleural reticular and
ground glass opacities and with some tractional bronchiectasis is due to mild
pulmonary fibrosis.  Moderate atelectasis is also seen in the right lung base.
Triangular-shaped nodule measuring 7 x 5 mm is seen in the right upper lobe
(4:  112).

IMPRESSION:  Since ___, minimal left lower pneumothorax has
resolved, whereas moderate, posteriorly layering, left pleural effusion has
increased and the left lower lobe has collapsed.

Left third to ninth rib fractures with marked displacement and overriding
fractured ends. No chest wall or pleural hematoma is present.

Pulmonary arterial hypertension.  Mild pulmonary fibrosis.  

7-mm right upper lobe nodule is likely benign, but followup CT is recommended
at one year for confirmation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female status post MVC, assess for traumatic
injuries.

COMPARISONS:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained from the thoracic inlet
through the pubic symphysis after uneventful administration of 130 cc of
Omnipaque contrast medium.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were prepared.

CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Thyroid gland is normal in appearance with
symmetric enhancement.  The aorta and major branches are patent with normal
three-vessel arch and atherosclerotic calcification noted in the ostium of the
left subclavian artery.  The heart and pericardium are normal without
pericardial effusion.  No axillary, hilar, supraclavicular or mediastinal
lymphadenopathy is seen.  The trachea and central airways are patent to the
segmental level.  The esophagus is unremarkable.  The lungs are without focal
consolidation.  Patchy nodular ground-glass opacification in the right middle
lobe and lingula is compatible with atelectasis.  A 5-mm nodule is seen in the
left upper lobe (2:10).  A 7-mm pleural nodule is also noted (2:38).  There is
no pleural effusion with minimal dependent atelectasis noted.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST:  The liver is normal in attenuation without
focal lesion, intra- or extra-hepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  The
gallbladder, pancreas, spleen and bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable. 
The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically without hydronephrosis.
The stomach, small and large bowel are unremarkable.  There is no free air or
free fluid in the abdomen.  There is no mesenteric or retroperitoneal
adenopathy.  The aorta and major branches are patent and normal in caliber.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The bladder and rectum are unremarkable.  The
uterus appears to be surgically absent.  Within the right adnexa, there is a
2.4 x 2.8 cm cyst (2:95).  There is no free pelvic fluid.  There is no pelvic
or inguinal pathologic adenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is no lytic or sclerotic bony lesion concerning for
osseous malignant process.  There is a minimally displaced fracture of the
left lateral eighth rib (2:33).  Soft tissues appear unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Left lateral eighth rib fracture with underlying atelectasis likely due to
splinting

2.  5 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule, given the patient's history of
smoking per ___ Society guidelines, a followup chest CT is recommended
in ___ months.

3.  Right adnexal cyst.  If the patient is postmenopausal, followup assessment
with pelvic ultrasound is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman hx COPD/asbestosis/pleural effusion admitted
with shortness of breath that is not improving with steroids/nebs. please
evaluate for PE. Thanks// hx COPD and shortness of breath that is not
improving with steroids/nebs-- eval for pe

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 3.2 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 41.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
8.4 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 4.9 s, 32.1 cm; CTDIvol = 6.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 206.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 216 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph ___.
CT chest with contrast ___.

FINDINGS: 

VESSELS:
The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

HEART: The heart is normal in size.  There is no evidence of pericardial
effusion.  Coronary atherosclerotic calcifications are noted.

AIRWAYS AND LUNGS:

There is layering debris within the trachea.  There is peribronchial
thickening of the central airways.  Mucous plugging is seen in the segmental
and subsegmental airways of the right upper lobe and bilateral lower lobes. 
There is a new large ground-glass opacity in the right upper lobe, which is
concerning for infection. Peribronchial nodular opacities measuring up to 6 mm
are seen in the anterior segment of the right lower lobe (series 6, image
175-186).  There is a new 3 mm pulmonary nodule in the lingula (series 6,
image 121).

Calcified bilateral pleural plaques are noted.  There is a small to moderate
left pleural effusion with surrounding pleural thickening and enhancement,
which is not significantly changed in size or appearance compared to prior CT
on ___.  Rounded atelectasis is noted in the lingula and left lower
lobe and is overall unchanged compared to prior CT.

LYMPH NODES: There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy.

SOFT TISSUES: The thyroid gland appears unremarkable. There are bilateral
subpectoral breast implants.  The left breast implant demonstrates several
curvilinear hyperdensities with surrounding hypodense fluid, which is
concerning for an intracapsular rupture.

ABDOMEN: Limited images of the upper abdomen demonstrate postsurgical changes
from cholecystectomy and gastric bypass.  There is a small hiatal hernia.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.  Partially
visualized postsurgical changes are seen from anterior cervical discectomy and
fusion of the lower cervical spine, as well as along the right sixth rib
(series 6, image 94).

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2.  New large, confluent ground-glass opacity in the right upper lobe, which
is concerning for pneumonia or atypical infection.
3.  Debris within the trachea and mucous plugging in the segmental and
subsegmental airways of the right upper lobe and bilateral lower lobes, which
most likely represents chronic bronchitis.
4.  New peribronchial pulmonary nodules measuring up to 6 mm in the right
lower lobe and a new 3 mm pulmonary nodule in the lingula.  Please see
recommendations for follow up of these nodules below.
5.  Stable appearance of the small to moderate left pleural effusion with
pleural thickening and enhancement.  Unchanged adjacent compressive and
rounded atelectasis in the lingula and left lower lobe.
6.  Intracapsular rupture of the left subpectoral breast implant.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  For incidentally detected multiple solid pulmonary nodules
measuring 6 to 8 mm, a CT follow-up in 3 to 6 months is recommended in a
low-risk patient, with an optional CT follow-up in 18 to 24 months. In a
high-risk patient, both a CT follow-up in 3 to 6 months and in 18 to 24 months
is recommended.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

radiology_facts.report_text:
TECHNIQUE:  5-mm axial sections were taken through the chest, abdomen, and
pelvis after the administration of IV and oral contrast.  Sagittal and coronal
reformats were obtained and reviewed.

COMPARISON:  CT torso, ___.

INDICATION:  A ___ male with stage III renal cell carcinoma.  Outside
hospital ultrasound of the left kidney concerning for mass.  Please evaluate.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  The thyroid is normal and enhances homogeneously.  There is no
cervical, mediastinal, or axillary lymphadenopathy.  The patient is status
post right upper lobe resection with stable changes.  There are multiple
right-sided pulmonary nodules, which are stable from the previous exam and
dating back to ___.  The most superior nodule measures 3 mm (3, 26).  There
is another small punctate sub-4-mm nodule just anterior to this region.  There
is a stable nodule and a scar (3, 27).  It is unchanged from previous exams. 
There is another 3-mm lesion (3, 33), which is stable.  The largest nodule is
at the right base and measures 5 mm (3, 46).  It measured 5 mm in the previous
exam.  There are no new nodules.  The left lung is clear.  Bilaterally, there
are no focal consolidations or pleural effusions.

ABDOMEN:  The liver is unremarkable without focal masses or perfusion defects.
The patient is status post cholecystectomy.  The spleen and pancreas are
normal.  The portal venous system is patent.  The bilateral adrenals are
normal.  The left kidney is normal without any masses or cysts.  There is no
hydronephrosis.  It enhances and excrete contrast appropriately.  The patient
is status post right nephrectomy.  There is no evidence of recurrence in the
resection bed.  The stomach and small bowel are unremarkable.  The abdominal
vasculature is normal in course and caliber.  There is no abdominal
lymphadenopathy.

PELVIS:  The large bowel is unremarkable without wall thickening, stranding,
or adjacent masses.  The urinary bladder and prostate are normal.  There is no
pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is mild DJD, most notable in the disc space between
L4 and L5.  There are no concerning sclerotic or lytic bony lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of recurrence in the resection bed or evidence of metastatic
disease. Left kidney is normal.
2.  Stable right pulmonary nodules without evidence of new nodules.

Results were discussed with Dr. ___ at 4:30, ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT TORSO WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male with history of renal cell carcinoma status post
resection of right kidney.  Please evaluate for interval changes.

COMPARISON:  CT torso with contrast dated ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multiple axial CT images were obtained through the chest, abdomen,
and pelvis following the administration of 100 cc of Visipaque intravenous
contrast.  Sagittal and coronal reconstructions were obtained.  No adverse
contrast reactions were reported.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  The thyroid gland demonstrates homogeneous attenuation without focal
lesions.  There is no supraclavicular, mediastinal, hilar, or retrocrural
lymphadenopathy.  No axillary lymphadenopathy.  Heart size is within normal
limits without pericardial effusion or thickening.  Thoracic aorta is normal
caliber.  Branches of the aortic arch are normal.  Pulmonary trunk is within
normal limits by size.

Changes of right upper lobe resection are again seen.  Multiple right sided
pulmonary nodules are stable dating back to the exam of ___. No
new nodules are identified.  The left lung is clear.  No confluent
consolidation or pulmonary mass.  No pleural effusions or pneumothorax.

ABDOMEN:  No free air.  No abdominal ascites or fluid collection.

The liver, spleen, and pancreas demonstrate homogeneous enhancement without
focal lesions. Incidental note is made of a small splenule.  No pancreatic
ductal dilatation.  Status post cholecystectomy. Adrenal glands are normal in
size without nodularity.

Changes of right nephrectomy.  The left kidney demonstrates homogeneous
enhancement without focal lesions.  No left-sided hydronephrosis or
hydroureter.  However, there is new mild periureteral stranding and
enhancement involving the left ureter from the level of the UPJ to the distal
third of the ureter.  

There is a small hiatal hernia. Enteric contrast reaches the distal transverse
colon.  Normal caliber bowel loops without diverticulosis or diverticulitis. 
Appendix not visualized.

No mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.  Abdominal aorta is normal
caliber without aneurysmal dilatation or dissection.  Celiac trunk, SMA, left
renal artery, and ___ are patent.  Changes of right renal artery and vein
ligation.  The IVC is patent.

PELVIS:  Urinary bladder is underdistended.  Prostate gland is within normal
limits.  Seminal vesicles are symmetric.  There is no pelvic lymphadenopathy
or fluid collection.  Incidental note is made of a small fat-containing left
inguinal hernia.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES AND SOFT TISSUES:  No acute fracture or destructive osseous
process.  Ill-defined lucency within the right ilium has been present since
the exam of ___ measuring 0.9 cm in length, likely a small intraosseous cyst.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Multiple stable right-sided pulmonary nodules unchanged since the exam of
___. No intrathoracic lymphadenopathy.
2.  No evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, or
pelvis.
3.  New mild left sided periureteral stranding from the level of UPJ to the
distal third ureter is of uncertain significance. There is no evidence of
ureteral obstruction.  Please correlate clinically for any symptoms of
infection or inflammatory change. Recommend close attention to this finding at
follow up imaging.

radiology_facts.report_text:
REASON FOR EXAMINATION:  Left arm and chest wall pain, worse with deep
inspiration.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained from thoracic inlet to upper
abdomen after administration of IV contrast.  The contrast was administered
through the left arm, which due to streak artifacts prevents assessment of the
left brachial plexus and left axilla.

Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with coronal and sagittal reformats.

FINDINGS:

There are no pathologically enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph
nodes.  Heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion. 
The aorta and pulmonary arteries are unremarkable.  The imaged portion of the
upper abdomen reveals no appreciable abnormality.

There are no bone lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

Right middle lobe consolidation, with some minimal atelectatic component is
demonstrated.  In addition, there is right upper lobe subpleural pulmonary
nodule, 4:64, less than 4 mm in diameter.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No definitive abnormality to explain patient's symptoms.  Assessment with
MRI for brachial plexus evaluation is recommended.  The assessment of brachial
plexus on CT is extremely limited.
2.  Right middle lobe opacity, most likely representing focus of infection,
but reevaluation with chest CT in three months for assessment of
stability/resolution is required.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PROCEDURE:  CT chest with contrast.  

REASON FOR EXAM:  ___ man with metastatic HCC.  Evaluate for mets to
lung.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT chest with IV Optiray contrast was performed.  5-mm and 1.25-
mm mm axial slices were acquired with coronal and sagittal reformats.

COMPARISON:  Previous CT chest ___.

FINDINGS:  There is a sub-2-mm right upper lobe nodule (4.64), which is stable
since the previous study.  No new nodules, mass, consolidation, pneumothorax
or pleural effusion. Although accurate assessment for sub 2mm pulmonary
nodules is somewhat limited due to respiratory artifact.

The caliber of the aorta and pulmonary artery is normal.  Airways are widely
patent to subsegmental levels.  No central or mediastinal lymph node
enlargement by CT size criteria.

In the limited views of the upper abdomen, there is an area of low attenuation
in the periphery of the liver which contains a central area of normal
parenchymal attenuation consistent with previous partial liver resection, The
liver is incompletely evaluated on this study and reference to the recent CT
abdomen should be made.  The spleen appears enlarged and there are multiple
large splenic hilar varices.

IMPRESSION:  

1.  Sub 2 mm right upper lobe nodule is stable.  No new pulmonary nodules or
mass although the evaluation is slightly limited by respiratory artifact.

2.  The upper abdominal organs are incompletely evaluated, except to note
probable splenomegaly with multiple large splenic varices and previous liver
resection.  Reference to the recent abdominal CT scan should be made for
further evaluation of this region.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man with history of hepatocellular carcinoma, status
post sorafenib treatment, now off therapy.  Please assess for progression of
disease.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior studies, most recent of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial acquired images were obtained through the abdomen prior to
and after the administration of 150 cc of Optiray intravenous contrast. 
Arterial and delayed images were obtained.  In addition, post-contrast images
were also obtained through the pelvis and chest.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  The lungs are clear bilaterally.  A 1.2 x 1.5
cm right hilar lymph node is seen, slightly increased in size since the chest
CT of ___.  There is no mediastinal or axillary lymphadenopathy. 
The heart size is normal.  There is a small hiatal hernia, stable.  Esophageal
varices are again noted.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  Again seen is a mottled,
heterogeneous pattern of enhancement of the liver without significant change
from the prior study.  Also again seen is an area of early enhancement
measuring approximately 14 x 11 mm with washout seen on the delayed phase
(3a:31 and 6:20) in segment IV.  This  and stable in size since the prior
study.  Another possible focus of arterial enhancement with washout on delayed
phase is noted at the inferior tip of the liver (3a:77 and 6:44); the borders
are vague, but it appears stable since the prior study. Again seen is a
rounded area of hyperdensity, corresponding to tissue glue as described on
prior studies, in segment IV with a peripheral area of low attenuation,
unchanged, at the prior resection site.

The gallbladder is absent.  The spleen again demonstrates a wedge-shaped
defect, consistent with infarct.  The kidneys are normal bilaterally.  The
pancreas and intra-abdominal loops of bowel are also within normal limits. 
Again seen are bilateral adrenal gland nodules which are stable in size since
the prior study with the right nodule measuring 14 x 9 mm and the left
measuring 18 x 13 mm.  Extensive portosystemic collaterals are again seen with
prominent splenorenal shunt.  Evaluation for lymphadenopathy is slightly
limited due to extensive collateral vessels.  Portal, splenic, and superior
mesenteric veins are patent.  Moderate amount of abdominal ascites is again
seen, stable in extent since the previous study.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  The rectum, prostate, and bladder
are within normal limits.  Again seen is 9.7 x 4.7 x 7.6 cm fat density mass
within the pelvis, which exerts mass effect on the bladder and pelvic loops of
bowel.  This is stable in size since the previous study but has increased in
size since ___.  In addition, there are areas of soft tissue density
within this fat density mass. These findings are concerning for a low-grade
liposarcoma.

Moderate amount of free fluid is again noted within the pelvis with a
right-sided fluid-containing inguinal hernia, stable.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Stable-appearing heterogeneously enhancing liver with at least one nodule
concerning for hepatocellular carcinoma in segment IV.  This is stable in size
since previous study.

2.  Moderate abdominal ascites, unchanged.

3.  Extensive portosystemic shunting, stable.  No evidence of venous
thrombosis.

4.  Stable size of fat density mass in the anterior left pelvis.  However,
this mass also contains area of soft tissue density and given increase in size
since ___, this is concerning for a low-grade liposarcoma.

5.  Bilateral adrenal nodules, stable in size since the previous study.

Findings were posted to the critical communication dashboard shortly after
review on ___.

bowel.  This is stable in size since the previous study but has increased in
size since ___.  In addition, there are areas of soft tissue density
within this fat density mass. These findings are concerning for a low-grade
liposarcoma.

Moderate amount of free fluid is again noted within the pelvis with a
right-sided fluid-containing inguinal hernia, stable.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious osseous lesions are identified.

1.  Stable-appearing heterogeneously enhancing liver with at least one nodule
concerning for hepatocellular carcinoma in segment IV.  This is stable in size
since previous study.

2.  Moderate abdominal ascites, unchanged.

3.  Extensive portosystemic shunting, stable.  No evidence of venous
thrombosis.

4.  Stable size of fat density mass in the anterior left pelvis.  However,
this mass also contains area of soft tissue density and given increase in size
since ___, this is concerning for a low-grade liposarcoma.

5.  Bilateral adrenal nodules, stable in size since the previous study.

Findings were posted to the critical communication dashboard shortly after
review on ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman who came in with abdominal pain, likely has
tubo-ovarian abscesses, now with hypotension and rising lactate.  Evaluate for
acute intrathoracic process.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 740 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CTs available on PACS at the time of this
dictation.  Reference is made with a chest radiograph dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

Tiny foci of air in the right supraclavicular region appears to be within the
lumen of the right subclavian vein, related to intravenous injection (series
4, image 25).  Common origin of the brachiocephalic trunk and left common
carotid artery is a normal variant.  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. 
The main, left, and right pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber without
evidence of a filling defect indicate the presence of any incidental, acute
large central pulmonary embolus.  Cardiac configuration is normal.  No
evidence of pericardial effusion.

No axillary or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.  Scattered mediastinal lymph
nodes are prominent, measuring up to 8 mm in the right upper paratracheal
station (series 4, image 39), 8 mm in the right lower paratracheal station
(series 4, image 60), and 15 x 17 mm in the subcarinal station (series 4,
image 82, 94).  A right upper hilar lymph node is enlarged, measuring 12 x 15
mm (series 4, image 80).  Left hilar lymph nodes are prominent but not
enlarged by CT size criteria.  Lymph nodes in the epicardial fat are enlarged,
for example measuring 19 x 10 mm (series 4, image 130), 19 x 8 mm (series 4,
image 125), and 15 x 11 mm (series 4, image 137).

A left pleural effusion is small and nonhemorrhagic.  Adjacent left lower lobe
moderate homogeneously enhancing parenchymal airspace opacities most
consistent with relaxation atelectasis.  A slightly smaller right pleural
effusion is also nonhemorrhagic with a small amount of fluid tracking into the
minor fissure.  Adjacent right lower lobe parenchymal opacity has slightly
heterogeneous enhancement, likely reflecting a combination of atelectasis and
aspiration/ infection in the appropriate clinical situation.  Diffuse
peribronchial or wall thickening is mild to moderate, worse in the lower
lobes.  The airways are patent to at least the subsegmental level.  No
pneumothorax.  No evidence of pleural thickening or enhancement.

2 right upper lobe pulmonary nodules measure 4 mm (series 4, image 78, 82). 
The right middle lobe pulmonary nodule measures 3 mm (series 4, image 89). 
Peribronchovascular nodules and ground-glass opacities in the right upper lobe
are likely infectious in etiology given patient symptoms (series 4, image 71).
Centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema is moderate.  No evidence of pulmonary
abscesses or septic emboli.

The imaged thyroid gland is unremarkable.

No osseous lesions in the chest cage concerning for malignancy or infection. 
No evidence of an acute fracture.  Soft tissue anasarca in the chest cage is
moderate.  No evidence of organized fluid collections in the chest cage.  No
no soft tissue gas.

 Please refer to the dedicated CT abdomen and pelvis report from the same day
for a description of sub-diaphragm findings.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Bilateral small non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions with adjacent
atelectasis and likely aspiration/infection in the right lower lobe given
provided history.

2.  Right upper lobe peribronchovascular nodules and ground glass with diffuse
peribronchiolar wall thickening suggests infection.

3.  Moderate centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema, worse in the upper lungs.

4.  No organized fluid collections in the thorax or evidence of septic
pulmonary emboli.
